M04_Q10.stem: What verification methods are required to demonstrate that Feature L03: Circadian Lighting Design has been achieved?

options.text: Letter of assurance from the MEP
An on-site spot measurement by the WELL Assessor
Letter of assurance from the Architect

M04_Q10.explanation: Feature L03 Circadian Lighting Design - Part 1 Meet Lighting for Day-Active People sets measurable thresholds at workstations: at least 150 EML or 240 EML for four hours, on the vertical plane at eye level。Because achievement depends on measured light levels, WELL verification uses on-site measurement/spot checks rather than only design letters or commissioning reports。

A.gap: 把 MEP 聲明誤當 L03 實測證據
A.followup: L03 EML at eye level 靠 letter，還是現場測量？

D.gap: 把建築師保證書誤作 circadian 測量
D.followup: 150/240 EML 達標能只靠 architect letter 嗎？

C.gap: 混淆 commissioning report 與 WELL 現場測量
C.followup: Commissioning report 能取代 L03 EML on-site measurement 嗎？

M04_Q11.stem: Feature L08: Electric Light Quality stipulates that projects who achieve this optimization understand how electric light is used in a space. For instance, color Rendering Index (CRI) and IES TM-30-15 are commonly used metrics used to determine?

options.text: The color rendering properties of a light source.

M04_Q11.explanation: Feature L08 Electric Light Quality - Part 1 Enhance Color Rendering Quality requires luminaires to meet color rendering thresholds such as CRI ≥90, CRI ≥80 with R9 ≥50, or IES TM-30 metrics including Rf/Rg/Rcs,h1。The feature text states CRI and IES TM-30-15 determine the color rendering properties of a light source。

C.gap: 把 color rendering 指標誤連到 glare
C.followup: CRI/TM-30 評估 glare，還是燈源 color rendering？

D.gap: 只抓到飽和度，漏掉整體 rendering
D.followup: Rf、Rg、Rcs 是只看 saturation，還是 rendering quality？

B.gap: 混合 chromatic 與 flicker 兩個 Part
B.followup: CRI/TM-30 對應 L08 color rendering，還是 flicker？

M04_Q12.stem: In an apartment building new construction project, which of the following is provided to all tenants in Part 1: Provide Visual Acuity of Feature L02: Visual Lighting Design?

options.text: Task lights at each work area.
Illuminance thresholds for common tasks conducted in spaces.
A minimum of 50% glazed envelope area in living space.

D.followup: L07 管 uniformity；window distance 是 L01 哪個 option？

M04_Q41.stem: Which of the following terms refers to windows and/or glazing in the outdoor envelope of a building?
M04_Q41.explanation: Feature L06 Daylight Simulation - Part 1 Conduct Daylight Simulation describes simulations used to inform choices for fenestration and shading. In WELL Light requirements, fenestration refers to windows and glazing in the building envelope that shape daylight access.

B.gap: 把 luminance 亮度量誤認為窗系統。
B.followup: Fenestration 是光亮度，還是 envelope glazing？

C.gap: 混淆 reflectance 與外殼窗洞系統。
C.followup: Surface reflectance 與 windows/glazing in envelope 是同一物嗎？

D.gap: 被 defenestration 相似字形誤導，選到錯誤詞。
D.followup: Daylight simulation 要優化 fenestration；defenestration 是該術語嗎？

M04_Q42.stem: Which of the following features outlines design parameters for windows to optimize the quantity and quality of daylight while minimizing unwanted glare?

options.text: Feature L04: Electric Light Glare Control

M04_Q42.explanation: Feature L05 Daylight Design Strategies - Part 1 Implement Daylight Plan sets design parameters for daylight access through workstation distance to transparent envelope glazing, envelope glazing area, and VLT >40%. Part 2 then integrates solar shading to prevent glare while maintaining daylight quality.

B.gap: 把 electric glare control 誤當成日光窗戶設計。
B.followup: L04 管 electric light glare；哪個 feature 管 glazing、VLT、shading？

C.gap: 把 electric illuminance 誤連到窗戶設計。
C.followup: L02 管 work-plane illuminance；daylight window strategy 在哪裡？

D.gap: 把視覺平衡誤認為 fenestration design 要求。
D.followup: L07 管 ratios 與 CCT；它是否規定 envelope glazing？

M04_Q43.stem: A new corporate headquarters building is being constructed in a dense urban area. During the design phase, lighting simulations demonstrate that 40% of regularly occupied space receives at least 300 lux (28fc) for over 50% of operating hours each year. What should the designers change to earn 2 points in Feature L01: Light Exposure?

options.text: Include light wells to ensure that 75% of regularly occupied space is reached by natural light.
Increase window area to ensure that 50% of regularly occupied space is reached by natural light.
Change building orientation to ensure that natural light reaches regularly occupied space for more than 60% of operating hours each year.
Introduce light shelves to increase minimum lux received to 400 lux.

M04_Q43.explanation: 題幹標示可能有誤；Feature L06 Daylight Simulation - Part 1 Conduct Daylight Simulation gives the higher tier when average sDA300,50% is achieved for more than 75% of regularly occupied floor area, or when 300 lux is achieved for more than 50% plus 100 lux for more than 95% over 50% of daylit hours. Feature L01 is a precondition and does not award 2 points.

B.gap: 把 50% 面積誤當成 L06 higher tier。
B.followup: L06 higher tier 要 >75%；50% regularly occupied space 足夠嗎？

D.gap: 把門檻改成 400 lux，忽略 300 lux。
D.followup: L06 target 是 300 lux over daylit hours，還是 400 lux？

C.gap: 誤改 operating hours，未抓到面積不足。
C.followup: 題幹已有 over 50% hours；L06 higher tier 還缺哪個 area threshold？

M04_Q44.stem: DALY is an acronym for what?
M04_Q44.explanation: Feature L08 Electric Light Quality - Part 2 Manage Flicker discusses flicker-associated conditions and notes that migraines accounted for millions of disability adjusted life years (DALYs). This acronym appears in the health-impact context for flicker, not as a lighting calculation metric.

A.gap: 把 DALY 誤當成 daily light exposure 累積量。
A.followup: L08 提到 migraines and DALYs；這是在量健康負擔嗎？

C.gap: 混淆 adjusted life years 的公共衛生術語。
C.followup: DALY 中的 adjusted 對應 disability，還是 deferred？

B.gap: 把 DALY 誤解成 annual detail 統計。
B.followup: L08 flicker health burden 的 DALY 是 annual report term 嗎？

M04_Q45.stem: Many features in the WELL Building Standard require performance testing to verify the feature. For Part 1: Provide Visual Acuity of Feature L02: Visual Lighting Design, a Visual Lighting performance test is required. What are the 5 parts of performance tests for feature verification?

options.text: Test Parameters, Test Quality, Reporting Metrics, Device Options.
Reporting, Physical Samples, Photographic Evidence, Compliance, Conditions.
Test Locations & Conditions, Test Method, Test Quantity, Reporting & Compliance, Device Requirements.

M04_Q45.explanation: Feature L02 Visual Lighting Design - Part 1 Provide Visual Acuity is a performance-tested precondition, and WELL Performance Verification protocols organize tests by Test Locations & Conditions, Test Method, Test Quantity, Reporting & Compliance, and Device Requirements. These categories structure how visual lighting measurements are verified against illuminance thresholds.

B.gap: 漏掉 location、method 與 compliance 架構。
B.followup: Visual Lighting test 是否需 Test Locations、Method、Compliance？

C.gap: 把文件審查素材當成 performance test 架構。
C.followup: L02 驗 illuminance；photos 或 samples 能替代 test method 嗎？

A.gap: 只抓到簡化字眼，未列 performance test 完整分類。
A.followup: Performance test 是否包含 Reporting、Compliance、Device 要求？

M04_Q46.stem: Humans are diurnal, meaning that we are prone to wakefulness during the day and sleepiness at night. What system in our bodies does light exposure stimulate?
M04_Q46.explanation: Feature L01 Light Exposure - Part 1 Provide Indoor Light explains that light is the main driver of the circadian system, which controls body processes and aligns humans with the natural day-night cycle. Feature L03 then sets EML/M-EDI thresholds to support circadian health for day-active people.

C.gap: 把外在 day-night cue 當成體內系統。
C.followup: L01 說 light 是 main driver；它驅動哪個 body system？

B.gap: 把負面 disruption 當成正常系統。
B.followup: L03 把 phase disruption 列為風險；健康目標是哪種 rhythms？

A.gap: 把失同步狀態誤當成光曝露要刺激的體內系統。
A.followup: L03 是 support circadian health，還是製造 desynchronized rhythms？

M04_Q47.stem: Electric lighting used indoors has a low frequency of flicker that are not present in daylight. What health conditions has Flicker been associated with?
M04_Q47.explanation: Feature L08 Electric Light Quality - Part 2 Manage Flicker states that flicker has been associated with eye strain, headaches, migraines, and epileptic seizures. The Part manages this through requirements such as reduced flicker operation, IEEE 1789-2015 recommended practices, or Pst LM ≤1.0 and SVM ≤1.6.

D.gap: 把 flicker 影響誤連到免疫疾病。
D.followup: L08 Part 2 列 migraines、headaches；有列 immune disorders 嗎？

B.gap: 混淆 Air 健康風險與 lighting flicker。
B.followup: Respiratory illness 屬 Air 概念；L08 flicker 主要造成什麼？

A.gap: 把 flicker 健康負擔泛化到心血管疾病。
A.followup: L08 flicker evidence 指向 migraines，還是 cardiovascular disease？

M04_Q48.explanation: WELL certification guidance uses AAP for Alternative Adherence Pathway, a route for demonstrating an alternative way to meet intent when standard feature requirements are not followed exactly. The provided WELL v2 standard excerpt does not define the acronym directly, so this is a certification-process term rather than a Light feature threshold.

D.gap: 把 AAP 誤解成 access protocol 或流程協定。
D.followup: AAP 是 access protocol，還是 alternative adherence route？

B.gap: 被 auditory pollution 字樣誤導。
B.followup: AAP 處理替代合規；是否代表 ambient auditory pollution？

C.gap: 把 professional association 誤當認證路徑。
C.followup: AAP 是 project compliance pathway，還是 audio professionals 組織？

M04_Q49.stem: Projects may use spreadsheets published by IWBI to calculate what?
M04_Q49.explanation: Appendix L1 states that Equivalent Melanopic Lux (EML) is calculated by multiplying photopic lux by the melanopic ratio, and that projects may use spreadsheets published by the journal authors or IWBI for this calculation. Feature L03 Circadian Lighting Design - Part 1 relies on EML/M-EDI thresholds for circadian lighting compliance.

D.gap: 把 spreadsheet 誤連到 luminance threshold。
D.followup: Appendix L1 公式 EML = L × R，是算哪個 metric？

B.gap: 混淆 visual glare 與 circadian EML 工具。
B.followup: L04 用 UGR 管 glare；IWBI spreadsheet 算的是什麼？

C.gap: 選到不存在且混合 visual acuity 的術語。
C.followup: Appendix L1 的 spreadsheet 是否用來算 Visual Acuity Myopathy？

M04_Q50.stem: What is the intent of Feature L09: Occupant Lighting Control?

options.text: Provide individuals with access to customizable lighting environments.
To allow for personal style in individual workspaces.
Maximize personal responsibility in regards to electricity.

M04_Q50.explanation: Feature L09 Occupant Lighting Control states its Intent is to provide individuals with access to customizable lighting environments. Part 1 implements this through lighting zones and control systems, while Part 2 provides supplemental lighting to meet individual task needs.

C.gap: 把 lighting control 誤解成用電責任。
C.followup: L09 Intent 是 personal control，還是 electricity responsibility？

B.gap: 把 customizable lighting 誤讀成個人風格裝飾。
B.followup: L09 zones/scenes 是為 personal style，還是 customizable lighting？

D.gap: 抓到滿意度結果，卻誤當 feature intent。
D.followup: L09 Intent 是 retention，還是 access to customizable lighting？

M05_Q01.stem: Approximately how much of the adult population is estimated to be physically inactive?
M05_Q01.explanation: Movement Concept background 與 Feature V01 Active Buildings and Communities - Part 1 Design Active Buildings and Communities 的 Issue 都指出，2016 global estimates 顯示 nearly a quarter，即約 23% adult population physically inactive。題目問 approximately，最接近的比例是四分之一。

C.gap: 高估成人身體不活動比例，混入其他族群數據。
C.followup: Movement 背景說成人約四分之一，哪個百分比最接近 23%？

B.gap: 把 developed countries 或概略風險數字誤當全球成人值。
B.followup: V01 Issue 的 global estimates 是比較 23%、29%、15%，題目問全球成人時應抓哪一個？

A.gap: 誤用開發中國家約十五趴的不活動估計比例。
A.followup: Movement 背景中 15% 對應哪類國家，而非全球成人？

M05_Q02.stem: An existing four-story building recently underwent a major renovation to convert its layout from 8 separate suites into one large headquarters for a corporate tenant. The new tenant is interested in open staircases to connect all four floors and promote walkability visually. According to Part 3: Promote Visible Stairs of Feature V03: Circulation Network, where should at least one of the staircases be located?

options.text: In view of the reception area

M05_Q02.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 3 Promote Visible Stairs 要求至少一座開放給 regular occupants、服務 project all floors 的 staircase，從 main point of entry 量測時需 physically and/or visibly before elevators/escalators。這是為了讓樓梯在電梯或 escalator 前先被看見或到達。

A.gap: 把便利性位置誤認為 V03 visible stairs 核心要求。
A.followup: V03 Part 3 從主入口量測時，樓梯相對電梯應在何處？

D.gap: 只抓到 reception visibility，忽略電梯前置順序。
D.followup: 即使接待區看得到樓梯，V03 Part 3 是否仍要求在電梯前？

B.gap: 混淆停車動線與主要入口後的樓梯可見性要求。
B.followup: V03 Part 3 的測量起點是停車區，還是建築主入口位置？

M05_Q03.stem: In a lobby on the way to their offices, what design elements should included in the staircase that is available to regular occupants to meet the requirements of Part 1: Design Aesthetic Staircases of Feature V03: Circulation Network?

options.text: Point-of-decision signage directing them to the staircase.
A lounge with moveable chairs and tables.

M05_Q03.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 1 Design Aesthetic Staircases 要求至少一座開放給 regular occupants 且服務 all floors 的 staircase，每層包含至少兩項 aesthetic strategies；清單包含 natural design elements，例如 plants、water features 或 images of nature。Point-of-decision signage 屬於 Part 2，不是 Part 1 的 aesthetic list。

A.gap: 混淆 V03 美學樓梯與決策點 signage 要求。
A.followup: V03 的決策點 signage 屬哪個 Part，是否列入美學清單？

B.gap: 把一般安全標示誤當成促進 stair use 的美學策略。
B.followup: V03 Part 1 的 aesthetic 清單中，是否包含 no-smoking signage？

C.gap: 把休憩家具誤認為樓梯間美學強化策略之一。
C.followup: Part 1 要求樓梯每層美學策略時，活動家具是否在清單內？

M05_Q04.stem: The lobby and central staircase of a building includes several large paintings. What other component of the staircase would qualify this project, according to Part 1: Design Aesthetic Staircases of Feature V03: Circulation Network?

options.text: Staircase with a minimum width of 1.4m [56 inches].

M05_Q04.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 1 Design Aesthetic Staircases 要求每層至少兩個 independent strategies；artwork 已算一項，music 也是清單中的合格 strategy。Signage 是 Part 2 Integrate Point-of-Decision Signage，不能補足 Part 1 的兩項美學策略。

B.gap: 把鼓勵走樓梯 signage 誤放進 Part 1 美學清單。
B.followup: 已有 artwork 時，第二項須從美學清單選；signage 屬哪個 Part？

D.gap: 把樓梯尺寸規格誤認為 WELL aesthetic strategy。
D.followup: V03 Part 1 是要求寬度門檻，還是 music、artwork、lighting 等策略？

C.gap: 把飲水便利設施誤當成樓梯美學促動策略之一。
C.followup: drinking fountain 能算 V03 Part 1 每層兩項美學策略嗎？

M05_Q05.stem: Which of the following incentive strategies should an employer implement to help meet the requirements of Part 1: Incentives for Eligible Employees of Feature V09: Physical Activity Promotion?

options.text: Flexible work hours to accommodate physical activity.
Offer an extra discount in the cafeteria.
Provide company sponsored safety equipment such as helmets and lights.
Award a $50 subsidy per month to occupants who engage in alternative transportation at least 3 days per week.

M05_Q05.explanation: Feature V09 Physical Activity Promotion - Part 1 Offer Physical Activity Incentives, Option 1 要求 eligible employees 至少提供兩種 physical activity promotion programs；清單明列 flexible work hours to accommodate physical activity。補助必須是 physical activity costs，例如 membership fees 或 group fitness classes，而非泛稱 alternative transportation。

D.gap: 把替代通勤補助誤等同身體活動成本補助項。
D.followup: V09 補助範例是會員費與團課時，通勤補助是否有被明確列出？

B.gap: 把餐飲折扣誤認為符合標準的身體活動誘因。
B.followup: cafeteria discount 是否連到活動參與、成本或保費減免？

C.gap: 把安全設備支援誤當成 V09 incentive program。
C.followup: helmets and lights 較像通勤支援；V09 incentive 有哪些類型？

M05_Q06.stem: A team of 15 employees are training for a city marathon. Each employee has participated in various events throughout the year. Assuming their employer has a policy that meets Part 1: Offer Physical Activity Incentives of Feature V09: Physical Activity Promotion, which of the following is each employee entitled to?

options.text: Monthly on-site training programs by certified personal trainers.
A subsidy to cover the costs of participation in sports events (races, sports teams, team fitness events).
Reductions in health care premiums based on physical activity engagement.
50% reimbursement for software or wearable technology that tracks physical activity and eating habits.

M05_Q06.explanation: Feature V09 Physical Activity Promotion - Part 1 Offer Physical Activity Incentives, Option 1 的合格 programs 包含 reductions in health care premiums based on physical activity engagement，並要求至少提供兩項 incentives。題幹的「entitled to」比標準語氣更強；標準是 project offers programs to eligible employees。

B.gap: 把活動賽事費用補助視為標準明列 entitlement。
B.followup: V09 補助範例是會員費或團課，sports event 是否明列？

D.gap: 混淆 V10 穿戴裝置補助與 V09 活動誘因。
D.followup: 50% wearable subsidy 出現在 V10，還是 V09 activity incentives？

A.gap: 把活動機會課程誤當成 V09 incentive entitlement。
A.followup: monthly trainer programs 較接近 V06；V09 incentive 清單有這項嗎？

M05_Q07.stem: A company is ordering new chairs for its 100 office workers. What feature is not required for the chairs to qualify under Part 3: Provide Chair Adjustability of Feature V02: Ergonomic Workstation Design?
M05_Q07.explanation: Feature V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design - Part 3 Provide Chair Adjustability 要求 all seating at workstations 可調整 seat height、seat depth，並再加一項 backrest/lumbar、backrest angle 或 armrest adjustability。removable wheels 不在要求清單中。

A.gap: 漏看座椅高度是所有工作站座椅必要調整項。
A.followup: V02 Part 3 對所有座椅列出的前兩個基本調整項，是 seat height 和哪一項？

B.gap: 忽略腰部支撐可作為額外座椅調整選項之一。
B.followup: V02 Part 3 的 additional adjustability 是否包含 lumbar support？

C.gap: 漏看 seat depth 與 seat height 同屬必備調整。
C.followup: Part 3 說 all seating 必須可調哪些基礎項目，seat depth 是否列在其中？

M05_Q08.stem: What is the minimum Walk Score needed to meet the requirements of Part 1: Select Sites with Pedestrian-friendly Streets of Feature V05: Site Planning and Selection?
M05_Q08.explanation: Feature V05 Site Planning and Selection - Part 1 Select Sites with Pedestrian-friendly Streets, Option 1 要求至少一個 functional building entrance 開向 pedestrian network，且 project 所在 zip/postal code 的 Walk Score 最低為 70。此門檻是 pedestrian-friendly streets 的量化路徑之一。

C.gap: 把更高 walkability 評級誤當 WELL 最低門檻。
C.followup: V05 Part 1 Option 1 寫的是 minimum Walk Score 多少，而不是理想高分多少？

D.gap: 混淆 V04 Bike Score 50 與 V05 Walk Score。
D.followup: V04 的 Bike Score 是 50；V05 的 Walk Score 是否相同？

B.gap: 低估行人友善街道的 Walk Score 最低要求。
B.followup: V05 Part 1 的 zip/postal code 路徑是否允許 Walk Score 40，還是要求更高分數？

M05_Q09.stem: In order to achieve the WELL Precondition Feature V01: Active Buildings and Communities, the project must earn at least one point from a design-based optimization. Which optimization feature is acceptable to achieve this point?

options.text: Feature V08: Physical Activity Spaces & Equipment.

M05_Q09.explanation: Feature V01 Active Buildings and Communities - Part 1 Design Active Buildings and Communities 要求 project 至少在 V03 Circulation Network、V04 Facilities for Active Occupants、V05 Site Planning and Selection 或 V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment 中取得一點。V08 是清單中的 design-based optimization。

C.gap: 把 V02 人體工學前提誤列為 V01 設計優化。
C.followup: V01 Part 1 明列的是 V03、V04、V05、V08；V02 是否出現在這個清單？

B.gap: 把活動課程機會誤作 V01 指定的設計優化。
B.followup: V06 提供免費活動課程；V01 的設計優化清單包含 V06 嗎？

D.gap: 把 self-monitoring wearable 策略誤認為建築設計優化。
D.followup: V10 是穿戴監測；V01 Part 1 的建築與場地清單包含它嗎？

M05_Q10.stem: A new employee at an office building has opted to ride their bike to work every day to avoid driving. Which of the following is required for Feature V04: Facilities for Active Occupants?

options.text: Bicycle maintenance tools (tire pumps, patch kits, and hex keys) are available.
Employees are provided bicycle locks and a yearly reimbursement of $175 for alternative transportation.
Complimentary shaded bicycle racks with private locks for every occupant.
Tire pumps and a monthly reimbursement of $15.

M05_Q10.explanation: Feature V04 Facilities for Active Occupants - Part 1 Provide Cycling Infrastructure, Option 2 Bike parking 要求提供 basic bike maintenance tools，例如 bike pump and patch kit，並與 long-term bike parking co-located，或提供 quarterly on-site bike maintenance services。補助或每人 private locks 不是此 Part 的必要條件。

B.gap: 把通勤報銷與車鎖誤作 V04 bike infrastructure 要求。
B.followup: V04 Part 1 Option 2 要求 bike parking 與工具，有提年度補助嗎？

D.gap: 抓到 tire pump 但混入不存在的月補助要求。
D.followup: 若有 tire pump，V04 還要 patch kit 或維修；月補助有列入嗎？

C.gap: 誤以為每位 occupant 都需專屬遮蔭車位與鎖。
C.followup: V04 長期 bike parking 是 5% regular occupants，還是每人一個？

M05_Q11.stem: To achieve Part 2: Provide Outdoor Physical Activity Space of Feature V08: Physical Activity Spaces, which of the following might be available to building occupants at a property that has achieved WELL Certification?

options.text: Walking trails with benches, 1.6 km (1.0 mi) from the building.
A fitness zone with all-weather fitness equipment.
Outdoor track facility at a local college, accessible by regular shuttle to and from the building.
Tennis courts 1 km (0.6 mi) from the building.

M05_Q11.explanation: Feature V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment - Part 2 Provide Outdoor Physical Activity Space 要求至少一種 outdoor physical activity space 在 project boundary 0.25 mi(400 m) walk distance 內、且 regular occupants 可免費使用；清單包含 fitness zone with all-weather fitness equipment。距離 1 km 或 1.6 km 的設施超出門檻。

A.gap: 認得 walking trail 類型，卻忽略 400 m 距離上限。
A.followup: V08 Part 2 允許 trail，但 walk distance 可達 1.6 km 嗎？

C.gap: 把接駁車可達誤當成 0.25 mi 步行距離。
C.followup: Part 2 要求 0.25 mi 步行距離，regular shuttle 能取代嗎？

D.gap: 認得 recreational court 類型，卻忽略 400 m 範圍。
D.followup: tennis court 可算球場，但 1 km 是否符合 V08 的 0.25 mi？

M05_Q12.stem: To encourage alternative transportation and daily fitness, a property manager recently installed showers and changing rooms at an urban property. The new area contains 1 shower and changing room for the first 100 regular building occupants, plus an additional shower for every 150 regular building occupants thereafter. What other additional onsite facility must be present in order to qualify this property for Part 2: Provide Showers, Lockers and Changing Facilities of Feature V04: Facilities for Active Occupants?

options.text: Treadmills for every 5 regular building occupants.
Treadmills for every 10 regular building occupants.

M05_Q12.explanation: Feature V04 Facilities for Active Occupants - Part 2 Provide Showers, Lockers and Changing Facilities 要求 showers with changing facilities 在 650 ft(200 m) walk distance 內，並且每個 shower 至少提供 five lockers，且 lockers co-located with shower facilities。treadmills 屬於 fitness equipment，不是此 Part 的 locker 要求。

D.gap: 低估 locker-to-shower ratio 的五比一要求。
D.followup: V04 Part 2 要每個 shower 至少五個 lockers，兩個足夠嗎？

C.gap: 把 V08 健身器材誤放到 V04 淋浴更衣設施。
C.followup: 題目已滿足 shower 數量後，V04 Part 2 還缺 lockers，還是 treadmills？

A.gap: 混淆 active occupant 支援設施與室內健身器材配置。
A.followup: V04 Part 2 缺的是 locker storage；treadmill ratio 有出現嗎？

M05_Q13.stem: An employee has access to her building's gym and regularly uses the treadmill. In combination with the treadmill, what additional amenity should she be able to use according to Part 1: Provide Indoor Activity Spaces of Feature V08: Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment?
M05_Q13.explanation: Feature V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment - Part 1 Provide Indoor Activity Spaces, Option 1 要求 dedicated fitness facility 免費提供，且至少包含 two types of exercise equipment；標準範例直接列出 free weights 與 treadmill。treadmill 屬 cardio，free weights 可補足另一種 exercise equipment type。

C.gap: 把另一台 cardio equipment 誤當成不同訓練類型。
C.followup: V08 範例用 treadmill 與 free weights，哪個補足另一種器材？

B.gap: 把補水設施誤認為 exercise equipment type。
B.followup: water station 可支援使用者，但是否算 V08 exercise equipment？

D.gap: 把相近 cardio 器材誤認為標準範例的第二類型。
D.followup: rower 雖是器材，但搭配 treadmill 時哪個更符合 V08 範例？

M05_Q14.stem: After finishing a workout at an onsite fitness facility, what would an employee have access to in a building which meets Part 2: Provide Showers, Lockers and Changing Facilities of Feature V04: Facilities for Active Occupants?

options.text: Both a shower and access to a locker.
Refreshment station with fruit and a water re-fill station.
Post-workout stretching equipment and access to a locker.

M05_Q14.explanation: Feature V04 Facilities for Active Occupants - Part 2 Provide Showers, Lockers and Changing Facilities 要求 showers with changing facilities 於 650 ft(200 m) walk distance 內提供，且每個 shower 至少 five lockers 並 co-located。標準沒有要求 post-workout equipment 或 refreshment station。

A.gap: 把健身後伸展器材誤作 V04 shower/locker 設施。
A.followup: V04 Part 2 是淋浴、locker、更衣；包含 stretching equipment 嗎？

C.gap: 把營養補水 amenities 誤當 active occupant 必要設施。
C.followup: fruit 或 refill station 有助舒適；V04 是否要求 shower 和 locker？

D.gap: 抓到 locker 但混入未要求的 stretching equipment。
D.followup: 若建築已符合 V04 Part 2，員工必須能使用 shower；這個選項是否漏掉 shower？

M05_Q15.stem: A company with 300 employees offers individual workstations to each employee in its office. Which of the following active furnishings could be offered to earn 1 point for Feature V07: Active Furnishing?

options.text: 150 adjustable height standing desks and 150 desk-top height adjustment stands.

M05_Q15.explanation: Feature V07 Active Furnishings - Part 1 Provide Active Workstations 要求 active workstations 至少達 50% of workstations 可得 1 point，且 supplemental desktop solutions 可算合格 active workstation。300 個 workstations 中 150 個正好達到 50%。

C.gap: 選了合格類型但數量只達三分之一，不足 50%。
C.followup: 300 個工位要達 V07 一點的 50% 門檻，至少需多少 active workstations？

A.gap: 忽略 100% active workstations 超過一點門檻。
A.followup: 若 300 個全是 active workstations，V07 90% 門檻會給幾點？

D.gap: 同時低估數量門檻，50 個遠低於 50%。
D.followup: treadmill desks 是合格類型，但 50/300 是否能達到 V07 的 at least 50% 要求？

M05_Q16.stem: A small office building has 10 employees. Five workspaces each have one standard desk and one side table for storage. To reduce prolonged sitting, what should 3 of the other 5 workstations include according to Part 2: Provide Height-Adjustable Work Surfaces of Feature V02: Ergonomic Design?

options.text: A workstation chair with adjustable height .

M05_Q16.explanation: Feature V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design - Part 2 Provide Height-Adjustable Work Surfaces 要求 at least 25% of all workstations 可由 user 調整到 seated and standing work，透過 manual/electric height-adjustable work surfaces 或 supplemental solutions。10 個 workstations 中提供 3 個已超過 25% threshold。

C.gap: 把替代坐具誤認為可坐站調整的工作桌面要求。
C.followup: V02 Part 2 調整的是 work surface，balance ball chair 能調桌面高度嗎？

A.gap: 混淆 V07 主動工位類型與 V02 可調工作面。
A.followup: treadmill desk 可屬 V07；V02 Part 2 要哪種可調工作面？

D.gap: 把 chair adjustability 誤作高度可調工作面要求。
D.followup: adjustable chair 屬 V02 Part 3；Part 2 重點是椅子還是工作面？

M05_Q17.stem: A building has recently renovated its gym according to Part 1: Provide Indoor Activity Spaces of Feature V08: Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment. Building occupants may see which of the following resistance training equipment?
M05_Q17.explanation: Feature V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment - Part 1 Provide Indoor Activity Spaces 要求 dedicated fitness facility 至少提供 two types of exercise equipment，標準範例包含 free weights 和 treadmill。free weights 是典型 resistance training equipment。

A.gap: 把核心穩定輔具誤認為主要 resistance equipment。
A.followup: V08 範例列出哪種 resistance equipment，與 treadmill 搭配？

D.gap: 把瑜伽墊這類活動輔助用品誤當阻力訓練器材。
D.followup: yoga mats 可支援活動，但是否等同 free weights 這種器材？

C.gap: 把 stair climber cardio 器材誤認為阻力訓練。
C.followup: 題目問阻力訓練器材，stair climber 與 free weights 哪個更符合？

M05_Q18.stem: A 5-story office building includes stairs located within 7.5 m (25 ft) of the main entrance. Which of the following design features would NOT promote the use of stairs and discourage reliance on elevators?

options.text: Light levels of at least 108 lux (10 fc).
Windows with views to the outdoors.

M05_Q18.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 1 Design Aesthetic Staircases 的合格 lighting strategy 要求 light levels of at least 215 lux(20 fc) when in use。108 lux(10 fc) 低於此 threshold，因此不符合該 stair-use promotion strategy。

C.gap: 誤判 daylight/views；windows 屬 V03 美學策略。
C.followup: V03 Part 1 清單是否包含 windows 或 skylights 提供 daylight？

B.gap: 誤判 plants；natural design elements 是合格策略。
B.followup: plants 是否符合 V03 Part 1 的 natural design elements 清單？

D.gap: 誤判 music；它明列於樓梯美學策略清單。
D.followup: V03 Part 1 第一項美學策略是什麼，music 是否能促進 stair use？

M05_Q19.stem: The Movement Concept states that if physical inactivity were reduced by 25%, approximately how many deaths could be averted globally?
M05_Q19.explanation: Movement Concept background 與 Feature V01 Active Buildings and Communities - Part 1 Design Active Buildings and Communities 的 Solutions 說明，如果 physical inactivity reduced by 25%，globally 可避免 over one million deaths。10% reduction 對應的是 more than half a million deaths。

D.gap: 低估 25% reduction 對全球死亡避免量的描述。
D.followup: Movement 將 25% reduction 連到 over one million，750,000 接近嗎？

A.gap: 把 10% reduction 的 half million 誤套到 25%。
A.followup: 背景中 half million 對應 10% reduction；25% reduction 對應多少？

C.gap: 選了中間估計，忽略 over one million 的明確敘述。
C.followup: V01 對 25% inactivity reduction 的用詞是 over one million 嗎？

M05_Q20.stem: Which of the following is NOT considered a key deterrent of physical activity?
M05_Q20.explanation: Feature V06 Physical Activity Opportunities - Part 1 Offer Physical Activity Opportunities 的 Issue 列出 key determinants/barriers，包括 time、convenience、motivation、self-efficacy、weather conditions、travel/family obligations、fear of injury、lack of social support 等。healthcare costs 是 inactivity 的經濟影響之一，不是該清單中的 key deterrent。

D.gap: 忽略時間明列為身體活動行為的決定因素之一。
D.followup: V06 Issue 的 determinants 是否包含 time，課程如何降低此障礙？

C.gap: 忽略 motivation 是身體活動參與的核心阻礙。
C.followup: V06 同時列出 motivation 與 self-efficacy，哪個選項才不是阻礙？

B.gap: 忽略 weather conditions 明列為活動阻礙因素。
B.followup: V06 Issue 是否把 weather conditions 列為活動 determinant？

M05_Q21.stem: According to WELL v2™, gamification can be used to encourage building occupants to do which of the following?

options.text: Get up and take occasional walks.

M05_Q21.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 1 Design Aesthetic Staircases 將 gamification 列為 staircase aesthetic strategy；V03 Solutions 也說 game elements 可鼓勵 desired behaviors，例如 stairclimbing。這裡的 gamification 是用於提高 stair use，而不是 productivity。

C.gap: 把一般移動提醒誤作 V03 遊戲化樓梯焦點。
C.followup: V03 gamification 位於樓梯 feature，舉例是 walks 還是 stairclimbing？

A.gap: 把遊戲化誤解成提升工作效率的工具與目標。
A.followup: V03 談 gamification 時，目標是 productivity 還是 stairclimbing？

D.gap: 混淆 V04 騎行支援與 V03 樓梯遊戲化策略。
D.followup: bike to work 由 V04 支援；gamification 在 V03 哪個樓梯 Part 出現？

M05_Q22.stem: Which of the following does NOT provide support at standing workstations, according to Part 4: Provide Support at Standing Workstations of Feature V02: Ergonomic Workstation Design?
M05_Q22.explanation: Feature V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design - Part 4 Provide Support at Standing Workstations 要求 regularly standing 50% or more working hours 的 workstations 至少納入兩項支持策略，例如 anti-fatigue mats、recessed toe space、footrest/footrail 或 leaning chair。frequent paid breaks 不在此清單中。

C.gap: 忽略 footrest/footrail 是 V02 Part 4 合格支持項。
C.followup: V02 Part 4 是否明列 footrest 或 footrail 作為站立工位支持？

A.gap: 忽略 leaning chair 是站立工位支持策略之一。
A.followup: 若員工站立超過 50% working hours，leaning chair 是否可算 Part 4 的兩項支持之一？

D.gap: 忽略 anti-fatigue mat 是標準明列支持策略。
D.followup: Part 4 第一類是否包含 anti-fatigue mats 或減衝地面？

M05_Q23.stem: To meet the space and equipment requirements for Part 1: Provide Indoor Activity Spaces for V08: Physical Activity Spaces, what percentage of the building's occupants should be able to use the dedicated fitness facility at any time?
M05_Q23.explanation: Feature V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment - Part 1 Provide Indoor Activity Spaces, Option 1 要求 dedicated fitness facility 至少有 two types of exercise equipment，且 equipment quantities allow use by at least 5% of regular occupants at any time。這是以同時可使用比例衡量的 sizing path。

B.gap: 把更高同時使用率誤認為 WELL 最低要求。
B.followup: V08 Part 1 Option 1 寫 at least 5% occupants，10% 是門檻嗎？

A.gap: 低估室內活動空間的器材同時使用容量要求。
A.followup: 若 fitness facility 只支援 3% occupants，是否達到 V08 的 5%？

D.gap: 把極低容量誤當作 V08 允許的最低比例。
D.followup: V08 要求 at any time 可供多少 regular occupants 使用，2% 與標準差距多少？

M05_Q24.stem: Proximity, connectivity, density, safety, and aesthetic are core design themes central to?
M05_Q24.explanation: Feature V05 Site Planning and Selection - Part 1 Select Sites with Pedestrian-friendly Streets 的 Solutions 說明，walkable neighborhoods 的設計主題集中在 proximity、connectivity、density、safety and aesthetics。這些主題支撐 pedestrian-friendly streets 與 walkability。

C.gap: 把較廣健康成果誤當作步行性專門術語使用。
C.followup: V05 將這些 themes 連到步行街區，是否叫 healthy communities？

A.gap: 把環境綠化概念誤認為步行社區的設計主題。
A.followup: proximity、connectivity、density 是描述綠社區，還是步行社區？

B.gap: 選了主觀吸引力詞，未對應 V05 條文用語。
B.followup: V05 Part 1 聚焦 walkability；是否稱為 desirable communities？

M05_Q25.stem: According to a 2016 study, it is estimated that what percentage of people use a wearable or app to track their health and fitness?
M05_Q25.explanation: Feature V10 Self-Monitoring - Part 1 Provide Self-Monitoring Tools 的 Solutions 引用 2016 estimates，指出 16 countries、20,000 individuals 中有 33% used a wearable or app to track physical activity and health parameters。Part 1 進一步要求 devices 至少 50% subsidy 或免費，並可追蹤 activity metrics 與 health behavior。

B.gap: 大幅低估 V10 引用的 wearable/app 使用率。
B.followup: V10 的 2016 estimate 是 33%；13% 是否在 wearable 描述中？

D.gap: 把四分之一概念誤套到 self-monitoring adoption。
D.followup: nearly a quarter 是 inactivity；V10 wearable/app 比例是多少？

C.gap: 把雇主使用 wearables 規劃比例誤當個人使用率。
C.followup: V10 另提 employers 約 44.5%；題目問 people use wearable/app 是哪個數字？

M05_Q26.stem: Studies have shown that an ergonomics program can reduce claims by up to 45% and provide an ROI of?
M05_Q26.explanation: Feature V11 Ergonomics Programming - Part 1 Implement an Ergonomics Program 說明 macro-ergonomics intervention 在 5 年內使 claims 減少 45%，並計算出 ROI of 10:1。這也是 V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design 背後的人體工學效益依據之一。

A.gap: 低估 ergonomics program 的投資回報，把 ROI 數值記得過保守。
A.followup: V11 Part 1 的案例同時提到 claims 減少 45%；它接著給出的 ROI 是幾比幾？

B.gap: 抓到 ROI 概念但混淆了標準中引用的具體比例。
B.followup: 回到 V11 Part 1 的 macro-ergonomics intervention，ROI 是用 compensation costs 與 program costs 算到多少？

C.gap: 把 ergonomics 的 ROI 效益誇大到標準未列出的數值。
C.followup: WELL 在 V11 Part 1 引用的是 45% claims reduction；原文是否有高於 10:1 的 ROI？

M05_Q27.stem: Which of the following is NOT a contributor to growing physical inactivity rates around the world?
M05_Q27.explanation: Feature V01 Active Buildings and Communities - Part 1 Design Active Buildings and Communities 的 Issue 指出 homes, schools, workplaces, communities, jobs and transportation systems 被設計成需要更少 movement、更多 sedentary activities。標準並未把 longer lifespans 列為造成 inactivity rates 增長的建築或社會環境因素。

D.gap: 忽略 transportation systems 正是標準列出的 sedentary 設計因素。
D.followup: V01 Part 1 的 Issue 如何描述 transportation systems 對日常 movement demand 的影響？

A.gap: 未連結省力化環境與 movement demand 下降的因果關係。
A.followup: 若環境與工作流程越來越 labor-saving，V01 想用 active design 補回什麼日常活動機會？

C.gap: 把 sedentary jobs 誤判為無關，忽略 workplaces 是核心場域。
C.followup: V01 Issue 同時提到 jobs 和 workplaces；這些場域如何增加 sedentary activities？

M05_Q28.stem: A project team has decided to pursue V07: Active Furnishings with several desk-top height adjustment stands. To what other WELLv2 feature could this contribute?

options.text: Feature V04: Facilities for Active Occupants

M05_Q28.explanation: Feature V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design - Part 2 Provide Height-Adjustable Work Surfaces allows supplemental solutions that raise or lower the work surface, monitor and input devices to seated and standing heights, with at least 25% of all workstations adjustable. Desktop height adjustment stands therefore can support both V07 active workstation intent and V02 seated/standing ergonomic requirements.

B.gap: 混淆 activity programming 與 workstation furniture 的合規路徑。
B.followup: V06 Part 1 要的是 no-cost programming；desk-top height adjustment stands 是否屬於課程或活動機會？

A.gap: 把工作站設備誤連到 active commuting 的 bike 與 shower 設施。
A.followup: V04 Part 1 和 Part 2 要 bike parking、showers、lockers；桌面升降架符合哪一項？

C.gap: 看到 movement 就聯想到樓梯動線，未辨識 workstation ergonomics。
C.followup: V03 的 focus 是 stair use、signage、visibility；desk-top stands 更接近 V02 哪個 Part？

M05_Q29.stem: The average adult is reported to be sedentary about how many hours every day?
M05_Q29.explanation: The WELL Movement concept overview reports that a 2011 study found self-reported time spent sitting ranged from 3-9 hours per day among adults globally. This context supports features such as V07 Active Furnishings - Part 1 Provide Active Workstations, which aims to reduce prolonged sitting.

D.gap: 低估全球成人 sitting time，忽略 sedentary behavior 的規模。
D.followup: Movement overview 說 sedentary behavior 正在上升；成人自陳 sitting time 的完整範圍是多少？

A.gap: 把範圍上限誇大，未對準 WELL 引用的 2011 study。
A.followup: 若 V07 要減少 prolonged sitting，標準前言引用的 sitting range 是否高到 14 hours？

B.gap: 記住了部分低端範圍，但漏掉標準列出的較高上限。
B.followup: Movement concept overview 的 sitting range 從 3 hours 延伸到幾 hours per day？

M05_Q30.stem: Which of the following is NOT considered sedentary behavior?

options.text: Playing fast-paced games at a computer.

M05_Q30.explanation: The WELL Movement concept defines sedentary behavior as very low-intensity, low-effort activities such as sitting, and Feature V09 Physical Activity Promotion - Part 1 includes TV viewing, recreational computer or smartphone use and gaming as sedentary behaviors to reduce. Waiting in line is not the sitting-based or screen-based sedentary behavior described there.

D.gap: 忽略 V05 把 driving 明確描述為偏向 sedentary 的活動。
D.followup: V05 Site Planning 為何要提升 walkability？它如何把 driving 與 sedentary activities 連起來？

B.gap: 被 fast-paced 誤導，未看到 computer gaming 仍屬 screen-based sedentary behavior。
B.followup: V09 Part 1 要減少 recreational computer use 與 gaming；速度快是否改變其久坐本質？

C.gap: 未掌握 sedentary behavior 的核心例子就是 sitting。
C.followup: Movement overview 用哪個典型動作說明 very low-intensity sedentary behavior？

M05_Q31.stem: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an active furnishing according to Feature V07: Active Furnishings?
M05_Q31.explanation: Feature V07 Active Furnishings - Part 1 Provide Active Workstations lists manual or electric height adjustable desks, supplemental raise/lower solutions, treadmill desks, bicycle desks and stepper machines. Wellness ball chair is not included in that enumerated active workstation list.

A.gap: 把 V07 明列的 height adjustable desk 誤認為未被提到。
A.followup: V07 Part 1 的第一類 active workstation 是否就是可調 seated/standing height 的 desk？

B.gap: 忽略 treadmill desk 是 V07 明確列出的 active workstation。
B.followup: V07 Part 1 在 supplemental solutions 後，還列出哪些帶運動形式的 desks？

D.gap: 未把 portable pedal 對應到 bicycle desk 類型的 active workstation。
D.followup: V07 Part 1 提到 bicycle desk；portable desk pedal 和這個類型有何相似？

M05_Q32.stem: Two types of exercise equipment should be provided in a quantity that would allow use by what percentage of regular building occupants in Part 1: Provide Indoor Activity Spaces of Feature V08: Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment?
M05_Q32.explanation: Feature V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment - Part 1 Provide Indoor Activity Spaces, Option 1 requires at least two types of exercise equipment in quantities that allow use by at least 5% of regular occupants at any time. This threshold applies to an on-site physical activity space provided at no cost.

C.gap: 把 V08 equipment capacity 門檻提高到標準未要求的比例。
C.followup: V08 Part 1 Option 1 對 regular occupants 的 use capacity 是 at least 幾 percent？

A.gap: 低估 V08 對室內運動設備可用量的最低要求。
A.followup: 若 fitness facility 要讓 occupants at any time 使用，V08 Part 1 的最低比例是多少？

B.gap: 將 activity space 規模想得過小，未符合 V08 的占用門檻。
B.followup: V08 Part 1 為何要求 two types of equipment 還要足夠多少 regular occupants 同時使用？

M05_Q33.stem: A project may provide access to off-site fitness facilities in Part 1: Provide Indoor Activity Spaces of Feature V08: Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment. What distance from the project boundary is acceptable for an off-site fitness facility?
M05_Q33.explanation: Feature V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment - Part 1 Provide Indoor Activity Spaces, Option 2 requires access to a fitness facility within a 650 ft (200 m) walk distance of the project boundary at no cost. The distance is measured from the WELL project boundary, not merely from an entrance.

D.gap: 選了較嚴格但非標準指定的 off-site facility 距離。
D.followup: V08 Part 1 Option 2 對 off-site fitness facility 的 walk distance 是 150 m 還是 200 m？

C.gap: 把可接受距離放寬，超過 V08 對 proximity 的限制。
C.followup: 若 facility 離 project boundary 有 250 m，是否仍在 V08 Part 1 的 650 ft 範圍內？

B.gap: 將 V08 的 650 ft threshold 誤縮成 100 m。
B.followup: V08 Part 1 的 off-site pathway 寫的是 650 ft；換算成 metric 是多少？

M05_Q34.stem: To earn points for Part 1: Design Aesthetic Staircases of Feature V03: Circulation Network, a project team would use what to promote the use of stairs?

options.text: Fire alarms located in the stairwells.

M05_Q34.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 1 Design Aesthetic Staircases requires at least one open staircase serving all floors to include at least two aesthetic elements on each floor, such as artwork and natural design elements including plants. These enhancements are intended to encourage everyday stair use.

D.gap: 把生命安全設備誤當成 V03 的 aesthetic stair prompt。
D.followup: V03 Part 1 列的是 music、artwork、light、daylight、nature、gamification；fire alarms 屬於哪類要求？

B.gap: 把氣味改善誤認為標準列舉的樓梯美化元素。
B.followup: V03 Part 1 是否列出 air fresheners，還是列出 artwork 與 natural design elements？

A.gap: 混淆鼓勵 stair use 與限制 elevator 使用的強制策略。
A.followup: V03 的策略是 aesthetic design 和 prompts；限制 elevator hours 是否出現在 Part 1 清單？

M05_Q35.stem: At an office building, occupants are discouraged by high gym member fees. According to Feature V09: Physical Activity Promotion, what should be offered to building occupants to encourage participation in fitness programs?

options.text: Free fitness education courses each quarter.
Reductions in health care premiums based on physical activity engagement.
The option of a standing bike desk or treadmill desk for each employee.

M05_Q35.explanation: Feature V09 Physical Activity Promotion - Part 1 Offer Physical Activity Incentives lists reductions in health care premiums based on physical activity engagement as one eligible incentive, alongside rewards, subsidies, flexible work hours and paid time off. The requirement is about incentive programs that promote participation, not furnishing every workstation.

A.gap: 混淆 education programming 與 V09 指定的 physical activity incentives。
A.followup: V09 Part 1 要至少兩種 incentives；quarterly education course 是否列在 eligible employee incentive 清單中？

B.gap: 把 active furnishings 當作解決 gym cost barrier 的 incentive。
B.followup: Treadmill desks 屬於 V07 active workstations；V09 Part 1 對高 gym fee 提供什麼 financial incentive？

D.gap: 誤把 workstation choice 視為 V09 的 fitness program participation incentive。
D.followup: V09 Part 1 關注 rewards、subsidies、premium reductions；standing bike desk 是哪個 feature 的語境？

M05_Q36.stem: Which of the following is an indirect benefit of regular physical activity?
M05_Q36.explanation: Feature V06 Physical Activity Opportunities - Part 1 Offer Physical Activity Opportunities cites workplace program benefits such as reduced healthcare costs, reduced disability costs and days lost, reduced absenteeism and increased productivity. Improved job performance is therefore an indirect organizational benefit, while the other choices are more direct health or fitness outcomes.

C.gap: 把直接身體健康效果誤當成 workplace indirect benefit。
C.followup: V06 的 workplace program evidence 除了健康外，還提到哪些 productivity 或 absenteeism 相關效益？

B.gap: 混淆 regular activity 的疾病預防效果與間接工作效益。
B.followup: V01 Issue 確實提 chronic disease；但 V06 提到的 workplace economic benefit 是什麼？

A.gap: 把維持 fitness 本身當作間接效益，未區分 outcome 層級。
A.followup: 如果 V06 的活動機會改善 fitness，對雇主端間接可能反映在哪些工作表現指標？

M05_Q37.stem: What is the estimated price of physical inactivity on the healthcare system globally?
M05_Q37.explanation: The WELL Movement concept overview states that in 2013 physical inactivity cost healthcare systems globally about $54 billion and contributed nearly $14 billion in productivity losses. This economic burden is part of the rationale for V01 Active Buildings and Communities - Part 1.

B.gap: 記得是 billion 級成本，但低估了 WELL 引用的金額。
B.followup: Movement overview 同一句還提 nearly $14 billion productivity losses；healthcare system cost 是多少 billion？

D.gap: 把全球 healthcare burden 的量級從 billion 誤降為 million。
D.followup: WELL 用全球 healthcare systems 說明 inactivity 成本；million 級是否足以對應該段脈絡？

A.gap: 抓到數字 54 但誤判單位，嚴重低估公共健康成本。
A.followup: Movement concept overview 的 estimate 是 $54 million 還是 $54 billion？它還提到多少 productivity losses？

M05_Q38.stem: Which one of these is considered an environmental barrier to physical activity?
M05_Q38.explanation: Feature V06 Physical Activity Opportunities - Part 1 Offer Physical Activity Opportunities identifies environmental barriers such as availability of sidewalks, parks and bicycle lanes. Lack of bike lanes directly matches that built-environment barrier category.

B.gap: 把 personal health risk 誤歸類為 environmental barrier。
B.followup: V06 Issue 同時列 fear of injury 和 environmental barriers；bike lanes 屬於哪一邊？

C.gap: 把 financial barrier 與 built-environment barrier 混為一談。
C.followup: 成本可能影響參與，但 V06 所稱 environmental barriers 舉了 sidewalks、parks 還有什麼？

D.gap: 把一般 travel constraints 誤認為具體環境設施缺口。
D.followup: V06 Issue 列 travel 和 environmental barriers；缺少 bicycle lanes 比 travel conditions 更具體在哪裡？

M05_Q39.stem: A company wants to encourage its staff to use fitness trackers. Which of the following would help them earn 1 point under Feature V10: Self-Monitoring?

options.text: Making individual fitness data available to all staff.
Offering a choice of colors to employees.
Subsidizing part of the cost of any such device.
Making the device free to employees.

M05_Q39.explanation: Feature V10 Self-Monitoring - Part 1 Provide Self-Monitoring Tools requires devices such as wearable fitness trackers to be available at no cost or subsidized by at least 50%, while allowing users to monitor their own metrics over time. Providing the device free to employees satisfies the cost requirement without raising the privacy concern of exposing individual data.

B.gap: 把使用偏好選項誤當成 V10 的 self-monitoring compliance requirement。
B.followup: V10 Part 1 要看 cost、metrics、dashboard；device color choice 是否影響這些要求？

C.gap: 只記得 subsidy 方向，漏掉至少 50% 的明確門檻。
C.followup: V10 Part 1 不是任意補助即可；subsidy 需要至少達到多少比例？

A.gap: 誤解 self-monitoring 為公開個資，違背 user-owned metrics 脈絡。
A.followup: V10 Part 1 要 users monitor their own metrics；把 individual fitness data 給全體 staff 是否符合？

M05_Q40.stem: A new office building's main entrance is located on a wide, well-used sidewalk. What other design elements should the building incorporate under Part 1: Select Sites with Pedestrian-friendly Streets of Feature V05: Site Planning and Selection?
M05_Q40.explanation: Feature V05 Site Planning and Selection - Part 1 Select Sites with Pedestrian-friendly Streets includes buffer protections along sidewalks such as benches, trees and planters, and also recognizes biophilic design elements on the street-level facade. Planters therefore fit the pedestrian-friendly streetscape strategy.

C.gap: 接近 benches 概念但選到標準未如此表述的 seating 類型。
C.followup: V05 Part 1 的 sidewalk buffer examples 包含 benches、trees、planters；all weather seating 是否被明列？

B.gap: 把社區資訊設施誤當作 pedestrian-friendly streetscape design element。
B.followup: V05 Part 1 強調 sidewalk buffers 和 facade design；community bulletin board 對 walkability 有哪個條文支持？

D.gap: 混淆 V04 bike parking 與 V05 pedestrian-friendly street elements。
D.followup: Bike racks 更接近 V04 cycling infrastructure；V05 Part 1 的 sidewalk buffer examples 有哪些？

M05_Q41.stem: Beth is a new employee at a company in a WELL building. Which of the following is Beth likely to see at her new workstation?

options.text: A welcome letter from the CEO.

M05_Q41.explanation: Feature V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design - Part 2 Provide Height-Adjustable Work Surfaces requires at least 25% of all workstations to be user-adjustable for both seated and standing work. The same feature also requires ergonomic orientation and chair adjustability, making adjustable workstation furniture the relevant WELL item.

B.gap: 忽略 V02 Part 3 要 workstation seating 具備 adjustability。
B.followup: V02 Part 3 對 chair adjustability 包含 seat height、seat depth，還有哪類 armrest 或 backrest 調整？

D.gap: 把一般 onboarding 禮貌流程誤當作 WELL workstation requirement。
D.followup: Beth 的 workstation 要對應 V02；welcome letter 與 ergonomic workstation design 有哪個 Part 連結？

C.gap: 把 sustainability office supplies 誤認為 Movement concept 的人體工學要求。
C.followup: V02 關心 neutral posture 和 adjustable furnishings；recycled notebooks 是否改善 seated/standing work fit？

M05_Q42.stem: Besides safety issues, what other 2 reasons are given for not choosing to bike to work?

options.text: No bike parking and no changing facilities.
No changing facilities and too expensive.
Bad weather and poor road conditions.
Bad weather and no bike parking.

M05_Q42.explanation: Feature V04 Facilities for Active Occupants - Part 1 Provide Cycling Infrastructure states that, besides safety concerns and car-culture norms, lack of bike parking and post-commute facilities are key reasons people do not cycle to work. Part 2 then addresses showers, lockers and changing facilities to remove that barrier.

D.gap: 抓到 bike parking，但把 post-commute facilities 誤換成 weather。
D.followup: V04 Issue 在 bike parking 之外，還提到哪種通勤後設施會影響 cycling to workplace？

B.gap: 抓到 changing facilities，但把 bike parking 漏成成本因素。
B.followup: V04 Summary 要同時提供 bike storage、showers、changing facilities；缺哪個會直接阻礙 cycling？

C.gap: 把一般戶外條件當成 V04 明列的 workplace facility barrier。
C.followup: V04 的解方是 bike parking、showers、lockers；bad weather 和 road conditions 是否是這個 Part 的主軸？

M05_Q43.stem: A new construction project will have 3 floors with stairwells servicing all floors. According to Part 2: Integrate Point-of-Decision Signage of Feature V03: Circulation Network, which one of these is not the best area to place signage to encourage stair use?

options.text: Near the base of each stairwell.

M05_Q43.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 2 Integrate Point-of-Decision Signage requires signage near the main building entrance or reception desk, at elevator or escalator banks on each floor and at the base of stairs and stairwell re-entry points on each floor. A parking deck is not one of the specified point-of-decision signage locations.

A.gap: 誤把 elevator bank 這個 required signage location 排除掉。
A.followup: V03 Part 2 為何要求在 elevator or escalator banks on each floor 放 signage？

B.gap: 未把 front door 對應到 main building entrance 的 signage 要求。
B.followup: V03 Part 2 第一個 signage location 是 main building entrance 或 reception desk；front door 接近哪一項？

C.gap: 忽略 stair base 和 re-entry points 是 V03 明定位置。
C.followup: V03 Part 2 對 stairs 的 signage 是只在入口，還是也在 base of stairs and re-entry points？

M05_Q44.stem: How close must the shower and changing facilities be to the project boundary under Part 2: Provide Showers, Lockers and Changing Facilities of Feature V04: Facilities for Active Occupants?
M05_Q44.explanation: Feature V04 Facilities for Active Occupants - Part 2 Provide Showers, Lockers and Changing Facilities requires showers with changing facilities to be available within a 650 ft (200 m) walk distance of the project boundary. It also requires at least five lockers for every shower, co-located with shower facilities.

B.gap: 把 V04 shower distance 誤縮成標準未使用的 450 ft。
B.followup: V04 Part 2 的 showers with changing facilities 應在 project boundary 幾 ft walk distance 內？

D.gap: 把可接受步行距離放寬到超過 V04 的 proximity limit。
D.followup: 若 shower facilities 離 boundary 有 1,000 ft，是否仍符合 V04 Part 2 的 650 ft requirement？

A.gap: 接近但仍高估 V04 對 shower/changing facilities 的距離門檻。
A.followup: V04 多個 proximity 條文使用 650 ft；Part 2 是否允許 750 ft walk distance？

M05_Q45.stem: Which of the following is NOT a design-based optimization to satisfy Feature V01: Active Buildings and Communities?

options.text: Feature V08: Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment
Feature V05: Site Planning and Selection

M05_Q45.explanation: Feature V01 Active Buildings and Communities - Part 1 Design Active Buildings and Communities requires at least one point in V03 Circulation Network, V04 Facilities for Active Occupants, V05 Site Planning and Selection or V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment. V09 Physical Activity Promotion is a separate incentive program feature and is not listed as a V01 design-based optimization pathway.

D.gap: 把 V01 明列的 Circulation Network pathway 誤當成非設計優化。
D.followup: V01 Part 1 的第一個可選 feature 是否就是 V03 Circulation Network？

C.gap: 忽略 Site Planning and Selection 是 V01 的列舉 pathway。
C.followup: V01 Part 1 是否把 V05 納入 design-based optimizations，並用 walkability 支援 movement？

A.gap: 未辨識 physical activity spaces 也被 V01 納入設計型路徑。
A.followup: V01 Part 1 列出的四個 features 中，是否包含 V08 Physical Activity Spaces and Equipment？

options.text: A way to encourage healthy behaviors.
A way to have fun in the workplace.

M05_Q46.explanation: Feature V03 Circulation Network - Part 1 Design Aesthetic Staircases lists gamification as an eligible stair enhancement, and the V03 Solutions text defines it as leveraging game elements to encourage desired behaviors. In that feature, the desired behavior is healthier movement such as stairclimbing.

B.gap: 把可能的附帶情緒效果誤當成 gamification 的 WELL 定義。
B.followup: V03 Solutions 說 gamification leverages game elements；它要鼓勵的是 morale 還是 stairclimbing behavior？

A.gap: 完全反向理解 gamification，誤以為它會降低 productive behavior。
A.followup: V03 將 gamification 放在鼓勵 stair use 的策略中；這與 lessening productivity 有何關係？

D.gap: 只看到 fun，漏掉其目的在於 behavior change。
D.followup: V03 的 gamification 可以 fun，但 WELL 關注它鼓勵哪種 healthy behavior？

M05_Q47.stem: Which is not a good way to calculate the number of peak visitors?
M05_Q47.explanation: Feature V04 Facilities for Active Occupants - Part 1 Provide Cycling Infrastructure uses peak visitors to size short-term bike parking at at least 2.5% of peak visitors, with minimum spaces. Actual figures, institutional knowledge and default occupancy assumptions are calculation bases, while visitor badges are an access-control practice rather than a calculation method.

B.gap: 誤排除 actual figures，忽略真實訪客數是可用估算依據。
B.followup: V04 Part 1 要用 peak visitors 算 2.5% bike parking；若已有 actual figures，為何不能用？

A.gap: 低估 institutional knowledge 在 visitor peak estimation 中的用途。
A.followup: 若沒有完整 visitor counts，熟悉營運模式的 institutional knowledge 如何幫助估 peak visitors？

D.gap: 把 default occupancy assumptions 誤當成不可用的估算方式。
D.followup: V04 需要 peak visitor 數量；沒有實測資料時，default occupancy assumptions 可提供什麼基準？

M05_Q48.stem: When is a smartphone app considered a self monitoring device according to Feature V10: Self-Monitoring?

options.text: A smart phone app is never acceptable for Feature V10.
When the app is supplied by the company's insurance provider.
When both the app and the monitoring device are subsidized by the company.
When the smart phone is supplied by the company.

M05_Q48.explanation: Feature V10 Self-Monitoring - Part 1 Provide Self-Monitoring Tools requires devices to be provided at no cost or subsidized by at least 50%, and to let users monitor their own metrics over time, including at least two physical activity metrics and one additional health behavior. A smartphone app can support the pathway when it is tied to a subsidized monitoring device that satisfies these measurement requirements.

D.gap: 把 smartphone ownership 誤當成 V10 對 monitoring tool 的核心要求。
D.followup: V10 Part 1 要的是 metrics monitoring 和 subsidy；company-supplied phone 本身能量測兩種 activity metrics 嗎？

B.gap: 誤以為供應者身份比 device subsidy 與 measurement capability 更重要。
B.followup: V10 Part 1 有要求 insurance provider 供應 app，還是要求 no cost/50% subsidy 和 metrics？

A.gap: 過度排除 app，忽略 V10 Issue/Solutions 提到 wearable or app tracking。
A.followup: V10 的背景提到 wearable or app tracking；app 在什麼條件下能與 monitoring device 一起符合 Part 1？

M05_Q49.stem: One of the top drivers of global disability in 2016 is?
M05_Q49.explanation: Feature V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design - Part 1 Support Visual Ergonomics states in its Issue section that in 2016 musculoskeletal disorders ranked among the top drivers of global disability. This is the health basis for reducing physical strain through ergonomic workstation design.

A.gap: 把 Air concept 的污染風險誤帶入 Movement ergonomics 題目。
A.followup: V02 的 Issue 聚焦 workstation strain；它列為 global disability driver 的是哪類 disorders？

B.gap: 把營養缺乏類問題誤認為 V02 引用的 disability driver。
B.followup: V02 Ergonomic Workstation Design 與 vitamin deficiency 有何關聯？標準提到的是哪種身體系統問題？

D.gap: 混淆 inactivity 的 chronic disease 與 ergonomics 的 disability driver。
D.followup: V01 會提 cardiovascular disease；但 V02 Issue 為何特別談 musculoskeletal disorders？

M05_Q50.stem: To support remote work ergonomics, a company can do any of the following except?

options.text: Ask for an inspection of the home office area.

M05_Q50.explanation: Feature V11 Ergonomics Programming - Part 3 Support Remote Work Ergonomics requires the ergonomics program to support remote scenarios and make ergonomic furnishings available through pre-approved direct purchases, reimbursement or subsidies. It does not require an inspection of the home office area as one of the listed support mechanisms.

C.gap: 誤排除 Part 3 明列的 pre-approved direct-purchases 支援方式。
C.followup: V11 Part 3 說 ergonomic furnishings 可透過哪些三種購買或補助方式提供？

B.gap: 忽略 subsidies 是 remote ergonomic furnishings 的合規提供方式。
B.followup: 如果 remote worker 需要 ergonomic chair，V11 Part 3 是否允許 company 用 subsidy 支援？

A.gap: 把 reimbursement 誤認為不合規，但它是條文列出的方式。
A.followup: V11 Part 3 在 pre-approved direct-purchases 之外，是否還明列 reimbursement？

M06_Q01.stem: Feature T05: Radiant Thermal Comfort can be achieved through the use of radiant heating and cooling elements, independent of ventilation systems, in which two space types?
M06_Q01.explanation: Feature T05 Radiant Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Implement Radiant Heating 與 Part 2 Implement Radiant Cooling 在目前 WELL v2 標準中要求至少 50% of regularly occupied project area 採用 radiant ceilings, walls, floors 或符合面積條件的 radiant panels。標準文字未把達成範圍限定為兩種特定 space types，因此此題的舊式表述需要保留疑點。

D.gap: 把會議用途空間誤認為 T05 指定範圍。
D.followup: T05 Part 1/Part 2 寫的是特定房間類型，還是 regularly occupied project area 的百分比？

A.gap: 抓到 regularly occupied 空間，但錯把範圍限成兩類。
A.followup: 若 T05 要求至少 50% project area，是否只鎖定 offices 與 conference rooms？

B.gap: 混淆通行空間與 regularly occupied area 的適用邏輯。
B.followup: Stairways 通常是否屬於 T05 計算的 regularly occupied project area？

M06_Q02.stem: According to the WELL Thermal Comfort concept the human body maintains a stable internal body temperature using what process?
M06_Q02.explanation: Homeothermy 指人體透過熱取得與熱散失的平衡維持核心體溫穩定；這是 T01 Thermal Performance - Part 1 Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment 所處理的熱舒適生理基礎。T01 的 requirement 以 PMV range、operative temperature、humidity 等條件建立可接受熱環境，但提供的標準文字未直接列出 homeothermy 一詞。

A.gap: 誤把變溫動物體溫策略套到人體熱平衡。
A.followup: T01 關注人體在室內維持穩定 comfort，這比較接近恆溫還是隨環境變溫？

B.gap: 選到非 WELL 熱舒適脈絡中的生物學概念。
B.followup: 題目問人體穩定 internal body temperature，這和竊取外部熱源的概念相同嗎？

D.gap: 混淆間歇性體溫變化與一般人體恆溫機制。
D.followup: 在 T01 的 thermal comfort 條件下，人體核心體溫是長期穩定還是週期性大幅變動？

M06_Q03.stem: In order to achieve Feature T05: Radiant Thermal Comfort, what minimum percentage of floor area in all offices and other regularly occupied spaces must meet the requirements set for in the ASHRAE Standard 55-2013 for thermal comfort through the use of hydronic radiant heating and/or cooling systems or along with electric radiant systems?
M06_Q03.explanation: Feature T05 Radiant Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Implement Radiant Heating 要求至少 50% of regularly occupied project area 由 radiant ceilings, walls, floors 或合格 radiant panels 供暖；Part 2 Implement Radiant Cooling 對 cooling 也使用相同的 50% 門檻。這個最低比例是判斷 radiant thermal comfort 覆蓋範圍的關鍵。

A.gap: 低估 T05 radiant 系統覆蓋面積門檻。
A.followup: T05 Part 1/Part 2 對 regularly occupied project area 的最低覆蓋比例是否達一半？

D.gap: 把少量示範性安裝誤當成 T05 合規比例。
D.followup: 如果只有五分之一面積採 radiant system，能否代表主要 occupied area 的熱舒適策略？

C.gap: 將其他 WELL 比例要求誤套到 radiant coverage。
C.followup: T05 的 radiant heating/cooling 覆蓋比例，是 30% 還是標準明列的 50%？

M06_Q04.stem: An open office with large west-facing windows has 29 identical workstations, 10 of which are located next to the exterior windows. According to Feature T04: Individual Thermal Control, which of the following allows occupants to work in a space with their desired temperature?
M06_Q04.explanation: Feature T04 Individual Thermal Control - Part 1 Provide Personal Cooling Options 與 Part 2 Provide Personal Heating Options 要求 all regular occupants 能透過個人 cooling/heating solution 調整自己的 thermal environment，例如 desk fan、heated chair、footwarmers 或能在 15 minutes 內造成 PMV +/-0.5 change 的方案。開放辦公室中的個人化設備比只處理窗邊日射更符合此 feature。

D.gap: 把遮陽控制誤認為個人冷暖設備要求。
D.followup: Curtains/blinds 能否讓每位 regular occupant 分別達到 T04 的 personal cooling/heating control？

C.gap: 混淆外窗熱負荷處理與個人熱舒適控制。
C.followup: Tinted windows 改變的是立面日射，是否提供每位 occupant 自己的 PMV 調整？

B.gap: 忽略共享 thermal zone 中 thermostat 不能代表個人控制。
B.followup: T04 允許 thermostat 時，該 room or thermal zone 是否可由多人 regularly occupied？

M06_Q05.stem: In the WELL Thermal Comfort concept which of the following is NOT a benefit of radiant temperature systems?
M06_Q05.explanation: Feature T05 Radiant Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Implement Radiant Heating and Part 2 Implement Radiant Cooling 的 intent 是 maximize volume of the space、reduce dust transmission、improve ventilation control and increase thermal comfort。Mean radiant temperature 是六個 core thermal comfort parameters 之一，radiant systems 會影響它，但標準沒有把「提高」它本身列為 benefit。

A.gap: 把冬季濕度效果與 T05 明列利益混在一起。
A.followup: T05 intent 明列哪些 benefit？其中是否直接寫到 higher relative humidity in winter？

B.gap: 忽略 radiant systems 減少 forced-air allergens circulation。
B.followup: T05 Solutions 說 radiant heating/cooling 不用 forced air 分配，這會如何影響 dust/allergens？

C.gap: 未連結 T05 intent 中 maximize volume 的空間效益。
C.followup: 若 radiant system 可整合於 floors、walls、ceilings，T05 的 maximize volume 指向什麼效益？

M06_Q06.stem: According to the Thermal Comfort concept, how many of the office buildings surveyed in the U.S. provided thermal environments that met generally accepted goals of occupant satisfaction?
M06_Q06.explanation: Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort 的 Issue 指出，U.S. surveyed office buildings 中只有 11% provide thermal environments that meet generally accepted goals of human satisfaction。這也支撐 T02 使用 occupant survey 並以 80% 或 90% satisfaction thresholds 給分。

A.gap: 把低滿意建築比例放大成約五分之一。
A.followup: T02 Issue 提到的 U.S. office buildings 比例，是 11% 還是接近 20%？

C.gap: 過度低估符合 occupant satisfaction 目標的建築比例。
C.followup: T02 背景數據是否低到 5%，或其實是略高於十分之一？

B.gap: 混入其他滿意度或健康研究百分比而非此調查值。
B.followup: 題目問 surveyed office buildings，T02 Issue 中該數字是否接近四分之一？

M06_Q07.stem: In the WELL Thermal Comfort concept how many primary personal and environmental variables contribute to an occupant's thermal comfort?
M06_Q07.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 1 Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment 的 Solutions 說 mechanically conditioned spaces 以 six core parameters 預測熱舒適：air temperature、humidity、air movement、mean radiant temperature、metabolic rate、clothing insulation。這六項構成熱舒適的主要個人與環境變數。

C.gap: 只記得環境參數，漏掉 metabolic rate 與 clothing。
C.followup: T01 的 six core parameters 是否同時包含 personal variables 與 environmental variables？

D.gap: 把 secondary factors 誤加入 primary factors 數量。
D.followup: T04 提到 age、sex 等 secondary factors，是否會增加 T01 的 primary parameter 數量？

B.gap: 漏算 mean radiant temperature 或 clothing insulation 等核心項。
B.followup: T01 列出 air temperature、humidity、air movement 後，還有哪些項目補足六個？

M06_Q08.stem: In the Thermal Comfort concept, which of the following ASHRAE Standard aims to provide a thermal environment, where at least 80% of people will perceive the thermal environment as acceptable.
M06_Q08.explanation: Feature T04 Individual Thermal Control - Part 1 Provide Personal Cooling Options 的 Issue 明確指出 many thermal comfort standards such as ASHRAE 55 aim to provide a thermal environment where minimum 80% of people perceive it as acceptable。T01 Thermal Performance 也引用 ASHRAE 55-2013 作為 thermal comfort conditions 的依據。

C.gap: 把非熱舒適標準號碼誤認為 ASHRAE thermal comfort。
C.followup: T01 references 中列出的 thermal environmental conditions standard 是哪個 ASHRAE 編號？

D.gap: 選到不存在於 WELL 熱舒適要求的 ASHRAE 編號。
D.followup: T04 Issue 說至少 80% 可接受時，括號中舉的是 ASHRAE 55 還是 30？

A.gap: 混淆標準編號，未對上 WELL 引用的 ASHRAE 55。
A.followup: T01 Part 1 的 mechanically conditioned comfort condition 引用哪個 ASHRAE standard？

M06_Q09.stem: Which of the following best describes homeothermy from the Thermal Comfort concept?

options.text: Stabling of external body temperature regardless of internal influence and regulated by the amygdala.
The balancing of heat gains and losses to maintain the body's core temperature within its narrow range, 36-38 degrees C [97-100 degrees F], and regulated by the hypothalamus.
Maintaining body core heat through internal means and regulated by the cerebellum.
Controlling body core temperature through internal means and regulated by the thalamus.

M06_Q09.explanation: Homeothermy 的核心是 balancing heat gains and losses to maintain core temperature，這與 T01 Thermal Performance - Part 1 Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment 以 thermal comfort parameters 控制人體熱交換環境的邏輯一致。提供的 WELL v2 標準文字可佐證 thermal comfort 生理背景與 PMV/temperature requirements，但未直接列出 36-38 degrees C 或 hypothalamus 這些細節。

C.gap: 抓到核心體溫但誤判為 cerebellum 調節。
C.followup: 題目描述 homeothermy 時，除了 core temperature，還要辨認哪個腦部調節中樞？

A.gap: 誤把外部體表溫度穩定當作 homeothermy 定義。
A.followup: Homeothermy 關注的是 external body temperature，還是 body core temperature 的窄範圍？

D.gap: 把 thalamus 與 hypothalamus 的體溫調節角色混淆。
D.followup: 若 T01 關注人體熱平衡，生理調節核心通常會指向 thalamus 還是 hypothalamus？

M06_Q10.stem: To achieve Part 1: Survey for Thermal Comfort of Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort, a post-occupancy survey should be administered how many times per year and in what months?

options.text: Once at the beginning of the year.
3 times per year in January, April and August.
4 times per year, once in each fiscal quarter.
At least twice a year, including once in June, July or August and once in December, January or February, at least six months after occupancy.

M06_Q10.explanation: Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort 要求 post-occupancy survey at least twice a year，且至少入住 six months 後執行；時間需包含一次 June, July or August，以及一次 December, January or February。這是用來捕捉冷暖季 occupant satisfaction 的固定要求。

A.gap: 忽略 T02 需要冷暖季各一次的調查頻率。
A.followup: T02 Part 1 是否允許一年只調查一次，還是要求夏季與冬季各涵蓋一次？

B.gap: 加入不必要月份，未對準 T02 指定季節窗口。
B.followup: T02 指定的是 January、April、August，還是 June-August 加 December-February？

C.gap: 把季度管理節奏誤當成 WELL survey requirement。
C.followup: T02 Part 1 要的是每季一次，還是至少兩次且落在指定夏冬月份？

M06_Q11.stem: Salesforce just occupied new office space in Chicago, Illinois. The office has 900 occupants that regularly report to this location. What is the minimum response rate needed for the surveys conducted at least twice a year for Part 1: Survey for Thermal Comfort of Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort?
M06_Q11.explanation: Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort 的 response table 規定，regular occupants more than 45 時，minimum number of responses 是 35% of those regular occupants。900 occupants 超過 45，因此採 35% response rate，而不是小樣本門檻。

B.gap: 把少於二十人的 80% 門檻誤套到大型辦公室。
B.followup: T02 response table 中 80% 適用於 less than 20 occupants，900 人應看哪一列？

C.gap: 誤以為大型樣本仍需接近全員回覆。
C.followup: More than 45 regular occupants 時，T02 是否要求 75%，還是明列較低比例？

A.gap: 低估 T02 對大型專案 survey responses 的最低比例。
A.followup: 900 occupants 屬於 more than 45，表格中的 minimum response rate 是否只有 25%？

M06_Q12.stem: If a project is able to exceed the thermal comfort satisfaction thresholds by 90% or more, how many points is the project awarded for Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort?
M06_Q12.explanation: Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort 的 satisfaction thresholds table 將 80% of regular occupants 對應 2 points，90% of regular occupants 對應 3 points。題目中的 90% or more 落在最高列出的 T02 門檻。

A.gap: 低估 T02 90% satisfaction threshold 的給分。
A.followup: T02 表格中 90% regular occupants satisfied 對應幾點，而不是最低點數？

C.gap: 把 80% threshold 的分數誤用到 90% threshold。
C.followup: T02 Part 1 中 80% 與 90% satisfaction thresholds 各自對應幾點？

B.gap: 超出 T02 Verified Thermal Comfort 此 feature 可得點數。
B.followup: T02 的 satisfaction thresholds table 最高列出幾點，是否有 4 points？

M06_Q13.stem: Projects seeking Feature T03: Thermal Zoning compliance should provide control over temperature in the space through thermostat being present in the thermal zone , or...

options.text: Work stations being located near operable windows
A lax dress-code and company-issued coats and blankets.
A digital interface accessible to occupants on a computer or phone
Supplied personal Fans and Space Heaters

M06_Q13.explanation: Feature T03 Thermal Zoning - Part 1 Provide Thermostat Control 要求至少 90% of regularly occupied spaces 透過兩種方式之一提供 temperature control：thermostats present within the thermal zone，或 occupants 可用 computer or phone 存取的 digital interface。這是 zoning 控制，不是個人風扇或 dress code。

D.gap: 混淆 T03 thermal zoning 與 T04 個人冷暖設備。
D.followup: T03 Part 1 要求 thermostat 或 digital interface，personal fans 更像哪個 feature 的策略？

B.gap: 把 T04 flexible dress code 誤當成 T03 zoning 控制。
B.followup: Dress code 在 T04 Part 3 出現，是否能替代 T03 的 thermal zone control？

A.gap: 誤把 operable windows 位置當作 thermostat control。
A.followup: T03 Part 1 的 control over temperature 是否以 workstation 靠窗來認定？

M06_Q14.stem: With the exception of high-humidity areas, the mechanical system has the capability of maintaining relative humidity at all times by what methods covered in Feature T07: Humidity Control.

options.text: Adding or removing moisture from the air.

M06_Q14.explanation: Feature T07 Humidity Control - Part 1 Manage Relative Humidity, Option 1 Mechanical humidity control 要求 all regularly occupied areas except high-humidity areas 的 mechanical system 能在 all times 維持 relative humidity between 30% and 60%。達成方法明列為 adding or removing moisture from the air。

B.gap: 把水資源回收誤認為 humidity control 機制。
B.followup: T07 Part 1 說 mechanical system 控制 relative humidity，是處理空氣含濕量還是 reclaiming water？

C.gap: 把空氣流動誤當成可直接加減濕的要求。
C.followup: Air circulation 能移動空氣，但 T07 Option 1 要求的是 adding/removing 哪個成分？

D.gap: 未辨認 T07 只列出單一加濕除濕方法。
D.followup: T07 Part 1 的文字是否同時列出 water reclaiming、air circulation 與加減濕？

M06_Q15.stem: According to Feature T09 β: Outdoor Thermal Comfort, at least 50% of what areas must be shaded for more than half of the daylight hours each day by tree canopies, awnings, or other structures?

options.text: Plazas, seating areas and other outdoor areas of congregation

M06_Q15.explanation: Feature T09β Outdoor Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Manage Outdoor Heat, Option 1 Outdoor shading 要求 at least 50% of pedestrian pathways and building entrances 在每天超過一半 daylight hours 有 tree canopies、awnings 或其他 structures 遮蔭。Parking spaces 是 at least 25%，plazas/seating/congregation areas 則是 between 25% and 75%。

A.gap: 把 congregation areas 的 25%-75% 範圍誤當 50% 題目門檻。
A.followup: T09β Part 1 對 plazas and seating areas 寫的是固定 50%，還是 25% 到 75%？

B.gap: 混淆 parking spaces 的 25% shading 門檻。
B.followup: T09β Outdoor shading 中 parking spaces 的最低遮蔭比例是多少，是否達 50%？

D.gap: 忽略 T09β 對不同 outdoor areas 設定不同百分比。
D.followup: 如果三類 outdoor areas 門檻不同，為什麼不能把 50% 套用到 all of the above？

M06_Q16.stem: Projects seeking points in Part 1: Provide Windows with Multiple Opening Modes of Feature T08 B: Enhanced Operable Windows will provide instructions for window operation through signage or other communications that indicate which of the following:

options.text: Windows are not to be opened when mechanical cooling is in operation (not required if no mechanical cooling is present or if mechanical cooling system is configured to disengage automatically when windows are open).
Windows with low openings are to be used during mild and/or warm weather.
Windows with high openings (if present) are to be used in cold weather.

M06_Q16.explanation: Feature T08β Enhanced Operable Windows - Part 1 Provide Windows with Multiple Opening Modes, Option 2 Window operation 要求 signage or communications 指出三件事：low openings 用於 mild and/or warm weather、mechanical cooling 運轉時不開窗、high openings 如存在則用於 cold weather。三項都是同一 Part 的 operation instructions。

A.gap: 只抓到機械冷房限制，漏掉高低開口使用情境。
A.followup: T08β Part 1 Option 2 是否只要求 cooling 時不要開窗，還有其他 opening-mode 指示？

C.gap: 只記得 cold weather 高開口，漏掉其餘 signage。
C.followup: 除了 high openings in cold weather，T08β 還要求說明 low openings 與 mechanical cooling 嗎？

B.gap: 只記得暖和天氣低開口，未完整列出指示。
B.followup: T08β Window operation 的 instructions 是否還包含 mechanical cooling 與 high openings？

M06_Q17.stem: For Commercial Kitchen Spaces, the maximum operative temperature cannot exceed what degrees F° (C° ) per Part 1: Provide Acceptable Thermal Comfort of Feature T01: Thermal Performance?
M06_Q17.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 1 Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment 在 Commercial Kitchen Spaces 段落明列 operative temperature in the kitchen does not exceed 80 °F (27 °C)。這是 commercial kitchen 的專屬上限，與一般 regularly occupied spaces 的 PMV 或 adaptive model 條件分開處理。

B.gap: 把較舒適室溫範圍誤作 commercial kitchen 上限。
B.followup: T01 對 commercial kitchen 是採一般辦公室舒適溫度，還是明列 80 °F 上限？

C.gap: 誤用一般室內低端溫度而非廚房最高操作溫度。
C.followup: Part 1 問 maximum operative temperature，70 °F 是上限還是太低的舒適值？

A.gap: 把偏冷溫度當成高熱廚房的允許上限。
A.followup: Commercial kitchen 的溫度限制是否會低到 65 °F，還是標準給較高 ceiling？

M06_Q18.stem: In Part 2: Achieve Thermal Comfort of Feature T02: Enhanced Thermal Performance, a post-occupancy survey is administered twice a year. What specific months must the surveys be administered?

options.text: June, July or August and March, April, or May
March, April or May and December, January or February
June, July or August and December, January or February

M06_Q18.explanation: Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort 要求 post-occupancy survey 至少 twice a year，包含一次 June, July or August，以及一次 December, January or February，且需 at least six months after occupancy。題幹使用的 feature/part 名稱與目前 WELL v2 文字不完全一致，但月份要求相同。

B.gap: 把春季調查誤當成 T02 指定暖季窗口。
B.followup: T02 survey 要求 warm-season window 是 March-May，還是 June-August？

A.gap: 保留夏季但漏掉冬季指定月份。
A.followup: T02 是否要求一次 December、January or February，以捕捉 cold-season satisfaction？

C.gap: 使用任意月份，未符合 T02 夏冬季窗口。
C.followup: May 與 November 是否出現在 T02 Part 1 指定的兩個 survey month groups 中？

M06_Q19.stem: With Feature T01: Thermal Performance projects, when conducting ongoing monitoring, the number and location of sampling points must comply with the requirements outlined where?
M06_Q19.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 2 Monitor Thermal Parameters, Option 1 Annual testing 要求 dry-bulb temperature、relative humidity、air speed（如使用 elevated air speed）與 mean radiant temperature 每年至少兩次監測。其 number and location of sampling points 必須 comply with requirements outlined in the Performance Verification Guidebook。

B.gap: 誤以為有 Thermal Comfort Index 作為採樣規範來源。
B.followup: T01 Part 2 提到 sampling points 時，引用的是 index 還是 WELL Performance Verification Guidebook？

D.gap: 把一般 performance guideline 名稱誤當正式文件。
D.followup: 標準文字中的 sampling points 文件名稱是否包含 Verification，而非 Guidelines Index？

C.gap: 把術語表誤認為測點數量與位置的規範。
C.followup: Thermal parameters glossary 能定義名詞，但 T01 採樣點要求實際在哪份 guidebook？

M06_Q20.stem: For Feature T01: Thermal Performance compliance, if a project's measurements are undertaken by a WELL Performance Testing Agent in compliance with the Performance Verification Guidebook, where are the results submitted to for recertification purposes?
M06_Q20.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 2 Monitor Thermal Parameters 的 note 說，若 measurements 由 WELL Performance Testing Agent 依 Performance Verification Guidebook 執行，each year and test location 的 results submitted to GBCI 可 averaged and utilized for recertification purposes。這裡指定的是認證審查結果提交對象。

C.gap: 混淆 LEED 常見機構與 WELL recertification 提交對象。
C.followup: T01 Part 2 note 中，results submitted to 哪個機構才可用於 recertification averaging？

A.gap: 把 WELL 擁有方誤認為測試結果提交機構。
A.followup: IWBI 與 GBCI 在 WELL 流程中角色不同，T01 note 明確寫哪一個？

D.gap: 把建築師協會誤當 WELL performance results 接收方。
D.followup: AIA 是否出現在 T01 recertification measurement note 的提交流程中？

M06_Q21.stem: When seeking Feature T03: Thermal Zoning compliance, the maximum size of each thermal zone is:

options.text: 850 ft² or 20 occupants, whichever is larger
650 ft² or 5 occupants, whichever is larger
650 ft² or 10 occupants, whichever is larger
750 ft² or 10 occupants, whichever is larger

M06_Q21.explanation: Feature T03 Thermal Zoning - Part 1 Provide Thermostat Control 要求至少 90% of regularly occupied spaces 具備 temperature control，且 maximum size of each thermal zone 是 650 ft² (60 m²) or 10 occupants, whichever is larger。更細的 one per 320 ft² or 5 occupants 是較高點數路徑，不是基本最大 zone size。

D.gap: 記對十人條件但把面積上限放寬到 750 ft²。
D.followup: T03 Part 1 的基本 zone size 面積是 650 ft²，還是更大的 750 ft²？

A.gap: 同時放寬面積與 occupants，低估 zoning 細緻度。
A.followup: T03 是否允許每 zone 到 850 ft² 或 20 occupants，還是要求更小？

B.gap: 混淆基本門檻與較高點數的五人細分要求。
B.followup: T03 表格中 one per 5 occupants 對應哪個較高點數路徑，而非 maximum size？

M06_Q22.stem: Feature T05: Radiant Thermal Comfort requires projects:

options.text: To use independently controlled ventilation systems.
To use radiant systems and independently controlled ventilation systems.
To use radiant systems and privately controlled ventilation systems.
To use radiant systems and privately controlled ventilation systems.

M06_Q22.explanation: Feature T05 Radiant Thermal Comfort 的 Summary 明確要求 projects use radiant systems and independently controlled ventilation systems。Part 1 Implement Radiant Heating 與 Part 2 Implement Radiant Cooling 進一步用至少 50% of regularly occupied project area 的 radiant coverage 來落實該策略。

A.gap: 只抓到 ventilation control，漏掉 radiant systems 本體。
A.followup: T05 Summary 是否只要求 independently controlled ventilation，還必須搭配 radiant systems？

D.gap: 把 independently controlled 誤讀成 privately controlled。
D.followup: T05 的 ventilation system 是強調 independent from heating/cooling，還是 private control？

C.gap: 重複 private ventilation 誤解，未使用標準正確措辭。
C.followup: 標準 Summary 中的關鍵詞是 independently controlled ventilation systems，還是 privately controlled？

M06_Q23.stem: Which WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to check thermal comfort parameters in their buildings that can be used as feedback for building managers and users to take appropriate actions.
M06_Q23.explanation: Feature T06 Thermal Comfort Monitoring - Part 1 Monitor Thermal Environment 要求 projects monitor dry-bulb temperature and relative humidity，至少 one sensor per 3,500 ft² of occupiable floor area，且 measurements 至少 every 15 minutes。其 Summary 說 sensors 的資料可作為 building managers and users 採取 appropriate actions 的 feedback。

D.gap: 混淆 T01 performance compliance 與 T06 monitoring feedback。
D.followup: T01 可要求監測，但哪個 feature 的 Summary 專門談 sensors 作為 managers/users feedback？

C.gap: 把 occupant survey 驗證誤認為即時參數監測。
C.followup: T02 是 survey satisfaction，還是用 sensors 持續 check thermal comfort parameters？

A.gap: 把 radiant system strategy 誤當 thermal monitoring feature。
A.followup: T05 重點是 radiant heating/cooling，哪個 feature 才要求 monitor dry-bulb temperature and RH？

M06_Q24.stem: This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to design outdoor spaces to avoid excessive wind and manage elevated temperatures through shading or other strategies.
M06_Q24.explanation: Feature T09β Outdoor Thermal Comfort 的 Summary 說 projects design outdoor spaces to avoid excessive wind and manage elevated temperatures through shading or other strategies。具體要求分在 Part 1 Manage Outdoor Heat 的 shading/modeling，以及 Part 2 Avoid Excessive Wind 的 wind-speed thresholds。

A.gap: 把室內 thermal zones 控制誤認為戶外熱舒適。
A.followup: T03 Part 1 是否處理 outdoor shading and wind，還是處理 indoor thermal zone control？

D.gap: 把 occupant survey 滿意度驗證誤當戶外設計策略。
D.followup: T02 Verified Thermal Comfort 主要透過 survey，是否包含 outdoor heat and wind modeling？

B.gap: 把室內 thermal performance baseline 誤認為 outdoor feature。
