B.followup: T01 Part 1 聚焦 indoor acceptable thermal environment，哪個 feature 名稱直接指 outdoor thermal comfort？

M06_Q25.stem: In Part 1: Manage Relative Humidity of Feature T07: Humidity Control , the mechanical system must have the capability of maintaining relative humidity in what range?
M06_Q25.explanation: Feature T07 Humidity Control - Part 1 Manage Relative Humidity, Option 1 Mechanical humidity control 要求 all regularly occupied areas except high-humidity areas 的 mechanical system 能 at all times 維持 relative humidity between 30% and 60%。Option 2 modeling 與 Option 3 long-term data 也使用 30% to 60% 作為 98% business hours 或六個月資料的判準。

B.gap: 把過低濕度範圍誤認為健康舒適區間。
B.followup: T07 為避免乾燥刺激與病原問題，是否允許 relative humidity 低到 0%？

A.gap: 只抓到下限附近，漏掉 T07 需要到 60% 上限。
A.followup: T07 Part 1 的 lower bound 是 30%，但 upper bound 是否也停在 30%？

D.gap: 把濕度範圍整體上移，超過 T07 60% 上限。
D.followup: T07 為限制 mold/pathogen growth，relative humidity 上限是 60% 還是 70%？

M06_Q26.stem: When determining occupant satisfaction, this WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects provide a high level of thermal comfort?
M06_Q26.explanation: Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort 要在 occupancy 至少六個月後，每年兩次進行 post-occupancy survey，並達到 80% 或 90% regular occupants 的 thermal comfort satisfaction threshold。這正是用 occupant satisfaction 驗證高水準 thermal comfort 的 feature。

C.gap: 混淆基準熱環境性能與用戶滿意度調查驗證。
C.followup: T01 Part 1 主要驗證 PMV 或 temperature range；哪個 feature 才要求 post-occupancy thermal comfort survey？

B.gap: 把 thermal zone 控制誤認為 occupant satisfaction 評估。
B.followup: T03 Part 1 是規定 zone size 與 thermostat control，是否有要求用 survey 判定滿意度門檻？

A.gap: 把感測器監測誤當成熱舒適滿意度調查機制。
A.followup: T06 Part 1 監測 dry-bulb temperature 與 relative humidity；它是否直接計算 occupants satisfaction？

M06_Q27.stem: This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to create indoor thermal environments that provide comfortable thermal conditions to the majority of people in support of their health, well-being and productivity.
M06_Q27.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 1 Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment 要 mechanically conditioned spaces 達到 PMV +/-0.5 至少 90% occupied hours 與 90% spaces，或 PMV +/-1.0 至少 98% hours 與 95% spaces。這個 precondition 的 intent 是提供 majority of building users 可接受的 thermal environment。

B.gap: 把提供多個 thermal zones 誤當成整體基準熱性能。
B.followup: T03 Part 1 強調 one zone per 650 ft2 或 320 ft2；哪個 precondition 才定義 majority comfort 的 PMV 基準？

A.gap: 把個人設備與 dress code 誤認為基礎熱環境要求。
A.followup: T04 Part 1-3 是個人 cooling、heating 與 dress code；它是否取代 T01 的 PMV/temperature performance？

C.gap: 把監測參數理解成創造舒適熱環境的前置要求。
C.followup: T06 Part 1 要 sensors 每 15 分鐘量測；哪個 feature 才要求 thermal conditions 達標？

M06_Q28.stem: In Part 1: Manage Outdoor Heat of Feature T09 β: Outdoor Thermal Comfort at least ___ of pedestrian pathways and building entrances must be shaded for more than half of the daylight hours each day by tree canopies, awnings, or other structures.
M06_Q28.explanation: Feature T09 β Outdoor Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Manage Outdoor Heat, Option 1 Outdoor shading 要 pedestrian pathways and building entrances 至少 50% 在每天超過一半 daylight hours 被 tree canopies、awnings 或 other structures 遮蔽。25% 是 parking spaces 的遮蔽門檻，不是 pathways and entrances。

B.gap: 把 parking spaces 的 25% 門檻套到 pedestrian pathways。
B.followup: T09 Part 1 Option 1 同時列 pathways、parking、plazas；哪一項才使用 at least 25%？

D.gap: 混淆 outdoor congregation areas 的 25%-75% 範圍與步道入口。
D.followup: T09 Part 1 對 plazas 和 seating areas 是範圍要求；pedestrian pathways 是否也是 75%？

A.gap: 把遮蔽比例提高到 WELL 未列出的更嚴格數字。
A.followup: 回到 T09 Part 1 Outdoor shading，原文對 pathways and building entrances 寫的是至少多少百分比？

M06_Q29.stem: For Feature T08 β: Enhanced Operable Windows compliance, projects must design windows with:

options.text: Controls for window operation are positioned less than 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.
Controls for window operation are positioned 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.
Controls for window operation are positioned higher than 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.
Controls for window operation are positioned not more than 1.7 m (5.6 ft) above the finished floor.

M06_Q29.explanation: Feature T08 β Enhanced Operable Windows - Part 1 Provide Windows with Multiple Opening Modes, Option 1 Window design 要 window operation controls positioned not more than 5.6 ft (1.7 m) above finished floor。關鍵是上限包含等於 1.7 m，而不是只能低於或高於。

C.gap: 把可及高度上限反向理解成必須高於 1.7 m。
C.followup: T08 Part 1 Option 1 的 controls 是為了 occupant 操作；要求是 above 還是 not more than 5.6 ft？

B.gap: 誤以為控制器必須剛好位於 1.7 m 單一高度。
B.followup: 如果 controls 位於 1.5 m，是否符合 T08 Part 1 的 not more than 1.7 m 要求？

A.gap: 抓到高度方向但漏掉標準允許等於 1.7 m。
A.followup: T08 Part 1 的文字是 less than 還是 not more than？等於 5.6 ft 時是否被排除？

M06_Q30.stem: For a project to achieve Feature 01: Thermal Performance, there are two options to choose from: Annual testing and Continuous Monitoring. If the project chooses Annual testing, what verification method is required to be submitted to IWBI?
M06_Q30.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 2 Monitor Thermal Parameters, Option 1 Annual testing 要 dry-bulb temperature、relative humidity、air speed（如適用）與 mean radiant temperature 每年至少兩次監測，並每年透過 WELL digital platform 提交結果。此 annual submission 對應 Ongoing Report 類型。

A.gap: 把一次性技術文件誤認為年度監測結果提交方式。
A.followup: T01 Part 2 Annual testing 要把量測結果 annually submitted；這比較像 Technical Document 還是 Ongoing Report？

B.gap: 把現場照片當成 thermal parameter annual testing 的證明。
B.followup: T01 Part 2 要提交 temperature、humidity、air speed、mean radiant temperature results；照片能呈現這些年度數據嗎？

D.gap: 混淆設計圖說與營運期間熱參數監測報告。
D.followup: Annual testing 發生在營運監測階段；architectural drawings 是否能證明每年兩次量測？

M06_Q31.stem: A project team is anxious to complete the project and earn their WELL Certification. For Feature T02: Verified Thermal Comfort, what time period must the project team wait to conduct the survey of tenants?

options.text: At end of first year of occupancy.

M06_Q31.explanation: Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort 要 post-occupancy survey administered at least twice a year，且必須 at least six months after occupancy。標準還指定其中一次在 June/July/August，另一次在 December/January/February。

A.gap: 忽略 post-occupancy survey 必須等待入住後資料成熟。
A.followup: T02 Part 1 為何要求 at least six months after occupancy，而不是 project completion 當下？

D.gap: 低估 T02 occupant survey 的最低入住後等待時間。
D.followup: 如果三個月已入住，是否已達 T02 Part 1 明文的 at least six months after occupancy？

B.gap: 把每年兩次調查誤解為必須等完整一年才開始。
B.followup: T02 Part 1 要 at least twice a year；第一次符合條件的最早時間是六個月還是一年？

M06_Q32.stem: The intent of Feature T04: Individual Thermal Control is to maximize and personalize thermal comfort among all individuals. Personal Cooling Options for all regular occupants may include:

options.text: A cooling mat for their feet.

M06_Q32.explanation: Feature T04 Individual Thermal Control - Part 1 Provide Personal Cooling Options 明列 chair with mechanical cooling system 作為 regular occupants 可用的個人 cooling option。其他替代方案也必須能在 15 分鐘內造成 PMV change of -0.5，且不改變其他 occupants 的 PMV。

C.gap: 把非標準列舉的臨時降溫用品當成合規設備。
C.followup: T04 Part 1 列出 desk fan、ceiling fan 與 mechanical cooling chair；hand-held spritzer 是否符合 PMV -0.5 證明？

A.gap: 把 heating footwarmers 概念誤延伸為 cooling mat。
A.followup: T04 Part 2 有 footwarmers 作為 heating option；T04 Part 1 的 cooling list 是否包含 cooling mat？

D.gap: 將未列出且難以影響 PMV 的鍵盤設備當作冷卻方案。
D.followup: T04 Part 1 要設備能改變個人 PMV；cooling keyboard 如何在 15 分鐘內達到 -0.5？

M06_Q33.stem: A law firm in Cincinnati would like to allow for thermal zones in their open office space. How can this company earn the most points for Feature T03: Thermal Zoning?

options.text: By providing one zone per 10 occupants.
By providing one zone per 200 ft2.
By providing one zone per 5 occupants.
By providing one zone per 2 occupants.

M06_Q33.explanation: Feature T03 Thermal Zoning - Part 1 Provide Thermostat Control awards the higher point tier when projects provide one zone per 320 ft2 (30 m2) or one zone per 5 occupants. One zone per 10 occupants is only the lower tier paired with 650 ft2 (60 m2).

A.gap: 選到 T03 較低分區密度，未達最高點數條件。
A.followup: T03 Part 1 的 points table 中，one per 10 occupants 對應幾點，one per 5 occupants 又對應幾點？

B.gap: 使用了 WELL 未列出的 200 ft2 zone 面積門檻。
B.followup: T03 Part 1 表格列出的面積門檻是 650 ft2 與 320 ft2；200 ft2 是否出現在標準中？

D.gap: 把分區密度加嚴到題目與標準未要求的程度。
D.followup: 若目標是 earn the most points，T03 Part 1 明列的最高 occupant ratio 是多少人一區？

M06_Q34.stem: Part 1: Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment of Feature T01: Thermal Performance has a verification method requirement for submitting materials to IWBI. What is the verification method for this option?

options.text: Letter of Assurance - Engineer, Performance Test, Technical Document

M06_Q34.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 1 Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment is verified through engineered thermal comfort documentation and performance testing because it requires PMV/temperature conditions for regularly occupied spaces. The standard's Part 1 thresholds include PMV +/-0.5 for 90% of occupied hours and spaces or PMV +/-1.0 for 98% of hours and 95% of spaces.

A.gap: 把熱舒適工程性能驗證誤交給 architect 單獨保證。
A.followup: T01 Part 1 涉及 PMV、operative temperature 與 testing；哪類專業與 verification method 更能證明這些數據？

B.gap: 把性能門檻驗證誤認為政策或營運排程文件。
B.followup: T01 Part 1 要證明 thermal conditions 達 PMV/temperature threshold；policy schedule 能否直接證明？

D.gap: 混淆 T01 Part 1 性能驗證與 Part 2 年度監測報告。
D.followup: On-going Report 對應 annual monitoring；T01 Part 1 的 acceptable thermal environment 需要哪些測試或技術文件？

M06_Q35.stem: Humidity plays a big role in indoor air quality and keeping occupants healthy inside of buildings. In order to manage long-term humidity data for Feature T07: Humidity Control, what relative humidity is required for at least the pervious six months?

options.text: Between 25% and 55% for at least 93% of all business hours of the year .
Between 40% and 60% for at least 98% of all business hours of the year .
Between 30% and 60% for at least 92% of all business hours of the year .
Between 30% and 60% for at least 98% of all business hours of the year .

M06_Q35.explanation: Feature T07 Humidity Control - Part 1 Manage Relative Humidity, Option 3 Long-term humidity data requires all regularly occupied areas except high-humidity spaces to have at least previous six months of humidity data between 30% and 60% for at least 98% of all business hours. It also requires meeting Feature T06 Thermal Comfort Monitoring.

B.gap: 把 relative humidity 下限錯記為 40%，縮窄標準允許範圍。
B.followup: T07 Part 1 Option 3 的 long-term humidity data 下限是 30% 還是 40%？

A.gap: 同時混淆濕度範圍與 business hours 達標比例。
A.followup: T07 Part 1 是否允許 25%-55% 且 93%，還是明確要求 30%-60% 與 98%？

C.gap: 記對 30%-60% 範圍但低估 98% business hours 要求。
C.followup: 若只有 92% business hours 在 30%-60%，是否達到 T07 Part 1 Option 3 的門檻？

M06_Q36.stem: As part of Feature T04: Individual Thermal Control, occupants can warm their individual environments. What is an example of an approved personal heating option?
M06_Q36.explanation: Feature T04 Individual Thermal Control - Part 2 Provide Personal Heating Options 明列 electric parabolic space heater 作為合規 warming option。標準也列出 electric heated chair or footwarmers、weekly washed blankets，或能在 15 分鐘內造成 PMV +0.5 且不影響他人的方案。

D.gap: 把一般 heating pads 誤認為標準明列的 heating option。
D.followup: T04 Part 2 明列的是 electric heated chair or footwarmers；heating pads 是否有相同列名或 PMV 證明？

C.gap: 漏掉標準限定為 electric parabolic space heater。
C.followup: T04 Part 2 的 heater 類型是否只是 generic electric space heater，還是指定 parabolic？

A.gap: 把 blankets 誤改成 electric blankets，偏離每週清洗要求。
A.followup: T04 Part 2 對 blankets 的要求是電熱功能，還是 washed at least weekly？

M06_Q37.stem: For performance verified environmental conditions in Feature T01: Thermal Performance, what is one of the PMV ranges of the thermal comfort conditions for mechanically conditioned regularly occupied spaces?
M06_Q37.explanation: Feature T01 Thermal Performance - Part 1 Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment, Option 1 lists mechanically conditioned regularly occupied spaces as meeting either PMV +/-0.5 or PMV +/-1.0. The PMV +/-1.0 pathway requires at least 98% occupied hours, at least 95% regularly occupied spaces, and at least two points in T03/T04.

C.gap: 把 PMV range 記成標準未列出的過嚴數值。
C.followup: T01 Part 1 的 mechanically conditioned PMV table 實際列出哪兩個 ranges？

A.gap: 接近 +/-0.5 但不是 WELL 表格中的正式門檻。
A.followup: 在 T01 Part 1 表格裡，較嚴格的 PMV range 是 +/-0.4 還是 +/-0.5？

B.gap: 把可接受 PMV 範圍放寬到 WELL 未允許的程度。
B.followup: T01 Part 1 是否允許 mechanically conditioned spaces 使用 PMV +/-1.5 作為 compliance？

M06_Q38.stem: How many preconditions are there for the Thermal Comfort Concept?
M06_Q38.explanation: The Thermal Comfort concept overview lists 1 precondition and 6 optimizations. The sole precondition is Feature T01 Thermal Performance, while T02 through T07 are optimizations in the module list.

C.gap: 誤把某個 optimization 也算成 Thermal Comfort precondition。
C.followup: 概念總表中 T01 標示為 PRECONDITION；T02-T07 各自標示為什麼類型？

A.gap: 將多數 Thermal Comfort features 誤歸類為 mandatory preconditions。
A.followup: WELL 總表清楚列 1 precondition、6 optimizations；你是否把 optimizations 也算進去了？

B.gap: 混淆 feature 數量與 precondition 數量。
B.followup: Thermal Comfort 有多個 features，但哪些 feature codes 旁邊實際寫 PRECONDITION？

M06_Q39.stem: What other features must Feature T09: Outdoor Thermal Comfort part 3 Support Outdoor Nature Access achieve to satisfy the feature requirement?

options.text: Part 1: Manage Outdoor Heat or Part 2: Avoid Excessive Wind of Feature T09: β Outdoor Thermal Comfort and Part 2: Provide Nature Access Outdoors of Feature M09: Enhanced Access to Nature.
Part 1: Provide Acceptable Thermal Environment and Part 2: Monitor Thermal Parameters of Feature T01: Thermal Performance.
Feature T07: Humidity Control and Feature T09: Enhanced Operable Windows

M06_Q39.explanation: Feature T09 β Outdoor Thermal Comfort - Part 3 Support Outdoor Nature Access requires the project to achieve Feature T09 β Part 1 or Part 2, and Feature M09 Part 2 Provide Nature Access Outdoors. It is a linked requirement between outdoor heat/wind mitigation and outdoor nature access.

D.gap: 混淆室內 humidity/window features 與 T09 outdoor nature access 條件。
D.followup: T09 Part 3 原文要求 T09 Part 1 or Part 2；它是否提到 T07 或 Enhanced Operable Windows？

B.gap: 把 T01 室內 thermal performance 誤當成 T09 戶外門檻。
B.followup: T09 Part 3 的 prerequisite-like link 是回到 T09 outdoor parts，還是 T01 indoor PMV monitoring？

C.gap: 只抓到 nature 概念，卻選錯 M09 Part 2 的指定 feature。
C.followup: T09 Part 3 指名的是 M09 Enhanced Access to Nature 的哪一個 Part，而不是 M02？

M06_Q40.stem: According to the overview of the Thermal Comfort concept in the WELL Building StandardTM, what percentage of office workers have expressed dissatisfaction with their thermal environment?
M06_Q40.explanation: The WELL Thermal Comfort concept overview states that as many as 41% of office workers have expressed dissatisfaction with their thermal environment. The same issue is repeated in Feature T02 Verified Thermal Comfort, which uses Part 1 Survey for Thermal Comfort to quantify occupant satisfaction at 80% or 90% thresholds.

C.gap: 低估 overview 中 office workers dissatisfaction 的引用比例。
C.followup: Thermal Comfort overview 在提到 only 11% buildings meet satisfaction goals 後，office workers dissatisfied 是多少？

D.gap: 把不滿意比例記成標準未引用的較高數值。
D.followup: T02 Issue 和概念 overview 都重複同一百分比；原文是否寫到 52%？

B.gap: 將 80% acceptable environment 概念誤轉成不滿意比例。
B.followup: WELL 提到 minimum 80% occupants acceptable；這和 expressed dissatisfaction 的 41% 是同一數字嗎？

M06_Q41.stem: Thermal comfort greatly influences our experiences in the places we live and work and is one of the highest contributing factors including__________?
M06_Q41.explanation: The Thermal Comfort concept overview states that thermal comfort is one of the highest contributing factors influencing overall human satisfaction in buildings. Feature T01 Thermal Performance operationalizes this baseline by requiring acceptable thermal environments for the majority of building users.

C.gap: 抓到 satisfaction 但漏掉原文限定的 buildings 情境。
C.followup: 概念 overview 的句子是 overall human satisfaction，還是 overall human satisfaction in buildings？

B.gap: 把 broad well-being outcome 當成該句的精確引用。
B.followup: Thermal Comfort overview 這句在談 contributing factors；原文接的是 satisfaction 還是 wellbeing？

A.gap: 將標準中的 human satisfaction 誤改成 ratings happiness。
A.followup: WELL Thermal Comfort overview 有提 building ratings 嗎，還是談 occupants 對 buildings 的 satisfaction？

M06_Q42.stem: This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to increase thermal control of the space, by allowing control of either the conditions of a thermal zone or movement between thermal zones.
M06_Q42.explanation: Feature T03 Thermal Zoning - Part 1 Provide Thermostat Control increases thermal control by requiring temperature control for at least 90% of regularly occupied spaces through thermostats or a digital interface. It also limits each thermal zone to 650 ft2 or 10 occupants for the lower tier, and 320 ft2 or 5 occupants for the higher tier.

D.gap: 把 occupant satisfaction survey 誤認為 spatial thermal control。
D.followup: T02 Part 1 是 survey threshold；哪個 feature 的 Part 1 才規定 thermostats 和 zone size？

A.gap: 混淆個人設備控制與 thermal zone 層級控制。
A.followup: T04 是 personal cooling/heating devices；題幹提到 movement between thermal zones 時更符合哪個 feature？

C.gap: 把 radiant system 技術誤當成 occupants 可控制分區。
C.followup: T05 Part 1-2 要 radiant heating/cooling 50% regularly occupied area；它是否談 zone movement？

M06_Q43.stem: This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to improve thermal comfort of people in the space through the provision of personal thermal comfort devices and flexible dress codes that support individual thermal preferences.
M06_Q43.explanation: Feature T04 Individual Thermal Control - Parts 1-3 cover personal cooling options, personal heating options, and flexible dress code. The feature summary explicitly links personal thermal comfort devices and flexible dress codes to supporting individual thermal preferences.

C.gap: 把 baseline thermal performance 與個人化控制措施混在一起。
C.followup: T01 Part 1 是 PMV/temperature compliance；哪個 feature 才列 cooling devices、heating devices 和 dress code？

B.gap: 把滿意度驗證誤認為提供個人熱舒適設備。
B.followup: T02 Part 1 要 survey；題幹中的 flexible dress codes 出現在哪個 feature 的 Part 3？

D.gap: 把 zone-level thermostat control 誤認為 individual devices。
D.followup: T03 控制 thermal zone；若題幹說 personal devices and dress codes，應找哪個 feature？

M06_Q44.stem: This WELL Thermal Comfort feature requires projects to maintain optimum relative humidity levels that are conducive to human health and well-being.
M06_Q44.explanation: Feature T07 Humidity Control - Part 1 Manage Relative Humidity requires relative humidity to be maintained between 30% and 60%, either through mechanical humidity control, modeling for at least 98% of business hours, or long-term humidity data. Its summary states that these optimum RH levels support human health and well-being.

D.gap: 混淆 humidity monitoring 與 humidity control 的達標要求。
D.followup: T06 Part 1 監測 dry-bulb temperature and RH；哪個 feature 規定 30%-60% RH control？

C.gap: 把 radiant heating/cooling 系統誤認為相對濕度管理。
C.followup: T05 Part 1-2 要 50% area radiant heating/cooling；是否有 30%-60% humidity threshold？

A.gap: 把個人 thermal devices 誤當成建築濕度控制策略。
A.followup: T04 是 personal cooling/heating；若題目問 relative humidity levels，應回到哪個 feature？

M06_Q45.stem: FeatureT08 β: Enhanced Operable Windows requires windows that can be opened at different elevations to:

options.text: Provide limited air flow at different outdoor temperatures
Restrict air flow at different outdoor temperaturs
Maintain air flow at different outdoor temperatures
Provide desired air flow at different outdoor temperatures

M06_Q45.explanation: Feature T08 β Enhanced Operable Windows summary says windows opened at different elevations provide desired air flow at different outdoor temperatures. Part 1 requires at least 70% of operable windows to support lower openings and, where heating is present, at least 30% to support high openings.

B.gap: 把多高度開窗的目的反向理解為限制 air flow。
B.followup: T08 summary 說 multiple elevations 是為了 restrict air flow，還是 provide desired air flow？

C.gap: 接近 air flow 概念但漏掉 desired airflow 的調節意圖。
C.followup: T08 Part 1 為何區分 low openings 與 high openings？是維持固定 airflow 還是依 outdoor temperature 調整？

A.gap: 誤以為標準目標是限制通風量而非提供合適氣流。
A.followup: T08 的 low openings 可提高 air exchange；這與 limited air flow 的說法是否一致？

M06_Q46.stem: According to Feature T09 β: Outdoor Thermal Comfort, heat island effects can greatly impact outdoor temperatures and make outdoor spaces too warm for building occupants to enjoy. Adverse health effects include heat-related illness, fatigue and increased heart rate. What is an example of a mitigation strategy to improve outdoor thermal comfort in a heat island?

options.text: Adding more structures, like metal sculptures.
Using sand to cover areas of concrete.

M06_Q46.explanation: Feature T09 β Outdoor Thermal Comfort - Part 1 Manage Outdoor Heat identifies landscaping and greenery, manufactured shading systems, reflective surfaces, and water features as countermeasure categories. The feature solutions also state that trees or vegetation provide evapotranspiration and shade, lowering surface and air temperatures.

A.gap: 把 native plant selection 誤當成 heat island mitigation 要點。
A.followup: T09 Part 1 的 countermeasures category 是 native-only planting，還是 broader landscaping and greenery？

B.gap: 忽略額外硬質結構可能增加蓄熱而非降溫。
B.followup: T09 issue 說 heat island 來自取代自然地貌的蓄熱材料；metal sculptures 能提供 shade 或 evapotranspiration 嗎？

D.gap: 抓到覆蓋硬鋪面概念但選了未列明的沙覆蓋策略。
D.followup: T09 Part 1 Option 2 提到 reflectance of manufactured surfaces；sand cover 是否等同高 albedo paving strategy？

M06_Q47.stem: According to Feature T06: Thermal Comfort Monitoring, what can cause the buildings occupants to experience itchy eyes, headache and throat irritation?

options.text: Cold temperatures, dry air, poor ventilation.
High levels of heat, humidity, poor ventilation.
High levels of outdoor pollen, poor ventilations, unfavorable outdoor weather.

M06_Q47.explanation: Feature T06 Thermal Comfort Monitoring issue states that unfavorable levels of heat, humidity and ventilation are associated with itchy eyes, headache and throat irritation. Part 1 then requires monitoring dry-bulb temperature and relative humidity with sensors at least once every 15 minutes.

A.gap: 混入 T07 低濕乾冷不適，偏離 T06 原文描述。
A.followup: T06 Issue 寫的是 unfavorable levels of heat, humidity and ventilation；它是否特別列 cold temperatures and dry air？

C.gap: 把 outdoor pollen 與 weather 因素誤加入 T06 症狀成因。
C.followup: T06 Part 1 監測的是 indoor dry-bulb temperature 和 RH；outdoor pollen 是否是此句的核心因素？

D.gap: 未辨識 all of the above 包含標準未列出的原因。
D.followup: 若某選項加入 outdoor pollen 或 cold/dry wording，是否仍能說全部都符合 T06 Issue 原文？

M06_Q48.stem: Outdoor weather, indoor occupancy and building physics and performance all have a direct impact on human perceptions of what WELL Concept?
M06_Q48.explanation: Feature T06 Thermal Comfort Monitoring issue states that outdoor weather, indoor occupancy, and building physics and performance, including ventilation rates, directly affect human perceptions of thermal comfort. Part 1 responds by requiring continuous monitoring of thermal comfort parameters such as dry-bulb temperature and relative humidity.

B.gap: 被 ventilation rates 誘導到 Air concept，而非 thermal comfort perception。
B.followup: T06 Issue 雖提 ventilation rates，但最後說它們影響的是 human perceptions of 哪個 concept？

A.gap: 把環境條件影響誤連到 Light，而非溫熱感受。
A.followup: Outdoor weather 與 indoor occupancy 會直接改變 lighting perception，還是 thermal comfort perception？

C.gap: 將 building performance 誤聯想到 Sound 而非熱舒適。
C.followup: T06 監測 dry-bulb temperature 和 relative humidity；這些參數屬於 Sound 還是 Thermal Comfort？

M06_Q49.stem: A new office building in San Diego is being designed for optimal Thermal Comfort for it's employees. To give employee's a little more control of their environment the building is adding operable windows. According to Part 1: Provide Windows with Multiple Opening modes of Feature T08 β: Enhanced Operable Windows, what percent of operable windows may be opened so that at least half of the opening is not more than 5.9 ft above the finished floor and opening is at least 1 ft in the smallest dimension?
M06_Q49.explanation: Feature T08 β Enhanced Operable Windows - Part 1 Provide Windows with Multiple Opening Modes, Option 1 Window design requires at least 70% of operable windows to open so at least half of the opening is not more than 5.9 ft (1.8 m) above finished floor and the opening is at least 1 ft (0.3 m) in the smallest dimension. At least one such window must be present in each room with operable windows.

B.gap: 嚴重低估 T08 low-opening operable windows 的比例要求。
B.followup: T08 Part 1 Option 1 對 lower openings 寫 at least 70%；20% 是否出現在這個要求中？

A.gap: 把常見半數門檻誤套到 T08 的 70% 開窗要求。
A.followup: 題幹中的 at least half of the opening 是描述開口高度，不是 operable windows 的百分比；實際百分比是多少？

D.gap: 把要求加嚴到 WELL 未列出的 90% 比例。
D.followup: T08 Part 1 low openings 明列 at least 70%；標準是否要求 90% operable windows？

M06_Q50.stem: According to Part 1: Provide Thermostat Control of Feature T03: Thermal Zoning, for optimal performance, temperature sensors should be placed at least how many feet away from exterior walls, windows and doors, direct sunlight, air supply diffusers, mechanical fans, and heaters?
M06_Q50.explanation: Feature T03 Thermal Zoning - Part 1 Provide Thermostat Control requires temperature sensors to be positioned at least 3.3 ft (1 m) away from exterior walls, windows and doors, direct sunlight, air supply diffusers, mechanical fans, heaters, or other significant sources of heat or cold. This placement supports accurate thermal zone control.

A.gap: 低估 sensor 與熱冷干擾源之間的最小距離。
A.followup: T03 Part 1 明文同時給出 3.3 ft 與 1 m；1.5 ft 是否達到這個距離？

B.gap: 把距離要求加嚴到標準未列的 4.2 ft。
B.followup: T03 Part 1 sensor placement 的 imperial value 是 3.3 ft，還是 4.2 ft？

C.gap: 選到接近但仍低於 1 m 的錯誤距離。
C.followup: 若 sensor 只離 direct sunlight 或 diffuser 2.8 ft，是否滿足 at least 3.3 ft 的要求？

M07_Q01.stem: In Part 1: Label Acoustic Zones of Feature S01: Sound Mapping, a new office project labels what acoustic zones as part of its sound mapping plan?

options.text: Noisy, Quiet, Mixed and Circulation zones
Loud, Quiet, Mixed and Circulation zones
Loud, Quiet, Equipment and Circulation zones
Noisy, Mixed, Equipment and Quiet zones

M07_Q01.explanation: S01 Sound Mapping – Part 1 Label Acoustic Zones 要求在樓層圖或示意文件標示 Loud zones、Quiet zones、Mixed zones 與 Circulation zones；若 Loud zones 直接鄰接 Quiet zones，還要提出重新配置或降低 sound transmission 的計畫。

D.gap: 把 Loud zones 誤換成 Noisy zones，且多放了 Equipment 類別。
D.followup: 回到 S01 Part 1 的四種 acoustic zone labels，哪些是正式 zone，哪些只是 Loud zones 的例子？

A.gap: 知道 Quiet、Mixed、Circulation，卻漏掉正式的 Loud zones 名稱。
A.followup: S01 Part 1 如何描述 loud equipment 或 activities，它使用的是 Noisy 還是 Loud 這個標籤？

C.gap: 混淆 Loud zones 範圍，把 Equipment 當成獨立 acoustic zone。
C.followup: 若 mechanical rooms 屬於 loud equipment，S01 Part 1 是把它列為哪一類 zone 的例子？

M07_Q02.stem: A cafeteria, a Category 3 space, is part of the build-out. What is the maximum sound pressure level (SPL) in dBAs for this space if you want to achieve the maximum number of points according to Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature SO2: Maximum Noise Levels.
M07_Q02.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels – Part 1 Limit Background Noise Levels 的 3-point 表格中，Category 3 的 Max SPL (LMax) dBA 門檻是不超過 55 dBA；Category 3 包含 open areas for concentration、PA spaces 與 dining areas。

A.gap: 把 Category 3 的 1-point Average SPL 誤當成最高得分 Max SPL。
A.followup: 題目問 maximum number of points 與 maximum SPL，S02 Part 1 表格中 Category 3 的 Max SPL 是哪一列？

B.gap: 把 Category 3 餐飲區誤套用更嚴格的 3-point Average SPL。
B.followup: 在 S02 Part 1 中，45 dBA 對 Category 3 是 Average SPL 還是 Max SPL？題目要求哪一種？

C.gap: 選到 1-point 的 Category 3 Max SPL，忽略最高得分要求。
C.followup: 若要 maximum number of points，S02 Part 1 應看 3-point 還是 1-point 門檻？兩者差多少 dBA？

M07_Q03.stem: According to Part 2: Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what is the minimum Noise Isolation Class (NIC) or Weighted Difference Level (Dw) between rooms for concentration and other regularly occupied spaces?
M07_Q03.explanation: S03 Sound Barriers – Part 2 Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls 的 Option 1: Noise isolation class 表格規定，rooms for concentration 與 other regularly occupied spaces 之間的 minimum NIC 或 Dw 為 40。

D.gap: 嚴重低估 concentration room 隔音需求，未對應 NIC/Dw 表。
D.followup: S03 Part 2 Option 1 的表格中，concentration rooms 這一列的 minimum NIC/Dw 是多少？

A.gap: 把 Loud zones 或更高隔音類型的門檻套到 concentration rooms。
A.followup: S03 Part 2 為什麼要按 wall type 分級？concentration rooms 與 Loud zones 的 NIC/Dw 是否相同？

B.gap: 把 STC/Rw 或 Loud zones 的較高數值誤當作 concentration NIC/Dw。
B.followup: 題目問的是 NIC 或 Dw，不是 STC/Rw；S03 Part 2 Option 1 對 concentration rooms 列的是哪個值？

M07_Q04.stem: According to Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what minimums are required for the doors that connect two occupiable rooms and doors to mechanical equipment rooms?

options.text: Doors must have a non-hollow core and seals at the head, jamb and base.
Doors must have a non-hollow core, a minimum STC/Rw of 30 and seals at the head, jamb and base.
Doors must have a non-hollow core, a minimum STC/Rw of 40 and seals at the head, jamb and base.
Doors must have a non-hollow core, a minimum STC/Rw of 35 and seals at the head, jamb and base.

M07_Q04.explanation: S03 Sound Barriers – Part 1 Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors 要求連接兩個 occupiable rooms 的門與 mechanical equipment rooms 的門需為 non-hollow core、minimum STC/Rw 30，並在 head、jamb、base 設 seals。

C.gap: 掌握門扇與密封要求，但把 STC/Rw 門檻提高到 40。
C.followup: S03 Part 1 對 doors 的 minimum STC/Rw 是獨立列出，是否與部分牆體 40 或 45 門檻相同？

A.gap: 只記得 non-hollow core 和 seals，漏掉 minimum STC/Rw 數值。
A.followup: 除了 head、jamb、base seals，S03 Part 1 對 doors 還要求哪個 STC/Rw 最低值？

D.gap: 知道需要 STC/Rw 門檻，但記成標準中未列的 35。
D.followup: S03 Part 1 的 doors 條文列出的是 30、35 還是 40？請對照 door requirement 原文。

M07_Q05.stem: The WELL Sound concept aims to bolster which of the following?
M07_Q05.explanation: WELL Sound concept introduction 明定目標是透過辨識與緩解 acoustical comfort parameters 來 bolster occupant health and well-being；S01 Sound Mapping – Part 2 Provide Acoustic Design Plan 也要求規劃 background noise、speech privacy、reverberation time 等舒適策略。

A.gap: 把可能效益縮小為 calmness 與 productivity，漏掉健康福祉核心。
A.followup: Sound concept 的開頭用哪個總目標描述 acoustical comfort，是否只限工作效率？

D.gap: 把 Sound concept 與健身、社交目標混淆，偏離聲學舒適。
D.followup: S01/S02/S04 關注 noise、privacy 與 reverberation，這些要求主要支援哪類 occupant outcome？

B.gap: 把 WELL 目標商業化為 profitability，忽略 health and well-being。
B.followup: WELL Sound concept 說要改善 occupant experience，但最終要 bolster 的是滿意度利潤還是健康福祉？

M07_Q06.stem: In Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels studies have shown exposure to traffic noise can lead to an increased risk of what health issues?
M07_Q06.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels – Part 1 Limit Background Noise Levels 設定 ambient background noise 上限；其 Issue 說明 traffic noise exposure 研究顯示會增加 cardiovascular system issues、diabetes、hypertension、stroke、depression 與 high blood pressure 風險。

B.gap: 把交通噪音健康風險簡化成短期不適，漏掉慢性疾病。
B.followup: S02 Issue 列出的 traffic noise 風險是 headaches，還是 diabetes、stroke、hypertension 等慢性問題？

A.gap: 抓到 hypertension 但混入 anxiety，未完整對應標準列舉。
A.followup: S02 Issue 在 hypertension 之外還列出哪些 metabolic 或 cerebrovascular 風險？

C.gap: 以一般壓力與心臟病表述替代標準列出的具體疾病組合。
C.followup: 請比較 myocardial 或 chronic stress 說法，S02 Issue 原文實際列出的三個關鍵病症是哪些？

M07_Q07.stem: One solution for interior noise as specified in Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels is?

options.text: Selecting HVAC equipment with lower sound ratings.
Providing occupants with noise reducing headphones.

M07_Q07.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels – Part 1 Limit Background Noise Levels 控制 interior/exterior ambient noise；Solutions 明確指出 interior noise sources 可透過 selecting HVAC equipment with lower sound ratings 與降低 ducts 內聲音來控制。

C.gap: 把 equipment room 隔音當成主要解法，未抓到 HVAC 選型要求。
C.followup: S02 Solutions 對 interior noise 是先談 equipment room insulation，還是 HVAC equipment sound ratings？

B.gap: 把責任轉給使用者配戴耳機，非 WELL 的建築系統控制策略。
B.followup: S02 Part 1 是要讓空間背景噪音達標，提供耳機能否改變 HVAC 或 ducts 的 noise source？

D.gap: 混淆 interior noise 與 exterior noise，選到 facade/windows 策略。
D.followup: S02 Solutions 如何區分 interior sources 與 exterior sources？windows 是處理哪一側的 noise？

M07_Q08.stem: In order to meet the minimum requirements of Part 1: Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces of Feature S05: Sound Reducing Surfaces, open workspaces must meet which of the following minimum NRC/aw at ceiling values?

options.text: 0.75 for at least 75% of available ceiling area
0.75 for at least 50% of available ceiling area

M07_Q08.explanation: S05 Sound Reducing Surfaces – Part 1 Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces 的 open workspaces 表格規定，1 point 最低 ceiling 要達 Minimum NRC/aw 0.75，且覆蓋 at least 75% of available ceiling area。

D.gap: 選到 2-point 的高性能天花要求，不是 minimum requirement。
D.followup: S05 Part 1 open workspaces 表格中，0.90 for all ceiling area 對應幾分？題目問 minimum 嗎？

B.gap: 記得 NRC/aw 0.75，但把 open workspaces 覆蓋率誤用成 50%。
B.followup: S05 Part 1 中 50% available ceiling area 是哪類空間？open workspaces 的比例是多少？

C.gap: 把 0.75 材料要求過度擴大為全部天花面積。
C.followup: open workspaces 的 1-point 門檻要求 all available ceiling area，還是 at least 75%？

M07_Q09.stem: Which WELL feature requires projects to strategize an acoustical plan that identifies sources of noise that can negatively impact interior spaces?
M07_Q09.explanation: S01 Sound Mapping – Summary 說此 feature 要求 projects strategize an acoustical plan，辨識可能 negatively impact interior spaces 的 noise sources；Part 2 Provide Acoustic Design Plan 進一步要求提出 acoustical solutions 與 implementation timeline。

B.gap: 引用了 Part 2 名稱，但漏掉題目問的是 WELL feature 名稱。
B.followup: 題目問 which WELL feature；S01 的 feature name 是 Sound Mapping，還是 Part 2 的 Acoustic Design Plan？

C.gap: 把 acoustical plan 當成 feature 名稱，未使用標準正式名稱。
C.followup: S01 Summary 提到 acoustical plan，但頁首 feature code 後面的正式名稱是什麼？

A.gap: 以近似詞 Sound Planning 代替 WELL v2 中不存在的 feature name。
A.followup: 在 WELL Sound 章節中，S01 的正式標題是否出現 Sound Planning 這個名稱？

M07_Q10.stem: According to Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels, to earn 3 Points, what is the maximum Sound Pressure Level (SPL) dBA measurement of an open dining area (a Category 3 space)?
M07_Q10.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels – Part 1 Limit Background Noise Levels 的 3-point 門檻中，Category 3 的 Max SPL (LMax) dBA 不得超過 55 dBA；open dining area 屬於 dining areas 的 Category 3。

A.gap: 把 Category 3 的 Max SPL 誤套用成更低的 Average SPL 或其他類別。
A.followup: S02 Part 1 表格裡 Category 3、3-point、Max SPL dBA 三個條件交會在哪個數值？

B.gap: 混用 dBC 與 dBA，且 60 dBC 不是該列門檻。
B.followup: 題目指定 dBA measurement；S02 Part 1 同列的 dBA 與 dBC 門檻能否互換？

C.gap: 單位與大小寫混亂，且 50 dBC 不是 Category 3 Max SPL。
C.followup: S02 表格中 Category 3 的 Max SPL 要看 dBA 還是 dBC？dBA 數值是多少？

M07_Q11.stem: Feature S04: Reverberation Time states that reverberation time is the length of time taken for a sound to decay by __ dB from an initial impulse level.
M07_Q11.explanation: S04 Reverberation Time – Part 1 Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds 以 reverberation time seconds 設計/驗證；其 Issue 定義 reverberation time 是聲音從 initial impulse level 衰減 60 dB 所需時間。

C.gap: 接近但少記 5 dB，未掌握 reverberation time 的標準定義。
C.followup: S04 Issue 對 reverberation time 的定義固定使用多少 dB decay，而不是近似值？

D.gap: 把 60 dB 衰減定義誤記為 50 dB。
D.followup: 若 Part 1 表格用 seconds 表示 t，前面的 Issue 如何定義這個 t 的 dB 衰減基準？

A.gap: 選到更低且大小寫錯誤的 dB 值，偏離標準定義。
A.followup: S04 的 reverberation time 是測聲音衰減到 45 dB，還是衰減 60 dB 的時間？

M07_Q12.stem: A new WELL project is concerned with having the proper sound barriers to protect the privacy of the buildings occupants. If choosing Option 2: Speech privacy in Part 2: Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what is the minimum NIC + NC or Dw + LAeq for an enclosed office?
M07_Q12.explanation: S03 Sound Barriers – Part 2 Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls 的 Option 2: Speech privacy 表格規定，Enclosed Quiet zones 到 enclosed offices 或 areas for conferencing, learning or sleep 的 minimum NIC + NC 或 Dw + LAeq 為 80。

B.gap: 把 80 誤提高到 90，超出 Speech privacy 表格列值。
B.followup: S03 Part 2 Option 2 中，enclosed office 對應的 minimum sum 是 80、85 還是 90？

A.gap: 套用到 open areas 或 circulation 的較低 speech privacy 門檻。
A.followup: 接收空間若是 enclosed office，是否能使用 open concentration 或 circulation 的 70 門檻？

C.gap: 未理解 NIC+NC/Dw+LAeq 是總和門檻，數值低到不符合表格。
C.followup: S03 Option 2 的 speech privacy 是單一背景噪音值，還是隔音量與背景聲等級的總和？

M07_Q13.stem: In Feature S04: Reverberation Time, a solution to reduce reverberation time is?

options.text: Use furniture that is made of wood.
Add reflective surfaces such as glass or stone.
Add absorptive surface finishes at ceiling and walls.
Purchase telecommunication/AV equipment that enhances speech intelligibility.

M07_Q13.explanation: S04 Reverberation Time – Part 1 Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds 要按 space type 達成 t 的秒數範圍；Solutions 說可在 ceilings、walls 與 furniture 加入 absorptive surface finishes，並減少 glass、drywall、stone 等 reflective surfaces。

A.gap: 把木製家具視為吸音策略，但材料本身未必提供足夠 absorption。
A.followup: S04 Solutions 強調 absorptive surface finishes；wood furniture 與 acoustic absorptive treatment 是否等同？

D.gap: 混淆 AV speech intelligibility 設備與降低 reverberation time 的材料策略。
D.followup: S04 提到 AV equipment 的改善，是在降低 reverberation 後提升效果，還是購買設備本身即可達標？

B.gap: 選到會增加 reflected sound energy 的硬反射表面。
B.followup: S04 Solutions 說 glass 或 stone 會如何影響 reflected sound energy 與 reverberation time？

M07_Q14.stem: To achieve points for Option 1: Reverberation time, design in Part 1: Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds of Feature S04: Reverberation Time, what is the maximum reverberation time in areas for speech?
M07_Q14.explanation: S04 Reverberation Time – Part 1 Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds 的 Option 1: Reverberation time, design 表格中，Areas for speech 的 reverberation time t 要介於 0.8 到 1.0 seconds。

A.gap: 套用到中等體積 learning/conferencing 的範圍，非 speech areas。
A.followup: S04 Part 1 表格中 Areas for speech 是 N/A volume；哪個 t range 專屬這一列？

D.gap: 把大型 learning/conferencing 的 0.6-1.0 誤當 speech areas 門檻。
D.followup: Areas for speech 不按 volume 分級；與 v > 20,000 ft3 的 learning/conferencing 有何不同？

C.gap: 選到偏長的 reverberation range，可能混到 fitness 或 music 類型。
C.followup: S04 Part 1 中 speech intelligibility 相關空間是否允許 1.4 seconds 的 reverberation time？

M07_Q15.stem: A number of studies have indicated that ________________________________ is a major cause of complaint and results in occupant dissatisfaction.
M07_Q15.explanation: WELL Sound concept introduction 說多項研究指出 internally generated noise 是 major cause of complaint，並會造成 occupant dissatisfaction；S02 Maximum Noise Levels – Part 1 Limit Background Noise Levels 因此設定 HVAC、exterior intrusion 等 ambient background noise 上限。

C.gap: 把問題轉到 Water 概念，未對應 Sound 章節的內部噪音。
C.followup: 這題出自 Sound concept，原文說 major cause of complaint 是 water taste 還是 internally generated noise？

A.gap: 把一般設施抱怨誤認為聲學舒適的核心問題。
A.followup: WELL Sound introduction 討論 HVAC、occupants 與 appliances 產生的聲音，與 parking 數量有何關聯？

B.gap: 把空間配置不足混入題目，未抓住 occupant dissatisfaction 的聲學來源。
B.followup: 在 Sound concept 中，occupant dissatisfaction 是由 bathrooms 不足，還是內部產生的 noise 引發？

M07_Q16.stem: In Part 1: Provide Minimum Background Sound of Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound, a sound masking system must be commissioned so that the sound pressure levels (SPL) do not exceed ______ in enclosed rooms labeled as Quiet zones.
M07_Q16.explanation: S06 Minimum Background Sound – Part 1 Provide Minimum Background Sound 要求 sound masking system 經 commissioning 後，enclosed rooms labeled as Quiet zones 的 SPL 不得超過 42 dBA；open areas with Quiet/Circulation zones 則是不超過 48 dBA。

D.gap: 選到過低且未列於 S06 的 38 dBA，誤把上限當更嚴格越好。
D.followup: S06 Part 1 對 enclosed Quiet zones 的 commissioned SPL 上限明列多少，而不是任意降低？

B.gap: 把 open areas 的 48 dBA 上限套到 enclosed Quiet rooms。
B.followup: S06 Part 1 分別列出 open areas 與 enclosed Quiet zones；兩者的 SPL 上限是否相同？

A.gap: 選到標準未列的中間值，未區分 42 與 48 dBA 場景。
A.followup: 若房間是 enclosed 且 labeled as Quiet zones，S06 表格該看 42 dBA 還是 open-area 的較高上限？

M07_Q17.stem: Interior walls must have a minimum Sound Transmission Class (STC) between adjacent Quiet zones per Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature: SO3 Sound Barriers?
M07_Q17.explanation: S03 Sound Barriers – Part 1 Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors 的 interior wall 表格規定，Between adjacent Quiet zones 的 minimum STC 或 Rw 為 50；若同時符合多類型，採用較嚴格值。

B.gap: 把 rooms for concentration 的 45 STC/Rw 誤套到 adjacent Quiet zones。
B.followup: S03 Part 1 中 adjacent Quiet zones 與 rooms for concentration 的 minimum STC/Rw 是否不同？

A.gap: 把 Loud zones 與其他 occupiable spaces 的最高牆體門檻套錯。
A.followup: S03 Part 1 哪一類 interior wall 需要 60 STC/Rw？adjacent Quiet zones 是否屬於那一類？

C.gap: 選到 circulation 與 regularly occupied spaces 的最低牆體門檻。
C.followup: 若牆體在兩個 Quiet zones 之間，能否只用 circulation zone 的 40 STC/Rw？

M07_Q18.stem: When noise from internal or external sources is increased in a space, occupants have been found to be?

options.text: Easily distracted, less productive and susceptible to burnout.
Less productive, less collaborative and depressed.
Easily distracted, hearing impaired and less collaborative.

M07_Q18.explanation: S01 Sound Mapping – Issue 說明 internal or external noise 增加時，occupants 會 easily distracted、less productive 且 susceptible to burnout；Part 1 Label Acoustic Zones 正是用 Loud、Quiet、Mixed、Circulation 分區來預防 acoustic disturbance。

D.gap: 抓到 distraction，但加入 hearing impaired 與 collaboration 表述，非原文組合。
D.followup: S01 Issue 直接列出的三個 occupant outcomes 是 distraction、productivity 與哪個 burnout 相關表述？

C.gap: 混入 collaboration 與 depression，未對應 S01 Issue 的精確結果。
C.followup: S01 Issue 是否說高噪音讓人 depressed，還是 susceptible to burnout？請核對原文。

B.gap: 以焦慮與憂鬱概括噪音影響，漏掉 productivity 與 burnout。
B.followup: 題目問 increased noise in a space；S01 Issue 用哪三個短語描述 occupants 的反應？

M07_Q19.stem: A 10-story office building in downtown New York City is aiming for WELL Certification. Due to the location, they are worried about the noise levels from the streets that could travel into the building's interior spaces. To help with this issue, the project team needs to design the floor plans with intent and use in mind. For example, a typical office can be categorized into what four key types of programming?

options.text: Concentration, socialization, teaching and break rooms
Break rooms, teaching, collaboration and socialization

M07_Q19.explanation: S01 Sound Mapping – Solutions 說 office floor plans 應依 intent and use 設計，典型 office 可分為 concentration、collaboration、socialization 與 learning 四種 programming；Part 1 再把這些用途轉為 acoustic zone labels。

D.gap: 把 learning 誤換成 teaching，接近但不是標準列舉的 programming。
D.followup: S01 Solutions 原文列出的第四種 office programming 是 learning 還是 teaching？兩者在 WELL wording 中有差別嗎？

A.gap: 漏掉 collaboration，且把 break rooms 當成四大 programming 類型。
A.followup: S01 Solutions 的四類 programming 是否包含 break rooms，還是以 collaboration 作為核心辦公活動？

C.gap: 以 break rooms、teaching 取代 concentration 與 learning，偏離 floor plan intent 分類。
C.followup: 若目標是減少社交空間干擾專注空間，S01 Solutions 為何必須保留 concentration 類別？

M07_Q20.stem: A suggested solution to exterior noise as specified in Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels is?

options.text: Providing occupants with noise reducing headphones.
Selecting HVAC equipment with lower sound ratings.

M07_Q20.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels – Part 1 Limit Background Noise Levels 控制 exterior noise intrusion 等 ambient background noise；Solutions 指出 exterior noise 可透過 building facade、windows 與 exterior penetrations 的 sound reduction 控制。

A.gap: 把 mechanical equipment room 的內部噪音處理誤當 exterior noise 策略。
A.followup: S02 Solutions 說 exterior sources 是透過 facade/windows，還是 equipment room insulation 來控制？

D.gap: 選到 interior HVAC noise 的正確策略，但題目問 exterior noise。
D.followup: lower sound ratings 的 HVAC equipment 對應 S02 的 interior sources；外部街道噪音應看哪個 building element？

C.gap: 把個人防護用品當成建築聲學解法，不能降低 exterior intrusion。
C.followup: S02 Part 1 要使背景噪音達標，noise reducing headphones 是否能改變 facade 或 windows 的隔音性能？

M07_Q21.stem: In Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels of Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels, a new apartment complex must test the background noise levels in the bedrooms to make sure they do not exceed an average of 35 dBA. What minimum period of time must the background noise be measured?

options.text: Testing must occur for 12 hours between 8 pm to 8 am
12 hour period that includes the hours from 10 pm to 7 am
6 hour period at any given time of the day

M07_Q21.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels – Part 1 Limit Background Noise Levels 對 dwelling units 規定，bedrooms 的 average background noise levels 不得超過 35 dBA (Leq)，且必須在至少 12-hour minimum time period 測量，並包含 10 pm 到 7 am。

D.gap: 測量時長不足，且未涵蓋夜間睡眠時段要求。
D.followup: S02 Part 1 對 dwelling bedroom 的 35 dBA Leq，是 6 小時即可，還是至少 12 小時且含夜間？

C.gap: 把最低 12 小時誤解為必須 24 小時，過度擴大要求。
C.followup: S02 Part 1 使用 minimum time period；它要求 24 hours，還是只要求包含 10 pm to 7 am？

A.gap: 時長接近但夜間時段錯誤，未包含標準指定 10 pm to 7 am。
A.followup: 若測量 8 pm 到 8 am，是否必然符合 S02 指定包含 10 pm 到 7 am？題目選項如何精確表述？

M07_Q22.stem: In Feature S03: Sound Barriers, there is evidence that links noise annoyance in multi-story housing to what health issues?

options.text: Poor mental health and perceived stress

M07_Q22.explanation: S03 Sound Barriers – Part 1 Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors 與 Part 2 Achieve Sound Isolation at Walls 都是為了提高 sound isolation；其 Issue 明確指出 multi-story housing 的 noise annoyance 與 poor mental health 及 perceived stress 有關。

B.gap: 以 depression 和 anxiety 替代標準中的 mental health 與 stress 表述。
B.followup: S03 Issue 對 multi-story housing 的健康連結，使用的是 depression/anxiety 還是 poor mental health/perceived stress？

A.gap: 加入 memory loss，偏離 Sound Barriers 針對 housing annoyance 的證據。
A.followup: memory retention 常出現在 reverberation/classroom 脈絡；S03 Sound Barriers 的 housing evidence 提到什麼？

D.gap: 抓到 perceived stress，但把 poor mental health 誤換成 anxiety。
D.followup: S03 Issue 的第二個結果是 perceived stress；第一個結果是否更廣義地寫作 poor mental health？

M07_Q23.stem: An area mapped as part of the project includes a conference room for collaboration. For this area to comply with Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature S03: Sound Barriers, what is the minimum STC or RW requirement of the conference room?
M07_Q23.explanation: S03 Sound Barriers – Part 1 Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors 的 interior wall 表格規定，areas for conferencing、learning or sleeping 與 other regularly occupied spaces 之間的 minimum STC 或 Rw 為 55；conference room 屬於 conferencing area。

D.gap: 把 concentration room 的 45 STC/Rw 門檻誤用於 conference room。
D.followup: S03 Part 1 中 conferencing areas 是否與 concentration rooms 同列？conference room 應對哪個 minimum STC/Rw？

C.gap: 把 adjacent Quiet zones 的 50 STC/Rw 套到 conferencing 空間。
C.followup: conference room for collaboration 是 Quiet zones 之間，還是 areas for conferencing 與其他 regularly occupied spaces？

A.gap: 套用 Loud zones 的 60 STC/Rw，雖較嚴但不是 conference room 最低值。
A.followup: S03 Part 1 哪一列才需要 60 STC/Rw？conference room 是否屬於 Loud zone？

M07_Q24.stem: The length of reverberation time is a function of what?

options.text: Sound absorbing properties, room height and number of doors
Room size, surface areas and sound absorbing properties of surface finishes
Room height, surface areas and sound absorbing properties of surface finishes
Room size, surface areas and number of doors

M07_Q24.explanation: S04 Reverberation Time – Part 1 Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds 依空間用途設定 t 的秒數；其 Issue 說 reverberation time 長短是 room size、surface area 以及 surface finishes 的 sound-absorbing properties 的函數。

A.gap: 把 room size 誤換成 room height，並加入 doors 數量。
A.followup: S04 Issue 說 reverberation time 是 room size 還是只看 room height？doors 是否列為主要函數？

C.gap: 抓到 surface areas 與吸音性，但把 room size 縮成 room height。
C.followup: 若只看 height，是否能代表 S04 Issue 中 room size 的整體 volume/scale 概念？

D.gap: 包含 room size 與 surface areas，但把吸音性錯換成門數。
D.followup: S04 Issue 的第三個因素是 doors number，還是 surface finishes 的 sound-absorbing properties？

M07_Q25.stem: Feature S04: Reverberation Time review studies showing high reverberation times in classrooms can link to many negative effects. Which of the following is not one of the many effects?

options.text: Reduces speech intelligibility among hard of hearing a non-native speaking populations

M07_Q25.explanation: S04 Reverberation Time – Part 1 Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds 旨在支援 speech intelligibility；其 Issue 說 classroom 高 reverberation times 會增加 auditory workload，降低 cognition、memory retention、concentration，並降低 hard of hearing 與 non-native speaking populations 的 speech intelligibility，未列出 hearing loss。

A.gap: 把標準明列的 reduced cognition 誤認為非負面效果。
A.followup: S04 Issue 對 classrooms 的 high reverberation times 是否明確提到 reduce cognition？

D.gap: 忽略 memory retention 是高混響在 classroom 的列舉影響。
D.followup: S04 Issue 中 cognition 旁邊還列了 memory retention 與 concentration；這些是否都是負面效果？

C.gap: 把 speech intelligibility 影響誤排除，未注意特定族群描述。
C.followup: S04 Issue 是否說 high reverberation 會降低 hard of hearing 與 non-native speakers 的 speech intelligibility？

M07_Q26.stem: A new learning center is looking for a way to improve the reverberation time within their classrooms. Which of the following options will assist in achieving this goal?

options.text: Add absorptive surfaces, alter room geometry and increase reflective surfaces.
Reduce reflective surfaces, alter room geometry and add absorptive surfaces.
Reduce reflected sound energy, increased reflective surfaces and add absorptive surfaces.
Reduce reflected sound energy, alter room geometry and add absorptive surfaces.

M07_Q26.explanation: S04 Reverberation Time, Part 1: Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds 要控制 classroom 等 learning spaces 的 reverberation time；WELL 說可透過減少 reflective surfaces、調整 room geometry、增加 absorptive surface finishes 來降低 reflected sound energy 並支援 speech intelligibility。

C.gap: 把增加反射面誤認為能降低 reverberation time。
C.followup: 若 S04 的目標是減少 reflected sound energy，為什麼增加 reflective surfaces 會讓 classroom 更難控制混響？

A.gap: 忽略 reflective surfaces 會延長聲音衰減。
A.followup: S04 說 room geometry 與 absorptive finishes 有幫助，那 reflective surfaces 在 reverberation time 中扮演什麼反向角色？

D.gap: 只說降低反射能量，未指出要減少反射面。
D.followup: 在實際設計中，若要減少 reflected sound energy，你會如何把這個目標轉成 surface material 選擇？

M07_Q27.stem: A solution in Feature S02: Sound Reducing Surfaces that minimizes reverberation, significantly reduces sound across human speech frequencies and can greatly reduce unwanted buildup of reflected sound is called?
M07_Q27.explanation: S05 Sound Reducing Surfaces, Part 1: Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces 說明 acoustical treatment 可在 human speech frequencies 顯著降低聲音，減少 reflected sound 的 buildup，並支援 reverberation 與 speech intelligibility 控制。

D.gap: 把整體舒適結果誤當成具體處理方案。
D.followup: S05 Part 1 要求的是哪一類 acoustic material 或 treatment，而不是最後感受到的 acoustical comfort？

A.gap: 把 speech intelligibility 誤認為材料處理名稱。
A.followup: 若 speech intelligibility 是改善後的 performance outcome，S05 用什麼 treatment 來降低 speech frequencies 的反射？

B.gap: 把 reverberation time 指標誤當成解決方案。
B.followup: S05 是用 acoustical treatment 影響 reverberation time；兩者哪一個是策略，哪一個是量測結果？

M07_Q28.stem: What area of a room typically provides the greatest area of coverage where extremely absorptive materials can offer the best outcome to reducing reverberation?
M07_Q28.explanation: S05 Sound Reducing Surfaces, Part 1: Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces 指出 ceilings typically provide the greatest area of coverage；高吸音材料用在 ceiling area 通常最能降低 reverberation 並控制 speech intelligibility 與 occupied noise levels。

D.gap: 誤以為地板是吸音覆蓋面積最大的位置。
D.followup: S05 為什麼特別指出 ceiling area，而不是 floor，通常提供最大可用 absorptive coverage？

C.gap: 混淆牆面處理與最大覆蓋面積來源。
C.followup: S05 也提到 wall treatment，但若題目問 greatest area of coverage，哪個 room surface 更符合原文？

B.gap: 把家具吸音輔助誤當主要覆蓋面。
B.followup: Furniture 可吸收部分聲音，但在 S05 的 coverage 邏輯中，它和 ceiling 的面積優勢有何差別？

M07_Q29.stem: A new project that includes a designated dining hall is looking to reduce sound by adding absorptive surfaces. If they are looking to earn 2 points, what is the minimum NRC/aw for ceilings?

options.text: 0.80 on at least 75% of available ceiling area
0.60 on at least 50% of available ceiling area

M07_Q29.explanation: S05 Sound Reducing Surfaces, Part 1: Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces 的 dining areas 表格要求 2 points 時，ceilings 的 minimum NRC/aw 為 0.90 for all available ceiling area；1 point 才是 0.75 on at least 50% of available ceiling area。

B.gap: 把較高覆蓋率與較低 NRC 門檻混在一起。
B.followup: S05 dining areas 的 2 points 同時要求 NRC/aw 數值與 ceiling area，哪一個數字必須達到 0.90？

A.gap: 低估 2 points 對 dining ceiling 的吸音係數。
A.followup: 若 dining hall 要拿 S05 Part 1 的 2 points，為什麼 all available ceiling area 還不夠，NRC/aw 也要更高？

D.gap: 把最低覆蓋與低 NRC 誤認為最高得分門檻。
D.followup: S05 表格中 dining areas 的 1 point 是多少覆蓋與 NRC/aw？它和 2 points 差在哪裡？

M07_Q30.stem: What is the intent of Feature S03: Sound Barriers?

options.text: To increase the level of speech privacy between vertically adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder acoustical comfort.
To increase the level of speech privacy between horizontally adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder acoustical comfort.
To increase the level of speech privacy between horizontally adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder thermal comfort.
To decrease the level of speech privacy between horizontally adjacent enclosures and highlight design constraints that may hinder acoustical comfort.

M07_Q30.explanation: S03 Sound Barriers, Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors 的 intent 是增加 enclosed spaces 之間的 sound isolation 與 speech privacy；牆體與門需達最低 acoustical separation，例如門須 non-hollow core、minimum STC/Rw 30 並有 seals。

D.gap: 把提升 speech privacy 誤解成降低隱私。
D.followup: S03 的牆與門要求是為了增加還是降低 enclosed spaces 之間的 speech privacy？

A.gap: 把 horizontal adjacency 誤換成垂直相鄰空間。
A.followup: S03 Sound Barriers 的核心是 enclosed spaces 間的牆與門，題幹提到的 adjacent enclosures 是哪種相鄰關係？

C.gap: 把 acoustical comfort 誤換成 thermal comfort。
C.followup: S03 的 STC/Rw 與 door seals 是 acoustic separation 工具，這和 thermal comfort 有什麼不同？

M07_Q31.stem: A WELL project is working on obtaining 1 point for Part 1: Enhanced Speed Reduction Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound. Which features must the project meet in order to earn this point?

options.text: Part 1: Label Acoustic Zones of Feature S01: Sound Mapping
Part 1: Provide Minimum Background of Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound
Part 1: Design for Sound Isolation at Walls and Doors of Feature S03: Sound Barriers
Part 1: Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds of Feature S04: Reverberation Time

M07_Q31.explanation: S06 Minimum Background Sound, Part 2: Provide Enhanced Speech Reduction 要求先達成 S06 Part 1: Provide Minimum Background Sound，且還需在 S03 Part 2 或 S05 Part 1 取得 two points；因此只列出 S06 Part 1 只能代表其中一項必要條件。

D.gap: 把 reverberation time 門檻誤當 S06 背景聲要求。
D.followup: S06 Part 2 要連動的是 speech reduction 與 background sound，S04 reverberation time 在條件清單中出現了嗎？

A.gap: 誤把 S01 acoustic zone labeling 當作得分條件。
A.followup: S01 labels 可支援聲學規劃，但 S06 Part 2 的明文 prerequisite 是否要求 Label Acoustic Zones？

C.gap: 混淆 S03 Part 1 設計要求與 Part 2 得分要求。
C.followup: S06 Part 2 指定的是 S03 Part 2 的 two points，為什麼 S03 Part 1 walls and doors 不足以替代？

M07_Q32.stem: According to the Feature S07: Impact Noise Management, impact noise travels through what structures?
M07_Q32.explanation: S07 Impact Noise Management, Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Impact Noise Rating 說明 impact noise travels through structures as vibrations，例如 walls, floors, columns, piping，之後再以 airborne noise 形式傳到 listener。

B.gap: 加入 doors，漏掉 piping 這個結構傳振例子。
B.followup: S07 描述 impact noise 的 vibration path 時，除了 walls、floors、columns，還明確列出哪個 building service element？

D.gap: 把 rafters 與 doors 誤當標準列舉結構。
D.followup: S07 的例子是否包含 rafters 或 doors？impact noise 更常沿哪些 structural elements 傳遞？

A.gap: 漏掉 floors 與 columns，混入未列舉 rafters。
A.followup: 若 impact noise 源自 footfall 或 gym equipment，為什麼 S07 會特別列出 floors 和 columns？

M07_Q33.stem: According to Feature S05: Sound Reducing Surfaces of the WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™), ceilings provide the ________ area of coverage where highly absorptive materials can offer the best performance in reducing reverberation.
M07_Q33.explanation: S05 Sound Reducing Surfaces, Part 1: Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces 的 solutions 說 ceilings typically provide the greatest area of coverage，讓 highly absorptive materials 在降低 reverberation 上有最佳表現。

B.gap: 把 greatest 完全反向理解成 least。
B.followup: S05 為何說 ceiling area 對 absorptive materials 特別有利？若是 least，會如何影響 reverberation 控制？

D.gap: 把性能評價 best 誤當面積描述。
D.followup: 題幹問的是 area of coverage 的大小，而不是材料 performance；S05 原文使用哪個 coverage 形容詞？

C.gap: 把正向覆蓋優勢誤判為最差位置。
C.followup: 若 ceiling 是 worst coverage，S05 為何還會把 ceiling NRC/aw 列為 Part 1 的主要門檻？

M07_Q34.stem: To meet the thresholds for Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Impact Noise Rating of Feature S07: Impact Noise Management, which ASTM and ISO standards (or equivalent) must be followed?
M07_Q34.explanation: S07 Impact Noise Management, Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Impact Noise Rating 要求 NISR 以 on-site measurement 執行，並依 ASTM E1007-19、ISO 16283 或 equivalent，由 acoustics professional 測量。

B.gap: 混用 Part 1 IIC 材料測試標準與 Part 2 現場 NISR。
B.followup: S07 Part 1 的 IIC ratings 使用 ASTM E492-09/ISO 717.2；Part 2 的 NISR on-site measurement 改用哪些標準？

A.gap: 把 ASTM 1573 sound masking 標準誤用到 impact noise。
A.followup: ASTM 1573-18 在 S06 sound masking 驗證出現；S07 Part 2 的 impact noise rating 是否使用同一標準？

D.gap: 只抓到 ISO 16283，卻搭配錯誤 ASTM。
D.followup: S07 Part 2 同時列出 ASTM 與 ISO；若 ISO 16283 正確，對應的 ASTM 編號是什麼？

M07_Q35.stem: A new WELL Certified building that is offering a dining hall for its employees wants to earn the max points for Feature S07: Impact Noise Management for impact noise rating. In order to achieve this, what is the minimum Normalized Impact Sound Ratings (NISR) level needed?
M07_Q35.explanation: S07 Impact Noise Management, Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Impact Noise Rating 的 table 中，Areas for Retail and Dining 在 2 points 時要求 floor-ceiling assembly 位於 below，minimum NISR 為 47；1 point 才是 42。

B.gap: 把 dining 的 1 point NISR 誤認為 max points。
B.followup: S07 Part 2 的 dining row 中，1 point 與 2 points 的 NISR 分別是多少？

A.gap: 套用了 fitness 或 enclosed concentration 的 2 points 門檻。
A.followup: S07 Part 2 不同 space type 的 NISR 不同；dining 是否和 fitness 共用 52 的 2 points 門檻？

D.gap: 使用了表格中不存在的中間數值。
D.followup: 若 WELL table 只列 42 與 47 給 retail and dining，50 這個 NISR 從哪個 requirement 推得出？

M07_Q36.stem: Depending on the type of surface materials, reverberation time and reflected sound energy has the potential of creating uncomfortable environments. In schools, where spaces need to be conducive to learning, what must the reverberation time support?
M07_Q36.explanation: S04 Reverberation Time, Part 1: Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds 的 intent 要設計 comfortable reverberation times，以支援 speech intelligibility、vocal effort 與 concentration；在 learning spaces 中，高 reverberation 會降低學生理解與記憶。

B.gap: 把控制手段 surface finishes 誤當學習結果。
B.followup: S04 可以透過 absorptive surface finishes 控制 reverberation，但學校空間最終要支援哪種 speech outcome？

C.gap: 把需降低的 reflected sound 誤當支持目標。
C.followup: S04 說 reducing reflected sound energy 可改善 reverberation；它是要被增加還是被控制？

D.gap: 把 speech privacy 與 classroom intelligibility 混淆。
D.followup: 在 classroom 中，S04 強調聽懂教學內容；這和 S03/S06 的 speech privacy 目標有何不同？

M07_Q37.stem: According to Part 2: Prioritize Audio Devices and Policies of Feature S07: Enhanced Audio Designs, which of the following does NOT aid in the support of individual acoustical needs?

options.text: All audio devices are managed internally by a qualified professional.
Eligible employees can request alternative workplace arrangements to meet their individual acoustic comfort needs.
Employees must purchase any specialized accessories they will need to utilize sound enhancement technology.
A minimum of one daily quiet hour is scheduled and signage is used to indicate both the location and intended activities of Quiet and Mixed Zones.

M07_Q37.explanation: S08 Enhanced Audio Devices, Part 2: Prioritize Audio Devices and Policies 要求至少三項支持 individual acoustical needs，其中 telecommunication accessories 應 upon request 提供，且 at no cost 或 subsidized by at least 50%；要求員工自行購買不符合此 requirement。

D.gap: 誤把 quiet hour 與 signage 視為不支援個人需求。
D.followup: S08 Part 2 明列 daily quiet hour 與 Quiet/Mixed Zones signage，這如何幫助 individual acoustic comfort？

A.gap: 忽略 qualified professional 管理 audio devices 是合格策略。
A.followup: S08 Part 2 為什麼要求 audio devices 由 qualified professional 或 IT 管理，而不是視為無關措施？

B.gap: 忽略 alternative workplace arrangements 是明列支持措施。
B.followup: 若員工因 acoustic comfort 需要調整工作安排，S08 Part 2 是否把這列為可採取的支持方式？

M07_Q38.stem: The effects of exterior noise from transportation have been shown to impact health and well-being in different ways. What health concern do both men and women share from spending time in spaces that are close to busy highways or airports?
M07_Q38.explanation: WELL Sound concept overview notes that transportation noise near roads and airports is linked to health impacts and specifically reports elevated myocardial infarction risk in men from nighttime road traffic noise and in women from nighttime air traffic noise. S02 Maximum Noise Levels, Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels operationalizes this concern by setting ambient noise thresholds such as Category 1 Average SPL 35 dBA for 3 points.

B.gap: 把一般壓力反應誤當該研究的共同疾病結果。
B.followup: 題幹問 busy highways 或 airports 相關研究中男女共同提到的 health concern，是否只是 stress levels？

A.gap: 抓到交通噪音相關疾病，卻非題目引述的共同結果。
A.followup: Sound overview 確實提到 hypertension，但那段 4,115 人研究指出男女風險升高的是哪一項？

C.gap: 把睡眠干擾誤當該研究的共同心血管結果。
C.followup: 交通噪音可能造成 sleep disturbance，但題幹問男女在道路與航空夜間噪音中共享的哪個風險？

M07_Q39.stem: Improving speech intelligibility in classrooms by implementing audio systems can help support participation for deaf and hard-of-hearing students, which is linked to what areas of improvement, according to WELL Feature S08: Enhanced Audio Devices?
M07_Q39.explanation: S08 Enhanced Audio Devices, Part 1: Provide Enhanced Speech Intelligibility 的 issue 說提升 classroom speech intelligibility 可支持 deaf and hard-of-hearing students 的 participation，並連結到 quality of life、social contact with peers 與 mental health 的改善。

A.gap: 只記到 mental health，漏掉另外兩項改善。
A.followup: S08 的 classroom participation 效益除了 mental health，還列出哪些與同儕和生活品質有關的結果？

B.gap: 只抓到 social contact，忽略完整三項效益。
B.followup: 若題幹問 linked to what areas of improvement，S08 是否只提到 peer contact 一個面向？

C.gap: 只記到 quality of life，漏掉心理與社交面。
C.followup: S08 把 speech intelligibility 對 deaf and hard-of-hearing students 的效益列成幾個面向？

M07_Q40.explanation: S07 Impact Noise Management, Part 2: Meet Thresholds for Impact Noise Rating 使用 Normalized Impact Sound Ratings (NISR) 作為現場量測 metric，並以不同 space type 的 minimum NISR 門檻判定得分。

B.gap: 把 NISR 誤認為機構名稱而非聲學指標。
B.followup: S07 Part 2 的 table 把 NISR 放在 minimum rating 欄位，這比較像 organization 還是 acoustic metric？

D.gap: 把 Impact 誤換成 Industrial，偏離 impact noise 主題。
D.followup: S07 處理 footfall、gym equipment 等 impact noise，縮寫中的 I 應對應哪個詞？

A.gap: 混入 reverberation，誤解 NISR 的 rating 對象。
A.followup: Reverberation time 屬 S04；S07 的 NISR 是衡量 impact sound rating 還是 reverberation？

M07_Q41.stem: For all spaces except dwelling units, commercial kitchen spaces, and industrial buildings; category 2 represents what type of area?
M07_Q41.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels, Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels 的 note 將 Category 2 定義為 enclosed areas for concentration；personal offices 屬於此類，背景噪音 3 points 時 Average SPL 門檻為 40 dBA、Max SPL 為 50 dBA。

A.gap: 把 conferencing 或 speaking spaces 誤歸 Category 2。
A.followup: S02 note 中 Category 1 是 conferencing、learning 或 speaking；large conference rooms 應更接近哪一類？

D.gap: 把 dining areas 誤歸 enclosed concentration。
D.followup: S02 Part 1 note 明列 dining areas 在 Category 3，為什麼它不屬於 Category 2？

C.gap: 把 meeting room 的協作性誤當個人專注空間。
C.followup: Small meeting rooms 通常用於 conferencing；S02 的 Category 2 是否描述這種用途？

M07_Q42.stem: For dwelling units, what is the maximum average background noise level allowed over a 12-hour period?
M07_Q42.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels, Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels 對 dwelling units 要求 bedrooms 的 average background noise levels，在至少 12-hour measurement period 且包含 10 pm 到 7 am 時，不得超過 35 dBA (Leq)。

D.gap: 把非住宅 Category 1 平均值誤套到 dwelling bedroom。
D.followup: S02 對 dwelling units 是單獨列 bedroom 12-hour requirement，為什麼不能直接用其他 category 的 40 dBA？

B.gap: 把 dBC 或較高噪音上限誤當 bedroom 平均 dBA。
B.followup: 題目問 average background noise over 12 hours；S02 dwelling unit 條文指定的是 dBA 還是 dBC？

C.gap: 把最大噪音或 dBC 高值誤當允許平均值。
C.followup: 若 bedroom 夜間平均達 70 dBA，會如何違反 S02 Part 1 對睡眠時段的 35 dBA Leq 要求？

M07_Q43.stem: What is the intent of Feature S06: Minimum Background Sound?

options.text: To increase acoustical privacy within and between occupied spaces.
To design spaces with sound reducing surfaces.
To decrease acoustical privacy within and between occupied spaces.
To increase the level of sound isolation and speech privacy.

M07_Q43.explanation: S06 Minimum Background Sound, Part 1: Provide Minimum Background Sound 的 intent 是 increase acoustical privacy within and between occupied spaces；它透過 dedicated artificial sound 與 sound masking system 改善 speech privacy。

C.gap: 把增加 acoustic privacy 完全反向理解成降低。
C.followup: S06 使用 sound masking 增加 signal-to-noise ratio 時，目標是讓 speech privacy 上升還是下降？

B.gap: 把 S05 sound reducing surfaces 誤當 S06 intent。
B.followup: 設計吸音表面是 S05 的核心；S06 的 dedicated artificial sound 要解決哪種 privacy 問題？

D.gap: 混淆 S03 sound isolation 與 S06 background sound。
D.followup: S03 用 walls and doors 增加 isolation；S06 是用什麼系統在 occupied spaces 間增加 privacy？

M07_Q44.stem: According to Feature S02: Maximum Noise Levels, exterior noise can be controlled best by?

options.text: Once a noise baseline has been determined.
Considering at the earliest possible stages of design

M07_Q44.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels, Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels 的 solutions 指出 exterior noise 可由 façade、windows 與 exterior penetrations 的 sound reduction 控制，且這些 sources 在 earliest possible stages of design 被考量時最容易控制。

A.gap: 誤以為材料便宜即可控制 exterior noise。
A.followup: S02 強調 façade、windows 和 penetrations 的 sound reduction，是否把 inexpensive materials 當成主要判準？

B.gap: 把施工階段當成最佳控制時點，晚於設計整合。
B.followup: 若 exterior noise 到 construction phase 才處理，S02 說比 earliest design stage 更容易還是更困難？

C.gap: 把量測 baseline 誤當噪音控制最佳時機。
C.followup: noise baseline 有助了解現況，但 S02 的 solutions 說控制 exterior sources 應從哪個階段開始？

M07_Q45.stem: According to Part 1: Provide Enhanced Speech Intelligibility of Feature S08: Enhanced Audio Devices, public address systems must be commissioned by an audio engineer in accordance with NFPA 72 (Annex D) or equivalent. What does NFPA stand for?
M07_Q45.explanation: S08 Enhanced Audio Devices, Part 1: Provide Enhanced Speech Intelligibility 要求 public address systems 由 audio engineer 依 NFPA 72 (Annex D) 或 equivalent commissioning；NFPA 指 National Fire Protection Association。

A.gap: 把正式標準組織誤解成不存在的零件機構。
A.followup: S08 Part 1 引用 NFPA 72 作為 public address commissioning 依據，這種引用通常是正式 code organization 嗎？

C.gap: 把 fire protection code 誤解成 frequency process。
C.followup: NFPA 72 常見於 fire alarm 與 public address commissioning；哪個詞能對應 fire protection？

B.gap: 把協會名稱誤判成噪音演算法。
B.followup: S08 要求 audio engineer 依 NFPA 72 commissioning，這比較像 algorithm 還是標準制定協會？

M07_Q46.stem: The human response to mechanical vibrations through a medium is known as
M07_Q46.explanation: WELL Sound concept overview 將 sound 定義為 human response to mechanical vibrations through a medium, such as air；S01 Sound Mapping, Part 1: Label Acoustic Zones 進一步把這些 noise experiences 轉成 Loud、Quiet、Mixed 與 Circulation zones 的 planning requirement。

C.gap: 把物理振動形式誤當人類感知結果。
C.followup: Sound overview 強調 human response；waves 是媒介中的振動形式，和 occupant perception 有何差別？

D.gap: 把特定反射現象 echo 誤當 sound 定義。
D.followup: Echo 是聲音反射後的現象；題幹問 mechanical vibrations through a medium 的人類反應，範圍是否更廣？

B.gap: 把聲納技術誤認為基本聲音概念。
B.followup: Sonar 是利用聲波的技術；WELL Sound concept 定義的是技術工具還是人對振動的感知？

M07_Q47.stem: It is best to consult an audio engineering professional when...

options.text: Preparing to launch a company podcast.
Implementing audio systems to improve speech intelligibility.

M07_Q47.explanation: S08 Enhanced Audio Devices, Part 1: Provide Enhanced Speech Intelligibility 說 telecommunication、speech reinforcement 與 public address systems 應由 professional in audio engineering commissioning，以改善 speech intelligibility 並滿足多元 occupant needs。

C.gap: 把一般媒體製作誤當 WELL audio system commissioning。
C.followup: S08 關注的是 building occupants 的 speech intelligibility；company podcast launch 是否屬於這個 WELL requirement？

A.gap: 把噪音造成生產力問題泛化成 audio engineering 情境。
A.followup: Noise productivity issue 可能涉及 S02 或 S05；S08 特別何時需要 audio engineering professional？

B.gap: 把材料吸音選型與 audio system commissioning 混淆。
B.followup: Choosing sound dampening material 比較接近 S05 acoustical treatment；S08 的 professional 主要負責哪種系統？

M07_Q48.stem: What feature in the Sound Concept of the WELL Building Standard focuses on achieving desired ambient noise levels?
M07_Q48.explanation: S02 Maximum Noise Levels, Part 1: Limit Background Noise Levels 的 intent 是達成 desired ambient noise levels，避免 HVAC、exterior noise intrusion 或其他 noise sources 影響 occupant health and well-being；其表格設定各 category 的 Average SPL 與 Max SPL thresholds。

D.gap: 把吸音表面策略誤當 ambient noise levels 功能。
D.followup: S05 主要處理 reflected sound buildup；若題目問 maximum ambient background noise thresholds，應回到哪個 feature？

A.gap: 把 acoustic zoning planning 誤當噪音上限控制。
A.followup: S01 Label Acoustic Zones 是聲學規劃基礎，但 desired ambient noise levels 的 dBA/dBC 門檻在哪個 feature？

C.gap: 被錯字與 audio devices 混淆，偏離背景噪音。
C.followup: Enhanced Audio Devices 支援 speech intelligibility；它是否設定 HVAC 或 exterior noise intrusion 的 background SPL thresholds？

M07_Q49.stem: How many preconditions are there for the WELL Sound Concept?
M07_Q49.explanation: WELL Sound concept 的 feature list 中只有 S01 Sound Mapping 標示為 precondition；S01 包含 Part 1: Label Acoustic Zones 與 Part 2: Provide Acoustic Design Plan，作為 Sound concept 的 mandatory foundation。

D.gap: 把部分 optimization 誤算成 precondition。
D.followup: Sound feature list 中 S02 到 S06 多數標為 optimization；你能指出除了 S01 還有哪個標 P 嗎？

A.gap: 把多個聲學主題都誤認為 mandatory precondition。
A.followup: WELL precondition 是所有專案必做；Sound concept 裡哪些 feature code 旁邊實際標示為 P？

C.gap: 把 Sound concept 的 feature 數量誤當 precondition 數量。
C.followup: S01 到 S06 都是 Sound features，但 feature 數量和 precondition 數量是否相同？

M07_Q50.stem: If your project would like to receive points from Part 2: Provide Enhanced Speed Reduction of Feature SO6: Minimum Background Sound, what other feature or part can be used to achieve points?

options.text: Achieve two points in Feature S04 Part 1 – Achieve Reverberation Time Thresholds
Achieve one point in Feature S05 Part 1 – Implement Sound Reducing Surfaces
Feature S06 Part 1 – Provide Minimum Background Sound
Achieve one point in Feature S03 Part 2 - Achieve Sound Isolation

M07_Q50.explanation: S06 Minimum Background Sound, Part 2: Provide Enhanced Speech Reduction 要求達成 S06 Part 1: Provide Minimum Background Sound，並另外在 S03 Part 2 或 S05 Part 1 取得 two points；B 與 C 的 one point 敘述低於 requirement。

D.gap: 把 S03 Part 2 所需 two points 誤記為 one point。
D.followup: S06 Part 2 明文要求 Achieve two points in S03 Part 2；若只有 one point，是否滿足條件？

B.gap: 把 S05 Part 1 所需 two points 誤記為 one point。
B.followup: S06 Part 2 可用 S05 Part 1，但要求幾個 points？和選項中的 one point 有何差異？

A.gap: 把 S04 reverberation time 誤當 S06 Part 2 連動條件。
A.followup: S06 Part 2 的替代條件列出 S03 或 S05；S04 reverberation time 是否在該條文中出現？

M08_Q01.stem: According to Part 3: Restrict Lead of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit for added lead in drinking water pipes, fittings, and solder intended for drinking water distribution and delivery?

options.text: 0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added lead in newly-installed building materials
Products can contain no more than 300 ppm (0.3%) of lead by weight

M08_Q01.explanation: X01 Material Restrictions – Part 3 Restrict Lead 規定飲用水 distribution and delivery 的 pipes、fixtures、fittings 與 solder，需由政府或授權機構核准，或具第三方驗證的 weighted wetted average lead 不超過 0.25%，也可標示 ANSI/NSF 372-compliant。

A.gap: 誤以為 drinking water fittings 必須完全零 lead。
A.followup: X01 Part 3 對飲用水管件採用的是 no added lead，還是 weighted wetted average 的門檻？

C.gap: 把 0.25% 門檻錯記成更嚴格的 0.05%。
C.followup: X01 Part 3 的 ANSI/NSF 372-compliant 對 lead weighted wetted average 寫的是哪個百分比？

D.gap: 混淆 lead by weight 與 wetted average 兩種規則。
D.followup: 飲用水 pipes/fittings/solder 的 lead 要看 by weight，還是接觸水面的 weighted wetted average？

M08_Q02.stem: In order to comply with Part 3: Restrict Lead of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit of lead in all newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break switches?

options.text: Products can contain no more than 100 ppm (0.1%) of lead by weight
0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added lead in newly-installed building materials

M08_Q02.explanation: X01 Material Restrictions – Part 3 Restrict Lead 要求 newly installed fire alarms、meters、sensors、relays、thermostats 與 load break switches 符合 RoHS restrictions，或產品 lead by weight 不超過 0.01%（100 ppm）。

D.gap: 誤把電子設備 lead 限制理解成絕對零添加。
D.followup: X01 Part 3 對這些 electronics 是否允許 RoHS 或 100 ppm by weight 作為合規路徑？

A.gap: 把飲用水管件的 0.25% 規則套到電子產品。
A.followup: X01 Part 3 中 drinking water products 與 fire alarms/meters 的 lead 評估單位有何不同？

B.gap: 記得是百分比限制，但把 0.01% 誤成 0.05%。
B.followup: 若標準同時寫 0.01% 與 ppm，換算後 X01 Part 3 對 electronics 是多少 ppm？

M08_Q03.stem: According to Part 1: Restrict Asbestos of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit of asbestos for acoustic treatments, joint protection, and ceiling product categories?

options.text: 0.25% weighted wetted average for all product categories
Product categories cannot contain over 1,000 ppm of asbestos by weight or area
0.05% weighted wetted average for all products categories
0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added asbestos in newly-installed or applied products within the project boundary

M08_Q03.explanation: X01 Material Restrictions – Part 1 Restrict Asbestos 規定 newly installed or applied products 中，acoustic treatments、joint protection、ceilings 等列名 product categories 的 asbestos 不得超過 1,000 ppm by weight or area。

D.gap: 把 asbestos 限制誤解成所有新產品完全零容忍。
D.followup: X01 Part 1 的 product categories 清單後面接的是 no asbestos，還是 not over 1,000 ppm？

A.gap: 混入 lead plumbing 的 weighted average 概念。
A.followup: Asbestos 的限制是 weighted wetted average，還是依 product category 的 by weight or area ppm？

C.gap: 同時混淆 weighted average 與 asbestos 門檻數值。
C.followup: X01 Part 1 用來判定 acoustic treatments 的單位，是百分比 weighted average 還是 ppm by weight or area？

M08_Q04.stem: To comply with Part 2: Restrict Mercury of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit of mercury for newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break switches?

options.text: 0.25% weighted wetted average for all product categories
Products contain no more than 0.1% (1000 ppm) of mercury by weight
0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added mercury in newly-installed building materials
0.05% weighted wetted average for all products categories

M08_Q04.explanation: X01 Material Restrictions – Part 2 Restrict Mercury 要求 newly installed fire alarms、meters、sensors、relays、thermostats 與 load break switches 符合 RoHS restrictions，或 mercury by weight 不超過 0.1%（1000 ppm）。

C.gap: 誤認 mercury 在這些設備中完全不得檢出。
C.followup: X01 Part 2 對 fire alarms/meters 是寫 no mercury，還是允許 RoHS 或 0.1% by weight？

D.gap: 把 lead wetted average 的錯誤百分比套到 mercury。
D.followup: X01 Part 2 的 mercury 限制是 weighted wetted average，還是產品重量百分比？

A.gap: 把 drinking water lead 的 0.25% 門檻混用到 mercury。
A.followup: 同在 X01 中，Part 2 mercury 與 Part 3 drinking water lead 的百分比門檻分別是多少？

M08_Q05.stem: To comply with Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management, a project manager who is overseeing a museum renovation needs to manage exposure risks for asbestos and lead. What other hazardous material must be managed if it is present in the building?
M08_Q05.explanation: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management 的 Summary 指明此 Feature 管理 hazardous building materials 的 exposure risks，包括 asbestos、lead 與 polychlorinated biphenyls（PCBs）。

A.gap: 把 X01 的 mercury restriction 誤放進 X02 管理範圍。
A.followup: X02 Summary 列出的三類 legacy hazardous building materials，除了 asbestos 與 lead 還有哪一類？

C.gap: 把一般材料健康疑慮 PVC 誤認為 X02 指定危害。
C.followup: X02 是針對既有建築的 asbestos、lead、PCBs，還是所有塑膠材料類別？

B.gap: 混淆 formaldehyde/VOC 風險與 X02 legacy hazards。
B.followup: Formaldehyde 更常出現在 VOC 或材料排放要求中；X02 Part 3 管的是哪種舊建材污染物？

M08_Q06.stem: To comply with Part 1: Manage Asbestos Hazards of Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management, an investigation of the project space must be conducted by an inspector certified under local regulation or a qualified professional with demonstrable experience where no local regulations apply. At a minimum, what discovery from the investigation must they provide?

options.text: Confirmation of the presence of asbestos is performed through Polarized Light Microscopy (PLM) or Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) testing.
A building permit waiver from the local municipality for the limited use of asbestos
A list of locations where mercury and formaldehyde are located

M08_Q06.explanation: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management – Part 1 Manage Asbestos Hazards 規定 asbestos investigation 至少需列出 PACM locations，並以 Polarized Light Microscopy（PLM）或 Transmission Electron Microscopy（TEM）確認 asbestos presence；材料超過 1% asbestos 視為 ACM。

C.gap: 把一般 SDS 文件誤當成 asbestos 現場調查證據。
C.followup: X02 Part 1 要求 inspector 調查時，是否需要實驗方法確認 PACM，而不只是 MSDS？

D.gap: 把 mercury/formaldehyde 清單錯當 asbestos hazard 調查。
D.followup: X02 Part 1 的調查對象是 asbestos-containing materials，需列出的是哪些 presumed material locations？

B.gap: 誤以為可用許可豁免取代 asbestos 調查與確認。
B.followup: X02 Part 1 允許用 municipal waiver 合規，還是要求 certified inspector/qualified professional 調查？

M08_Q07.stem: To comply with Part 1: Manage Pests of Feature X10: Pest Management and Pesticide Use, which of these elements must be included with a management plan for pest control that is based on integrated pest management (IPM) principles?

options.text: List of identified pests accessible to all occupants
Pest management flow chart accessible to all occupants
Records of pest tolerance thresholds with data supporting pesticide application
Records of pest monitoring data, pest events, pesticide applications, control actions and emergency responses

M08_Q07.explanation: X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use – Part 1 Manage Pests 要求 IPM management plan 包含 roles/responsibilities、objectives、prevention measures、pest tolerance thresholds/control strategies，以及 pest monitoring data、pest events、pesticide applications、control actions 與 emergency responses 的 records。

C.gap: 只抓到 tolerance thresholds，漏掉完整 IPM records 要求。
C.followup: X10 Part 1 的 plan records 是否只支持 pesticide application，還要涵蓋 pest events 與 emergency responses？

B.gap: 把公開流程圖誤認為標準列明的 IPM plan 元素。
B.followup: X10 Part 1 明列的 occupant-facing 要求是 notification/SDS 可得，還是 flow chart accessible？

A.gap: 只想到 pest identification，忽略 plan records 與控制策略。
A.followup: X10 Part 1 的 objectives 包含 pest identification，但 plan 還要求哪些 monitoring 與 application records？

M08_Q08.stem: According to Part 3: Manage Polychlorinated Biphenyl (PCB) Hazards of Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management, if the caulk is to be disturbed or removed, analysis of the presumably PCB-containing material must follow protocols. How are the protocols mandated?

options.text: By state legislative representatives or by specific US EPA or ISO testing methods
By local laws or, in absence of local laws, by any applicable US EPA or ISO testing methods

M08_Q08.explanation: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management – Part 3 Manage Polychlorinated Biphenyl（PCB）Hazards 規定若 caulk 將被 disturbed or removed，presumably PCB-containing material 的分析須遵循 local laws mandated protocols；若無 local laws，則採 applicable US EPA 或 ISO testing methods。
