A.gap: 把合規文件 assurance letter 誤當 PCB testing protocol。
A.followup: X02 Part 3 針對 caulk 分析要求的是驗證信，還是由 local laws/EPA/ISO 定義的測試方法？

B.gap: 以 technical document 取代實際 mandated testing protocols。
B.followup: PCB-containing caulk 的分析是否只需技術文件，或必須按照 X02 Part 3 指定的 protocol 來源？

C.gap: 知道 EPA/ISO，卻誤加入 state representatives 來源。
C.followup: X02 Part 3 對 protocol 的第一順位是 state representatives，還是 local laws？

M08_Q09.stem: According to Part 1: Manage Exterior CCA H azards of Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management, if the exterior of a structure was built before laws instituting restrictions on the use of CCA (Chromium Copper Arsenate) and it's found, what can the project team do to comply?

options.text: Replace all wood structures containing CCA in accordance with the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
Project team can choose to remediate only CCA infused materials that are within the project boundary where human presence is expected
Dispose of CCA-containing woods following applicable laws, without incinerating nor wood chipping
Provide a verified letter of assurance so wood structures containing CCA can be left in place

M08_Q09.explanation: X03 CCA and Lead Management – Part 1 Manage Exterior CCA Hazards 要求識別 CCA-containing wood 後，以兩種方式之一處理：依 applicable laws disposal，且不得 incinerating 或 wood chipping；或定期使用 penetrating, oil-based, semi-transparent stains 防止 arsenic leaching。

D.gap: 誤以為 assurance letter 可讓 CCA wood 原樣保留。
D.followup: X03 Part 1 在 identify CCA 後，是否允許只靠 letter，還是要 disposal 或 protective stain treatment？

A.gap: 把必須全部替換誤當成唯一合規處置方式。
A.followup: X03 Part 1 Address CCA-containing woods 明列的兩條路徑中，除了 disposal 還有哪一種 treatment？

B.gap: 忽略 Part 1 已限定 project boundary 與 expected human presence。
B.followup: X03 Part 1 的適用範圍是否已包含 human presence 條件，處置時還能自行只挑部分材料嗎？

M08_Q10.stem: According Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management, when is a lead assessment not applicable for a project undergoing WELL v2 Certification??

options.text: Project does not have artificial turf.
Project has accounted for heat island effect.
Project has loose-fill rubber from recycled tires present on playgrounds.
Project has paint peeling on existing playground equipment.

M08_Q10.explanation: X03 CCA and Lead Management – Part 2 Manage Lead Hazards 針對 outdoor bare soil、artificial turf fibers、loose-fill rubber from recycled tires 與 existing playground equipment paint 進行評估；若專案沒有 artificial turf，就不適用 synthetic turf fibers 的 lead assessment。

B.gap: 把 heat island effect 與 lead hazard assessment 混淆。
B.followup: X03 Part 2 的 lead hazards 清單是否包含 heat island effect，還是 soil、turf、rubber 與 playground paint？

C.gap: loose-fill recycled rubber 仍屬需評估的 lead hazard。
C.followup: X03 Part 2 對 recycled tire loose-fill rubber 設了什麼 lead testing/remediation 條件？

D.gap: peeling playground paint 正是需取樣評估的情境。
D.followup: X03 Part 2 對 cracked、peeled 或 chipped playground equipment paint 要求做什麼 lead analysis？

M08_Q11.stem: A project has conducted a wipe test for lead loadings of synthetic turf fibers, if found what is the µg/ft2 limit for lead that will make this remediated according to Part 2: Manage Lead Hazards of Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management?

options.text: If the wipe-testing results show lead loadings greater than 80 µg/ft2 replace with turf containing lead concentrations less than 300 mg/kg it must be remediated
If the wipe-testing results show lead loadings greater than 40 µg/ft2 replace with turf containing lead concentrations less than 136 mg/kg it must be remediated
If the wipe-testing results show lead loadings greater than 140 µg/ft2 replace with turf containing lead concentrations less than 136 mg/kg it must be remediated

M08_Q11.explanation: X03 CCA and Lead Management – Part 2 Manage Lead Hazards 規定 synthetic turf fibers 若 total lead concentration 超過 136 mg/lb（300 mg/kg），需做 dust-wipe testing；若 surface dust-lead loading 超過 40 µg/ft2（430 µg/m2），則更換為 lead concentration 低於 136 mg/lb（300 mg/kg）的 turf。

C.gap: 忽略 synthetic turf lead 未知或超標時需 wipe testing。
C.followup: X03 Part 2 在 turf fibers lead concentration 超過 136 mg/lb 後，下一步是否要做 dust-wipe testing？

D.gap: 把 dust wipe threshold 誤記為 140 µg/ft2。
D.followup: X03 Part 2 對 surface dust-lead loading 的觸發值是 40 還是 140 µg/ft2？

A.gap: 同時錯記 wipe threshold 與 replacement turf 濃度。
A.followup: X03 Part 2 要求 replacement turf 的 lead concentration 低於 136 mg/lb，這與 300 mg/kg 如何對應？

M08_Q12.stem: A WELL project is assessing the paint on existing playground equipment. What percentage of lead, if found, will require the abatement process?
M08_Q12.explanation: X03 CCA and Lead Management – Part 2 Manage Lead Hazards 要求 cracked、peeled 或 chipped 的 existing playground equipment paint 取樣做 lead laboratory analysis；若 sample lead concentration over 90 ppm，需 remove or encapsulate paint。

A.gap: 把 playground paint 的 lead trigger 大幅低估。
A.followup: X03 Part 2 對 playground equipment paint 的 removal/encapsulation 門檻，是不是明寫 over 90 ppm？

D.gap: 接近但仍低於標準列出的 90 ppm 門檻。
D.followup: 如果樣本是 60 ppm，依 X03 Part 2 是否已達 remove or encapsulate 的觸發值？

C.gap: 把門檻放得過高，會漏掉 90 ppm 以上危害。
C.followup: X03 Part 2 使用 over 90 ppm 作為觸發值，若等到 120 ppm 才處理會少涵蓋哪些情況？

M08_Q13.stem: Lead is an existing external hazard that must be identified and remediated on a project. Where may lead be present on a project site?

options.text: LED lighting used throughout a project.
Paints used on outdoor structures and playgrounds.
Flooring used in a commercial kitchen.

M08_Q13.explanation: X03 CCA and Lead Management 的 Issue 指出 existing external hazards 中 lead may be present in paints used on outdoor structures and playgrounds；Part 2 Manage Lead Hazards 也要求評估 existing playground equipment paint、soil、artificial turf 與 loose-fill rubber。

D.gap: 把室內 furniture 誤當 X03 exterior lead hazard。
D.followup: X03 的 lead hazards 是室內家具材料，還是 project site 的 outdoor structures、playgrounds、soil 與 turf？

C.gap: 把室內 commercial kitchen flooring 與外部 lead hazard 混淆。
C.followup: X03 Part 2 對 flooring 是否有 lead paint assessment，還是聚焦 outdoor bare soil 與 playground equipment？

A.gap: 把 lighting 系統誤認為外部 lead paint 來源。
A.followup: X03 Issue 提到 lead 可能存在於哪些 outdoor structures 或 playground materials，而非 LED lighting？

M08_Q14.stem: The requirement for managing product emissions of newly applied adhesives, sealants, paints, and coatings to meet the California Department of Public Health (CDPH) Standard Method v1.2 can be found in which WELL Feature?

options.text: Feature: X11 Cleaning Products and Protocols
Feature: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management

M08_Q14.explanation: X06 VOC Restrictions – Part 1 Limit VOCs from Wet-Applied Products 要求 newly installed interior paints、coatings、adhesives、sealants 與 finished poured floorings 的 VOC emissions，至少 75% of products by surface area or volume 需符合 CDPH Standard Method v1.2、AgBB 或 EU LCI/EN 16516 等標準。

C.gap: 把 hazardous legacy materials 管理誤認為 VOC emissions。
C.followup: CDPH Standard Method v1.2 在 WELL v2 中是 X02 的 asbestos/lead/PCB 管理，還是 X06 的 VOC emissions？

A.gap: 把 cleaning protocols 與 wet-applied product emissions 混淆。
A.followup: X11 管 cleaning products/protocols；新塗料與 sealants 的 CDPH emissions 要回到哪個 Feature？

B.gap: 把 X01 材料成分限制誤當低 VOC 排放要求。
B.followup: X01 restricts asbestos、mercury、lead；CDPH Method v1.2 是限制這些元素，還是評估 VOC emissions？

M08_Q15.stem: In Feature X06: VOC Restrictions, if a project is in full compliance of both product categories, by Cost or Surface Area, how many points could they achieve?
M08_Q15.explanation: X06 VOC Restrictions – Part 2 Restrict VOC Emissions from Furniture, Architectural and Interior Products 的 Table 2 規定 one compliant product category 得 1 point，at least two compliant product categories 得 2 points；題目說 both product categories full compliance，因此可取得 2 points。

B.gap: 誤以為 VOC category compliance 不給分。
B.followup: X06 Part 2 Table 2 中，一個 compliant product category 是否已能取得 1 point？

D.gap: 只計入一個 compliant product category 的分數。
D.followup: 題幹說 both product categories full compliance，X06 Part 2 對 at least two categories 給幾分？

C.gap: 高估 Part 2 product category compliance 的最高分。
C.followup: X06 Part 2 Table 2 的 product category 達成分數上限是 2 points 還是 3 points？

M08_Q16.stem: Which of the following standards establishes limits for the VOC content of furniture for Feature X06: VOC Restrictions?  CDPH  ANSI/BIFMA  SCAQMD  SMACNA
M08_Q16.explanation: X06 VOC Restrictions – Part 2 Restrict VOC Emissions from Furniture, Architectural and Interior Products 列出 compliance methods，包括 CDPH Standard Method v1.2、AgBB、EU LCI/EN 16516，以及 furniture 專用的 ANSI/BIFMA e3-2014 sections 7.6.1 or 7.6.2；SCAQMD 是 Part 1 wet-applied VOC content，SMACNA 不在此清單。

A.gap: 把 SCAQMD wet-applied content 標準誤當家具 VOC emissions。
A.followup: X06 Part 2 的 furniture compliance 清單中，有 SCAQMD Rule 1168，還是它出現在 Part 1？

B.gap: 混入 SCAQMD 與 SMACNA，漏掉 ANSI/BIFMA。
B.followup: 題幹問 furniture VOC content/emissions；X06 Part 2 是否列出 SMACNA，或列出 ANSI/BIFMA e3-2014？

C.gap: 知道 ANSI/BIFMA，卻錯把 SCAQMD 也列為家具標準。
C.followup: SCAQMD Rule 1168 在 X06 是用於 adhesives/sealants 的 VOC content，還是 furniture category？

M08_Q17.stem: A Fortune 500 company is leasing 5 floors of an existing building. The Business Manager has approved new desks and chairs for 62 employees on one of the floors. The VOC content of at least what percentage (by cost) of the new desks and chairs must meet ANSI/BIFMA e3-2014?
M08_Q17.explanation: X06 VOC Restrictions – Part 2 Restrict VOC Emissions from Furniture, Architectural and Interior Products 的 Table 1 對 Furniture, millwork and fixtures 設定 threshold for compliance 為 75% by cost，且可用 ANSI/BIFMA e3-2014 sections 7.6.1 or 7.6.2 作為測試方法。

D.gap: 大幅低估 furniture by cost 的 VOC compliance threshold。
D.followup: X06 Part 2 Table 1 對 furniture, millwork and fixtures 的 threshold 是 10% 還是 75% by cost？

C.gap: 把材料比例門檻誤記為較低的 30%。
C.followup: 若只有 30% desks and chairs 符合 ANSI/BIFMA，是否達到 X06 Part 2 的 furniture threshold？

A.gap: 把 50% 常見採購門檻誤套到 VOC furniture。
A.followup: X06 Part 2 Table 1 中，furniture, millwork and fixtures 是 50% by cost 還是 75% by cost？

M08_Q18.stem: The presence of pests in indoor environments can have negative health implications for humans. While pesticides can manage insects and rodents, they also bring with them inherent toxicity. What childhood disease have studies shown an increase as a result of pesticide exposure?

options.text: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease in children.

M08_Q18.explanation: X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use 的 Issue 指出 exposure to specific pesticides may present danger to human health，研究顯示 increased likelihood of cancer in children、breast cancer，以及 maternal exposure 相關 birth defects。

B.gap: 把 pesticide exposure 影響誤連到兒童 COPD。
B.followup: X10 Issue 舉例的 pesticide health evidence 是 childhood COPD，還是 increased likelihood of cancer in children？

A.gap: 將一般不適疲勞誤當標準明列疾病。
A.followup: X10 Issue 中 studies shown increase 的 childhood outcome 是 fatigue，還是更嚴重的 cancer risk？

C.gap: 把常見室內環境疾病 asthma 誤當 pesticide evidence。
C.followup: 雖然 pests 與室內環境可影響健康，X10 Issue 具體提到 pesticide exposure 增加哪種 childhood disease？

M08_Q19.stem: An integrated pest management plan (IPM) is a way to limit the use of pesticides in and around buildings. If there is any pesticide application within the project, what must be available to occupants and owners?
M08_Q19.explanation: X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use – Part 1 Manage Pests 規定若有 pesticide application，plan provisions 包括 occupant notification；並且 plan、implementation records、Safety Data Sheets（SDSs）of pesticides 與 inspection results 都必須 available to occupants and owners。

C.gap: 把 pest evidence 照片誤認為 occupants/owners 可得文件。
C.followup: X10 Part 1 要向 occupants and owners 提供的是 pest photos，還是 pesticide SDSs 與 plan records？

B.gap: 混淆 pests 資訊與 pesticide 安全資料要求。
B.followup: 如果有 pesticide application，X10 Part 1 要求可取得的是 pests data sheets 還是 Safety Data Sheets？

A.gap: 把 pest identification 細節誤當 pesticide disclosure。
A.followup: X10 Part 1 的 notification 要包含 pesticide name、registration number 等；scientific name of pests 是否足夠？

M08_Q20.stem: The phthalates (plasticizers) DEHP, DBP, BBP, DINP, DIDP, and DNOP are limited to 0.01% (100 ppm) in flooring and wall coverings according to which of the following features?

options.text: Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management

M08_Q20.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions – Part 2 Select Compliant Architectural and Interior Products 規定至少 50% by cost 的指定產品類別中，flooring products 的 HFR、PFAS 與 orthophthalates，以及 ceiling/wall panels 的 HFR 與 orthophthalates，需為 100 ppm（0.01%）by weight or less。

C.gap: 把 optimization 類第三方化學評估誤當 restrictions。
C.followup: 100 ppm orthophthalates 限制是在 X05 Part 2 的產品成分類門檻，還是 X08 Materials Optimization？

B.gap: 把 plasticizer 成分限制誤認為 VOC emissions/content。
B.followup: X06 管 VOC emissions；DEHP、DBP、BBP 等 phthalates 的 100 ppm limit 是哪個 X05 Part？

D.gap: 把既有 hazardous materials 管理誤放到新產品限制。
D.followup: X02 管 asbestos、lead、PCBs 既有風險；flooring/wall covering phthalates threshold 是否屬 X05？

M08_Q21.stem: Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions gives 1 point if, at minimum, 50% by cost of newly installed furniture, millwork, and fixtures contain halogenated flame retardants at less than _____?
M08_Q21.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions – Part 1 Select Compliant Interior Furnishings 規定至少 50% by cost 的 newly installed furniture, millwork and fixtures，其 textiles 與 plastics 中 halogenated flame retardants（HFR）等列名化學類別需為 100 ppm（0.01%）by weight or less。

C.gap: 把 HFR threshold 誤記得比標準更嚴格。
C.followup: X05 Part 1 對 textiles/plastics 的 HFR threshold 是 10 ppm，還是 100 ppm by weight？

B.gap: 把 100 ppm 門檻放寬成 1000 ppm。
B.followup: 若 HFR 為 1000 ppm，是否仍符合 X05 Part 1 的 100 ppm（0.01%）要求？

D.gap: 大幅放寬 HFR 限值，忽略 0.01% 要求。
D.followup: X05 Part 1 將 HFR 限在 0.01% by weight，這換算為多少 ppm？

M08_Q22.stem: Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions highlights the concerns of orthophthlates, halogenated flame retardants and ______?
M08_Q22.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions 的 Issue 說明本 Feature 關注 orthophthalates、halogenated flame retardants（HFR）以及 per-fluorinated compounds（PFCs）等材料中具健康疑慮的 chemical classes；Part 1/2 進一步以 100 ppm（0.01%）門檻限制 HFR、PFAS/相關 fluorinated compounds 等。

C.gap: 把 X01/X03 的 lead hazard 混入 X05 主要化學類別。
C.followup: X05 Issue 提到 orthophthalates、HFR 與哪一類 fluorinated compounds，而不是 lead？

A.gap: 把 CCA legacy exterior wood hazard 誤當 X05 化學關注。
A.followup: CCA 是 X03 的 existing wood structures 風險；X05 關注的新產品 chemical classes 包含什麼？

D.gap: 將 water/營養相關 nitrate 誤認為材料限制化學。
D.followup: X05 Issue 列的是 building products 中的 chemical classes，nitrate 是否出現在這個清單？

M08_Q23.stem: Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions sets a standard for textiles and plastics in furniture. We also see a standard set for electrical and electronic products. What must newly installed electrical and electronic products must be compliant to?
M08_Q23.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions – Part 1 Select Compliant Interior Furnishings 的 Option 2 Electrical and electronic products 要求 Appendix X1 指定的 all newly installed electrical and electronic products compliant with RoHS restrictions。

C.gap: 把 thermal comfort 標準 ASHRAE 55 誤用於 electronics。
C.followup: X05 Part 1 Option 2 管 electrical/electronic products，是否會引用 ASHRAE 55？

A.gap: 把 adhesive/sealant VOC content 標準誤套到電子產品。
A.followup: SCAQMD Rule 1168 在 X06 wet-applied products 中使用；X05 electronics 要符合哪個 restrictions？

D.gap: 混淆材料健康認證與指定 electronics restriction。
D.followup: X05 Part 1 對 newly installed electrical and electronic products 是要求 Cradle to Cradle，還是 RoHS restrictions？

M08_Q24.stem: Which of the following chemicals found in Textiles and Plastics must be limited to a maximum of 100 ppm in newly installed furniture, millwork, and fixtures, according to Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions?
M08_Q24.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions – Part 1 Select Compliant Interior Furnishings 規定至少 50% by cost 的 newly installed furniture, millwork and fixtures，其 textiles 與 plastics 中 halogenated flame retardants（HFR）、PFAS、lead、cadmium、mercury 等需為 100 ppm（0.01%）by weight or less。

B.gap: 把 X01 asbestos restriction 混入 X05 textiles/plastics 清單。
B.followup: X05 Part 1 對 furniture textiles/plastics 的 100 ppm 清單是否包含 asbestos？

C.gap: 把 X10 pesticide 管理誤認為 furniture chemical threshold。
C.followup: Pesticides 屬 X10 pest management；X05 Part 1 中 textiles/plastics 明列哪些 restricted chemical classes？

D.gap: 把 formaldehyde 材料排放議題誤當 X05 100 ppm 項目。
D.followup: X05 Part 1 的 100 ppm list 包含 HFR、PFAS 與重金屬；formaldehyde 是否在這個 list？

M08_Q25.stem: Which of the following is NOT an acceptable third-party verification that demonstrates Part 3: Select Products with Third-Party Verified Ingredients of Feature X07: Materials Transparency?

options.text: Cradle to Cradle™ Material Health Certificate

M08_Q25.explanation: X07 Materials Transparency – Part 3 Select Products with Third-Party Verified Ingredients 要求至少 15 distinct permanently installed products/furniture 的 ingredients 透過 Declare label、HPD Public Repository、Cradle-to-Cradle Gold/Platinum Material Health、Product Lens Certification 或 Product Health Declaration 等方式 disclosed，且 ingredient disclosure 需由第三方 verified；GreenScreen Benchmark 不在 Part 3 的可接受 disclosure 清單。

B.gap: 誤排除 Part 3 明列的 Cradle-to-Cradle Material Health。
B.followup: X07 Part 3 是否列出 Cradle-to-Cradle Gold/Platinum Material Health Certificate 作為 disclosure 路徑？

A.gap: 誤以為 HPD 不是可接受的 ingredient disclosure。
A.followup: X07 Part 3 對 HPD 的要求是 published in HPD Public Repository，這是否列在可接受清單？

C.gap: 把 Declare Label 錯排除，忽略 ILFI disclosure 路徑。
C.followup: X07 Part 3 的第一項是否就是 International Living Future Institute operated Declare label？

M08_Q26.stem: In Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions, how much Halogenated flame retardants (HFR) can be found in the textiles and plastics in newly installed furniture, millwork, and fixtures?
M08_Q26.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions, Part 1 Select Compliant Interior Furnishings 要求 textiles 與 plastics 中 HFR、PFAS、lead、cadmium、mercury 等化學類別各自不得超過 100 ppm (0.01%) by weight，除非 local code 要求更高含量。

B.gap: 誤把 X05 的 HFR 門檻記得過嚴，忽略 100 ppm。
B.followup: X05 Part 1 明列的 HFR 上限是多少 ppm？是否另有更低值？

A.gap: 低估 HFR 限制強度，誤以為數百 ppm 仍可合規。
A.followup: 若 X05 Part 1 明列 HFR 為受限 chemical class，允許值是否能高到 450 ppm？

D.gap: 選了未出現在標準中的任意數字，未對照 0.01% 換算。
D.followup: 100 ppm 與 0.01% by weight 是同一門檻；55 ppm 在 X05 Part 1 有被列為要求嗎？

M08_Q27.stem: In Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions, (max 1 Point), newly installed furnishings, built-in furniture, interior finishes, and finish materials must comply by at least what percent compliance by cost to fulfill the requirement?
M08_Q27.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions, Part 1 Select Compliant Interior Furnishings 要求至少 50% by cost 的 newly installed furniture, millwork and fixtures，且至少 10 distinct products，符合材料限制或不含 textiles and plastic。

C.gap: 低估 X05 by cost 門檻，混入其他材料比例。
C.followup: X05 Part 1 合規比例是 50% by cost，還是較低比例？

B.gap: 選了接近但不存在的比例，未抓到標準採整數 50% 門檻。
B.followup: 題幹問 Part 1 的 minimum compliance by cost，標準是否允許 45% 這種中間值？

D.gap: 把要求誤加嚴，將最低門檻提高到標準未要求的比例。
D.followup: 若 WELL 只要求 at least 50% by cost，55% 是合規成果還是最低要求？

M08_Q28.stem: Which of the following WELL features promotes the use of products that have been independently verified to be free of many suspected hazards?

options.text: Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management

M08_Q28.explanation: WELL v2 的相近條文是 X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions, Part 1/Part 2，要求限制 HFR、PFAS、heavy metals、orthophthalates 等成分；但「independently verified」與「free of many suspected hazards」更貼近 X08 Materials Optimization 的篩選與認證邏輯。

C.gap: 把基礎 hazardous materials 禁限用與進階產品驗證混為一談。
C.followup: X01 是限制 asbestos、mercury、lead，還是要求產品經第三方材料健康優化認證？

A.gap: 把既有場地風險管理誤當產品成分驗證要求。
A.followup: X03 管 CCA 與 lead 場地風險，是否驗證新產品成分？

D.gap: 把感染控制策略誤當材料化學危害篩選要求。
D.followup: X12 處理 respiratory particles 與 hand touch，和材料 hazards 有何不同？

M08_Q29.stem: An urban development has recently completed construction, and new tenants are moving in. The project team is tabulating all products (including furnishings, furniture, and finishes) to report for Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions. What metric should the project team use to report compliance?
M08_Q29.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions, Part 1 與 Part 2 都以 at least 50% by cost of newly installed products 作為合規比例；ppm by weight 是成分濃度門檻，不是整體產品盤點的回報 metric。

C.gap: 混淆產品合規比例與化學濃度測量，誤以為兩種 metric 可任選。
C.followup: X05 的 100 ppm by weight 用於材料成分；50% compliance 是按哪個採購或產品統計基準？

D.gap: 把 ppm by weight 誤作產品盤點 metric。
D.followup: X05 盤點產品合規時，標準寫 by weight 還是 by cost？

A.gap: 使用標準未採用的 volume metric，忽略成本比例。
A.followup: X05 Part 2 的產品合規比例，有沒有使用 volume 作為基準？

M08_Q30.stem: Feature X11: Cleaning Products and Protocols requires a project develops and implements a cleaning plan. What must this plan detail?

options.text: The time each cleaning function takes to complete
Green cleaning products from regular cleaning products

M08_Q30.explanation: X11 Cleaning Products and Protocols, Part 1 Improve Cleaning Practices 要求 cleaning plan 詳列 extent and frequency of cleaning、清潔責任、可用 supplies 與 adherence documentation process。

C.gap: 把 cleaning plan 縮小成單一清掃操作，漏掉完整管理項目。
C.followup: X11 Part 1 要 detail 的是 sweeping 指令，還是整體清潔範圍、頻率與責任分工？

A.gap: 把清潔工時誤當 WELL 要求，漏看頻率與責任。
A.followup: X11 Part 1 要記錄 cleaning frequency，是否要求逐項工時？

B.gap: 混淆 cleaning plan 與 preferred products。
B.followup: 題目問 cleaning plan，應先看 X11 Part 1 還是 Part 2？

M08_Q31.stem: According to Part 1: Improve Cleaning Practices of Feature X11: Cleaning Products and Protocols in the WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™), the cleaning plan must outline a training program. Who is the training delivered to?

options.text: Management staff and board of directors

M08_Q31.explanation: X11 Cleaning Products and Protocols, Part 1 Improve Cleaning Practices 要求 training delivered to all relevant personnel，包括 building management、building operators 與 contracted cleaning staff，且每年及 protocol 改變時提供。

C.gap: 把溝通對象誤當訓練對象，漏掉清潔相關人員。
C.followup: X11 training 是給 regular occupants，還是執行清潔的相關人員？

B.gap: 把 relevant personnel 限縮成全職員工。
B.followup: 若只訓練 full-time employees，contracted cleaning staff 會不會被漏掉？

A.gap: 誤以為訓練只需高層治理，忽略現場操作人員。
A.followup: X11 的 PPE 與 cloth handling training 應由董事會還是現場人員掌握？

M08_Q32.stem: A janitorial company is beginning work at a WELL Certified building, and several requirements related to the WELL Building Standard are written into the contract. Since the project has achieved Feature X11: Cleaning Products and Protocols, which of the following requirements must be met?

options.text: Mops, rags and dusters used to clean all non-porous surfaces must consist of microfiber with a denier no higher than 1.5.
Walk-off mats should be wet cleaned every day.
Cleaning products, soaps, shampoos, disinfection and sanitization products are not manufactured with ingredients classified under the Globally Harmonized System (GHS).

M08_Q32.explanation: X11 Cleaning Products and Protocols, Part 2 Select Preferred Cleaning Products 要求 cleaning、disinfection and sanitization products 採 low-hazard/safer 認證，或 SDS 依 EU CLP/California SB 258 揭露且 Section 3 不含特定 Category 1、1A、1B GHS hazard codes。

C.gap: 把清潔頻率想成固定每日濕洗，非明文要求。
C.followup: X11 Part 1 要說明 extent and frequency，有指定 mats 每日濕洗嗎？

A.gap: 套用過細的 microfiber 規格，忽略實際條文。
A.followup: X11 對 reusable cloths 要 color-coding，是否列出 denier 1.5？

B.gap: 混淆 HVAC 濾網等級與 vacuum cleaner 要求。
B.followup: X11 對 vacuum cleaners 寫 HEPA rated filters，是否是 MERV 8？

M08_Q33.stem: Part 2: Select Compliant Architectural and Interior Products in Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions gives 1 point if, at minimum, 50% by cost of flooring components contain halogenated flame retardants at less than
M08_Q33.explanation: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions, Part 2 Select Compliant Architectural and Interior Products 要求至少 50% by cost 的 flooring products 中 HFR、PFAS 與 orthophthalates 各為 100 ppm (0.01%) by weight or less。

C.gap: 把 X05 Part 2 門檻誤記為更嚴格的 10 ppm。
C.followup: X05 Part 2 對 flooring HFR 的 ppm 上限是多少？

B.gap: 混淆 X01 的 1000 ppm 與 X05 的 HFR 門檻。
B.followup: 1000 ppm 常見於 legacy restrictions，X05 HFR 是多少 ppm？

A.gap: 大幅放寬 HFR 限制，未掌握 0.01% by weight 的換算。
A.followup: 如果 100 ppm 等於 0.01%，2000 ppm 會比 X05 Part 2 允許值高出多少倍？

M08_Q34.stem: Feature X07: Material Transparency includes Part 3: Select Products with Third-Party Verified Ingredients. What verification method is required through IWBI?
M08_Q34.explanation: X07 Materials Transparency, Part 3 Select Products with Third-Party Verified Ingredients 要求至少 15 distinct permanently installed products and furniture 披露全部 ingredients，且 disclosure 由第三方驗證；這類 ingredient disclosure 與 certification evidence 通常以 Technical Document 提交。

C.gap: 誤以為工程師保證書可取代成分揭露完整證據。
C.followup: X07 Part 3 要第三方驗證成分，Engineer letter 能證明產品證書嗎？

A.gap: 把現場照片誤當材料透明度文件主要證據來源。
A.followup: On-site Photos 能顯示安裝，但能否證明 Declare 或 HPD 成分資料？

D.gap: 混淆一次性產品文件審查與營運期持續報告。
D.followup: X07 Part 3 是要提交 product ingredient documentation，還是每年回報運轉數據？

M08_Q35.stem: In order to achieve optimization Feature X07: Materials Transparency, at least 50% by count or 25 distinct, permanently installed products and furniture ingredients are disclosed by the manufacturer, a disclosure organization, or a third party through which of the following?
M08_Q35.explanation: X07 Materials Transparency, Part 1 Select Products with Disclosed Ingredients 要求至少 50% by count 或 25 distinct permanently installed products and furniture 透過認可方式揭露成分，其中包含 International Living Future Institute 的 Declare label。

B.gap: 混淆 Petals 與 ingredient disclosure 工具。
B.followup: X07 Part 1 清單中有 Petals，還是 Declare label？

A.gap: 把 LEED 評分系統當成直接成分揭露標籤。
A.followup: X07 要產品層級 ingredient disclosure，LEED 本身是標籤嗎？

C.gap: 只記得 ILFI，未分清 Challenge 與 Declare label。
C.followup: X07 Part 1 明列的是 Living Building Challenge，還是 Declare？

M08_Q36.stem: In order to fulfill the requirement of Feature X10: Pest Management and Pesticide Use, how are pests primarily managed?

options.text: Finalizing a pest control use listing
There is no active management of pests

M08_Q36.explanation: X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use, Part 1 Manage Pests 要求 for all indoor and outdoor spaces 實施 based on integrated pest management (IPM) principles 的 pest control management plan，並列出 objectives、thresholds、records 與低危害 pesticide 條件。

A.gap: 誤以為只限制 pesticide 使用地點，漏掉 IPM。
A.followup: X10 Part 1 是禁室內 pesticide，還是室內外都採 IPM？

B.gap: 只抓到 pesticide listing，漏掉 IPM plan 核心。
B.followup: 只有 pesticide listing，能涵蓋 X10 的 objectives 與 thresholds 嗎？

C.gap: 忽略 X10 要求主動 pest management plan。
C.followup: X10 Part 1 要每年評估 plan effectiveness，能沒有 active management 嗎？

M08_Q37.stem: The intent of Feature X12: Contact Reduction is

options.text: to implement strategies to streamline movement through common areas.
to implement strategies to reduce human contact with respiratory particles and surfaces that may carry pathogens.
to implement strategies to prevent minor impact injuries.
to implement strategies to reduce the risk of airborne disease.

M08_Q37.explanation: X12 Contact Reduction 的 intent 是以 design and policy strategies 減少與 respiratory particles 及可能帶有 pathogens 的 surfaces 接觸；Part 1 聚焦 respiratory particle exposure，Part 2 聚焦 surface hand touch。

C.gap: 把 contact reduction 誤解成碰撞傷害預防。
C.followup: X12 Issue 討論 pathogens 與 fomites，是否關於 minor impact injuries？

A.gap: 只看到動線策略，漏掉降低暴露的健康目的。
A.followup: X12 的 circulation strategies 服務於哪種 exposure reduction？

D.gap: 只聚焦 airborne disease，漏掉手觸表面風險。
D.followup: X12 Part 2 為何要處理 surface hand touch？

M08_Q38.stem: What are some of the design features that can be implemented to meet the requirements of Feature X12: Contact Reduction?

options.text: Require corporate HR policies around mandatory sick days.
Facial tissues provided in all common areas.
Design and implement physical barriers in the project to prevent respiratory particles.

M08_Q38.explanation: X12 Contact Reduction, Part 1 Reduce Respiratory Particle Exposure 接受 screens, protective furnishings or other engineering controls，以減少 reception、check-out counters 等高互動位置的 particle exchange。

A.gap: 混淆 X11 清潔計畫與 X12 barrier strategy。
A.followup: corporate cleaning schedule 對應 X11；X12 Part 1 要哪些 barrier？

B.gap: 把 HR 病假政策當作 X12 設計措施。
B.followup: X12 Part 1 列 marks、screens、self-service，含 sick days 嗎？

C.gap: 把衛生用品提供誤認為 X12 的設計控制。
C.followup: X12 Part 1 的 respiratory particle design feature 是什麼？

M08_Q39.stem: All of these are policies in Part 1: Reduce Respiratory Particle Exposure that can be implemented to meet the requirements of Feature X12: Contact Reduction except

options.text: Marks to Increase Distance Between People
Encourage use of many entrances/exists whenever possible to avoid clustering
Create self service systems to control ingress and egress

M08_Q39.explanation: X12 Contact Reduction, Part 1 Reduce Respiratory Particle Exposure 明列 queuing marks、screens/protective furnishings、self-service systems，以及 circulation strategies 中的 separate entry and exit doors；它不是要求泛泛鼓勵使用 many entrances/exits。

A.gap: 把正式 distancing strategy 誤判為例外。
A.followup: X12 Part 1 是否明列 queuing marks to increase distance？

C.gap: 漏看 screens 和 furnishings 是明列策略。
C.followup: X12 Part 1 是否允許 screens 減少 particle exchange？

D.gap: 未辨認 self-service systems 是明列控制。
D.followup: X12 Part 1 是否包含 self-service systems 控制 ingress 或 egress？

M08_Q40.stem: Regarding Part 2: Address Surface Hand Touch of Feature X12: Contact Reduction, the requirements for hands-free operation must be met for the following functions, except

options.text: Water bottle fillers, water faucets and dispensers.

M08_Q40.explanation: X12 Contact Reduction, Part 2 Address Surface Hand Touch 的 hands-free operation 清單包括 regularly used pedestrian entry doors、elevators、water bottle fillers/water faucets/soap and paper towel dispensers 等；handrails、handlebars 等是 hand hygiene support 的 high-touch surfaces，不屬於 hands-free operation 清單。

C.gap: 把 entry doors 誤判為 hands-free 例外。
C.followup: X12 Part 2 是否列 regularly used pedestrian entry doors？

D.gap: 忽略 elevators 被列為 hands-free 候選項目。
D.followup: Part 2 要至少三項 hands-free，elevators 是否在清單內？

B.gap: 漏看飲水與水龍頭設備屬於 hands-free 清單。
B.followup: X12 Part 2 是否列 water fillers、faucets 與 dispensers？

M08_Q41.stem: Which material selection process does not promote the selection of products that have been audited to minimize impacts on human and environmental health as listed in Feature X08: Materials Optimization?

options.text: REACH Restriction, Authorization and SHVC list
Cradle to Cradle Basic Level restricted Substances List v4
Living Building Challenge's Red List v4.0

M08_Q41.explanation: X08 Materials Optimization, Part 1 Select Materials with Enhanced Chemical Restrictions 接受 Living Building Challenge Red List v4.0、Cradle to Cradle Basic Level Restricted Substances List v4、REACH Restriction/Authorization/SVHC lists 與 LEED v4.1 Advanced Inventory & Assessment；US EPA 40 CFR Part 273 不在此材料選擇清單中。

D.gap: 把 X08 明列 Red List 路徑誤認為不合規。
D.followup: X08 Part 1 是否明列 Living Building Challenge Red List v4.0？

C.gap: 未辨認 Cradle to Cradle RSL v4 是合規路徑。
C.followup: X08 是否允許 Cradle to Cradle Basic Level RSL v4？

B.gap: 漏看 REACH lists 是 X08 正式合規選項。
B.followup: X08 Part 1 是否接受 REACH Restriction、Authorization 與 SVHC？

M08_Q42.stem: Why should a project team ensure a waste stream plan is created and followed for a Battery company in Sarasota, Florida that wants to obtain a WELL Certification?

options.text: To comply with the requirements of Feature X09: Waste Management.
To improve the recycling rate by occupant.
To meet the minimum recycling space required.
To comply with the requirements of Feature: X04 Site Remediation.

M08_Q42.explanation: X09 Waste Management, Part 1 Implement a Waste Management Plan 要求對 batteries、pesticides、mercury-containing lamps/equipment 與 electrical/electronic equipment 制定並執行 waste management plan，包含 waste sources、collection、spill protocols 與 off-site shipment。

D.gap: 混淆 site remediation 與 hazardous waste。
D.followup: batteries waste stream 屬於 X09，還是 X04 brownfield assessment？

B.gap: 把 hazardous waste plan 誤解為一般回收率改善。
B.followup: X09 intent 是提高 recycling rate，還是降低 hazardous waste exposure？

C.gap: 套用一般回收空間概念，漏掉 hazardous waste 管理。
C.followup: X09 要 clearly labeled receptacles，是否只是 recycling space 面積？

M08_Q43.stem: During a renovation of a project in Sarasota, FL, is demonstration prior remediation of lead paint in the building. What does Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management require to verify this prior remediation?
M08_Q43.explanation: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management, Part 2 Manage Lead Paint Hazards 要求在既有建築中識別 lead paint hazards；若 local laws 要求入住前完整移除，project must demonstrate that risk has been addressed by relevant legal documentation, such as certificates of occupancy or remediation reports。

A.gap: 把 prior remediation 證明誤認為只需照片。
A.followup: X02 Part 2 prior remediation 能只靠照片，還是需 remediation reports？

B.gap: 過度依賴 owner assurance，缺少 remediation 證據。
B.followup: 證明 lead paint risk 已處理，owner letter 與報告差在哪？

C.gap: 把 prior remediation 情境誤當一般技術文件提交。
C.followup: X02 prior remediation 要說明已處理風險，還是提交新產品規格？

M08_Q44.stem: Which of the following actions taken by the project team does not follow the intent of Feature X09: Waste Management?

options.text: Creating a recycling and materials recovery plan.
Making the building owner purchase trash bins 30 lb or larger.
Identify sources of waste and create strategies to minimize waste generation.
Establishing and Identifying Roles and Responsibilities for a Waste Management Plan.

M08_Q44.explanation: X09 Waste Management, Part 1 Implement a Waste Management Plan 要求 identification of roles、sources of waste、strategies to minimize waste generation、separately contained clearly labeled receptacles、spill protocols 與 shipment protocols；標準沒有要求 building owner 購買 30 lb or larger trash bins。

C.gap: 誤判 X09 明列的 waste sources 與 minimization。
C.followup: X09 Part 1 b 是否要求 sources of waste 與 minimization？

A.gap: 把材料回收計畫誤視為偏離 X09 intent。
A.followup: X09 waste plan 是否需要 collection strategies 與 shipment？

D.gap: 漏看 roles 與 responsibilities 是 plan 必備項。
D.followup: X09 Part 1 a 是否明列 roles、responsibilities and vendors？

M08_Q45.stem: A project team is working on a building in Orlando that was constructed prior to any applicable laws banning or restricting lead and asbestos. How often are they required to revisit strategies for managing existing hazards?
M08_Q45.explanation: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management 要求若 asbestos-containing materials 以非移除方式管理，follow-up inspection 不得超過三年；Part 2 對留置且穩定化或 enclosure 的 lead-containing paints 也要求至少每三年 re-inspection。

C.gap: 忽略既有 hazardous materials 留置時必須定期追蹤完整性。
C.followup: 若 ACM 或 lead paint 沒有完全移除，X02 是否允許永遠不再檢查？

D.gap: 把 X10 年度評估誤套到 X02 hazards。
D.followup: X02 對 asbestos 與 lead re-inspection 是每年還是每三年？

B.gap: 低估 hazard monitoring 頻率，把三年延為五年。
B.followup: X02 Part 1 f 說 follow-up inspection 不能超過幾年？

M08_Q46.stem: A team in Sarasota is renovating an old Fuel Station into a WELL Certified farm-to-table restaurant. What standard must be used for the site assessment, prior to construction?

options.text: Soil Quality Guidance on Sustainable Remediation Standard
Phase I and Phase II Environmental
San Francisco Reduce Risk Pesticide List

M08_Q46.explanation: X04 Site Remediation, Part 1 Assess and Mitigate Site Hazards 對 fuel station 等 past industrial activities 要先依 local environmental site assessment regulation 或 ASTM E1527-05 Phase I site assessment 評估；若顯示可能有 contaminants，再依 local regulation 或 ASTM E1903-97 Phase II site assessment 量化與判定 remediation needs。

A.gap: 只抓 remediation 語意，漏掉 Phase I/II 條文。
A.followup: X04 site assessment 首步明列哪個 ASTM Phase I 標準？

C.gap: 混淆 pesticide 清單與 site assessment。
C.followup: San Francisco pesticide list 能用於 fuel station assessment 嗎？

D.gap: 把非 WELL 指定的 Soil Quality Guidance 一併納入而誤選 Both
D.followup: X04 指定的是 ASTM E1527 Phase I 與 E1903 Phase II；選項 A 的 Sustainable Remediation 標準是否在其列？

M08_Q47.stem: An office building in Minneapolis is working to comply with Part 2: Restrict Mercury of Feature X01: Materials Restrictions. For all spaces, what restrictions are detailed for newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break switches?

options.text: Contains no more than 0.1% (1000 ppm) of mercury by weight

M08_Q47.explanation: X01 Material Restrictions, Part 2 Restrict Mercury 要求 newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats and load break switches 符合 RoHS restrictions，或 mercury by weight 不超過 0.1% (1000 ppm)。

D.gap: 把限制 mercury 含量誤解成完全不得含有 mercury。
D.followup: X01 Part 2 對這類設備是否給了 no more than 0.1% (1000 ppm) 的替代門檻？

C.gap: 辨認到 RoHS 類似概念但名稱錯誤，且題目選項並非標準文字。
C.followup: 標準寫的是 RoHS restrictions；若選項拼成 RoSS，是否仍能精確對應 WELL 條文？

B.gap: 混入 thermal comfort 標準，與 mercury 限制無關。
B.followup: ASHRAE 55 能否證明 X01 mercury by weight 限制？

M08_Q48.stem: What is the intent of the Materials Concept within a building?

options.text: To reduce human exposure to chemicals that may impact health during construction, remodeling, furnishing and operation of buildings.
To minimize the impact of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) emitted by products on indoor air quality.
To mitigate environmental contamination and associated exposure to hazards present in certain wastes.
To achieve high levels of indoor air quality across a building's lifetime.

M08_Q48.explanation: Materials concept overview 指出，WELL Materials concept aims to reduce human exposure, whether direct or through environmental contamination, to chemicals that may impact health during construction, remodeling, furnishing and operation of buildings；VOCs、waste 與 IAQ 都只是其中部分議題。

B.gap: 只抓 VOC 子議題，未涵蓋 Materials concept。
B.followup: Materials overview 除 VOCs 外，還包括 legacy chemicals 與 waste 嗎？

C.gap: 把 X09 waste intent 誤當 Materials 總體目的。
C.followup: X09 waste exposure 能代表所有建材與營運化學暴露嗎？

D.gap: 混淆 Air concept 的 IAQ 與 Materials 化學暴露。
D.followup: Materials overview 提到 IAQ，是整體目標還是 VOCs 產品排放脈絡？

M08_Q49.stem: An office building in Jacksonville does not include exterior areas, bare soil, artificial turf, playgrounds, or surfaces containing loose-fill rubber within the project boundary. What verification method would the project team use to document compliance with Part 1: Manage Exterior CCA Hazards of Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management?
M08_Q49.explanation: X03 CCA and Lead Management, Part 1 Manage Exterior CCA Hazards applies to existing wood structures outside the building envelope but within the project boundary where human presence is expected；若題述邊界內沒有相關 exterior elements，合規重點是說明不適用範圍，而非提交檢測數值。

B.gap: 把不適用說明誤當營運期持續報告要求項目。
B.followup: 沒有 exterior CCA 風險時，X03 需要 ongoing data 嗎？

A.gap: 以為照片足以證明無 exterior hazards。
A.followup: 照片能輔助場地，但如何說明無 bare soil、turf 或 playgrounds？

D.gap: 把無適用範圍的敘述型合規誤當成技術測試文件。
D.followup: 若沒有要測 CCA 或 lead 的材料，Technical Document 的測試數據從何而來？

M08_Q50.stem: WELL Feature X04: Site Remediation requires site assessment, testing, and remediation for the development of contaminated sites. What verification method is required to satisfy the requirements of this feature?
M08_Q50.explanation: X04 Site Remediation, Part 1 Assess and Mitigate Site Hazards 要求 environmental site assessment、Phase I/Phase II assessment 或 local regulation、sampling strategy 與 sustainable remediation plan；這些 assessment reports、sampling results 與 remediation documentation 屬於 Technical Document 型證據。

B.gap: 誤以為 X04 由現場 performance test 驗證。
B.followup: X04 要 assessment reports，和空氣或水質 Performance Test 有何不同？

D.gap: 把施工照片當主要證據，漏掉 Phase I/II 評估。
D.followup: On-site Photos 能否證明 ASTM Phase I/II assessment 已完成？

C.gap: 過度依賴業主聲明，缺少污染評估與技術資料。
C.followup: X04 sampling strategy 與 remediation plan 能只靠 owner letter 嗎？

M09_Q01.stem: A non-profit is working on their WELL Certification for their outreach facility. What sort of mental health promotion program must the organization offer to all regular occupants in order to support their mental health and well-being?
M09_Q01.explanation: M01 Mental Health Promotion, Part 1 Promote Mental Health and Well-being 要求至少 two of the listed programs, policies or resources 對 all employees and students 免費提供，並且每年向 regular occupants 溝通可用資源。單一 program 或只記頻率都不足以滿足此 Part 的數量要求。

B.gap: 把 M01 Part 1 的 at least two 誤記成單一方案即可。
B.followup: M01 Part 1 列出五類 mental health 支援時，條文要求 project 至少提供幾類給 employees and students？

D.gap: 只抓到 quarterly awareness 頻率，忽略需從清單選兩項。
D.followup: quarterly education 是 M01 Part 1 清單中的一項；若只提供這一項，是否達到 at least two 的門檻？

C.gap: 混淆 weekly restorative programming 與整體 mental health promotion 數量。
C.followup: weekly mindfulness programming 在 M01 Part 1 是可選策略之一，還是整個 feature 的唯一最低要求？

M09_Q02.stem: Projects seeking points in Part 1: Promote Mental Health and Well-being of Feature M01: Mental Health Promotion lists requirements that support and promote occupant mental health. What is one of these requirements?

options.text: Send daily emails with inspirational quotes and simple health tips.
Offer work-breaks every morning that include wellness quotes and quick health tips.
Encourage employees to sign up for a library card along with a list of pre-approved book recommendations.
Education or awareness efforts on mental health and well-being, offered quarterly, either in-person or virtually.

M09_Q02.explanation: M01 Mental Health Promotion, Part 1 Promote Mental Health and Well-being 明列 education or awareness efforts on mental health and well-being 須 quarterly 提供，且可 in-person 或 virtually。這是 at least two available at no cost 的可選要求之一。

B.gap: 把一般 wellness break 當成 M01 明列 mental health program。
B.followup: M01 Part 1 的 awareness effort 是否要求聚焦 mental health and well-being，而不只是早晨短句或健康提示？

A.gap: 誤把 inspirational email 視為合格教育，缺少 mental health 主題要求。
A.followup: 若 email 只是 inspirational quotes，是否符合 M01 Part 1 要求的 mental health and well-being education or awareness？

C.gap: 將閱讀推薦誤認為標準列出的 mental health 支援資源。
C.followup: M01 Part 1 的五項策略中，有要求 library card 或書單，還是要求 training、policy、restorative programming 等？

M09_Q03.stem: Mental health awareness is emerging as an important workplace topic. Failure to address mental health in the workplace contributes to the following:

options.text: Higher employee productivity, but with greater health risks.
High turnover, absenteeism and increased possibility of accidents.
Although important, no measurable drawbacks are associated with not addressing mental health in the workplace.
Lower cohesion in employee collaboration projects.

M09_Q03.explanation: M01 Mental Health Promotion, Part 1 Promote Mental Health and Well-being 回應的 Issue 指出，未處理 workplace mental health 會造成 high turnover、presenteeism and absenteeism、increased accidents and injuries 及 burnout 等成本。此 feature 因此要求提供 supportive programs, policies and resources。

D.gap: 只抓到團隊合作層面，漏掉標準列出的營運與安全後果。
D.followup: M01 Issue 描述 failure to address mental health 時，是否只談 collaboration，還是列出 turnover、absenteeism 與 accidents？

A.gap: 誤以為壓力可提高 productivity，與 M01 的 lost productivity 脈絡相反。
A.followup: M01 背景提到 depression and anxiety 對全球經濟的影響，是提升 productivity 還是造成 lost productivity？

C.gap: 否認 workplace mental health 缺口有可衡量後果。
C.followup: 若 M01 Issue 已列 turnover、absenteeism、sick leave 和 accidents，還能說沒有 measurable drawbacks 嗎？

M09_Q04.stem: Alice is tired from working late nights on a project at work and her office has a space provided for short naps. In Feature M06: Restorative Opportunities, which of the following furniture options should her office have available in the in the space for employees to relax?

options.text: A couch measuring at least 1.8m (6 ft) between the armrests.
A comfortable mattress on a cot.
At least one fully reclining furniture option.
An empty space with a sleeping bag and mat.

M09_Q04.explanation: M06 Restorative Opportunities, Part 2 Provide Nap Policy and Space 的 nap space option 要求 designated quiet zone 中，每 100 eligible employees 至少有 one fully reclining furniture option。條文例子包含 bed、daybed、couch、chair、cushioned roll-out mat 或 nap pod，但重點是 fully reclining。

B.gap: 抓到可休息家具概念，但誤以為特定 cot mattress 是標準文字。
B.followup: M06 Part 2 是指定 cot mattress，還是要求每 100 eligible employees 有 fully reclining furniture option？

A.gap: 混入其他空間家具尺寸印象，未對準 nap space 的 reclining 要求。
A.followup: M06 Part 2 的 nap space 條文是否規定 couch 長度，或只要求 furniture 能 fully recline？

D.gap: 把臨時睡袋鋪設當成合格 nap space，忽略指定家具與 quiet zone。
D.followup: 若只有 sleeping bag and mat，是否滿足 M06 Part 2 對 acoustically and visually separated quiet zone 的要求？

M09_Q05.stem: A WELL building in California knows employee health is one of the most important aspects of the WELL Building Standard™ because it impacts aspects of work including productivity, concentration and even the health of coworkers. Which of the following should be offered to employees in accordance with Feature M03: Mental Health Services?

options.text: A licensed counselor on-site for each 60 employees.
Workplace policies that encourage ill employees to stay home or work remotely.
Mental health days in addition to sick days.
Mental health screening for common mental health conditions.

M09_Q05.explanation: M03 Mental Health Services, Part 1 Offer Mental Health Screening 要求 clinical screening 或 self-assessment screening tool 對 all employees and students 免費提供，至少涵蓋 stress、depression、anxiety and substance use。工具也必須 confidentially 提供，並包含 directed feedback 或 next steps。

A.gap: 誤把 access to services 理解成固定比例的 on-site counselor。
A.followup: M03 Part 1 對 screening tool 有要求每 60 人一位 counselor，還是要求 confidential screening 與 next steps？

B.gap: 混淆感染或病假政策與 M03 的 mental health services。
B.followup: M03 Part 1 要提供的是 common mental health conditions screening；stay home policy 屬於哪種健康風險管理？

C.gap: 接近 workplace support 脈絡，但不是本題所問的 screening requirement。
C.followup: M03 Part 3 確實提到 sick leave 可用於 mental health needs；但 Part 1 要提供哪種 screening？

M09_Q06.stem: Feature M05: Stress Management requires projects to assess stress within the organization and create a plan for relieving or modifying sources of stress. What is Part 1 of this WELL feature?

options.text: Identify Opportunities to Address Employee Stress.

M09_Q06.explanation: M05 Stress Management 的 Part 1 名稱是 Develop Stress Management Plan，要求 project assessment 至少三項 organization- or project-wide metrics，辨識 address employee stress 的 opportunities，並 outlining implementation plan。其他選項多半是 Part 1 內部步驟而非 Part name。

C.gap: 把 Part 1 內的子要求誤當成整個 Part 的名稱。
C.followup: M05 Part 1 中 identify opportunities 是條文步驟之一；完整 Part title 是否更廣，包含 develop plan？

A.gap: 用概括性主題取代標準中的正式 Part name。
A.followup: 題目問 Feature M05 的 Part 1 名稱，標準是寫 Addressing Employee Stress 還是 Develop Stress Management Plan？

D.gap: 只看到 employee participation 概念，誤認為 Part 1 主軸。
D.followup: M05 Part 1 確實要找 employee participation opportunities，但這是 implementation plan 的哪一層要求？

M09_Q07.stem: A company in Portland knows connection to nature can allow for relief of stress. What can this company do to fulfill the requirements of Feature M02: Nature and Place?

options.text: Subsidize desk plants and instructions on their care.
Incorporate a water feature on each floor of the building.
Integrate natural materials, plants, water and views of nature.

M09_Q07.explanation: M02 Nature and Place, Part 1 Provide Connection to Nature 要求 throughout the space 整合 natural materials, patterns, shapes, colors, images or sounds，並至少包含 plants、water 或 nature views 其中之一。完整整合自然材料與 plants/water/views 最貼近條文要求。

C.gap: 只選單一 water feature，忽略 M02 要 throughout space 整合多種自然連結。
C.followup: M02 Part 1 是否只要求每層一個 water feature，還是要求在 circulation routes、shared areas、workstations 整合自然元素？

B.gap: 把個人桌盆栽補助當成專案層級的 spatial design requirement。
B.followup: M02 Part 1 關注 project space 的 nature integration；desk plant subsidy 是否涵蓋 common circulation routes 和 shared rooms？

A.gap: 把自然主題裝飾誤認為足夠，未包含 plants、water 或 nature views。
A.followup: M02 Part 1 雖可用 images/patterns，但條文還要求 plants、water 或 nature views 至少哪一類？

M09_Q08.stem: A building manager in Washington wants to increase the morale and mood of her employees. In Feature M02: Nature and Place, which of the following areas should she incorporate nature?
M09_Q08.explanation: M02 Nature and Place, Part 1 Provide Connection to Nature 要求自然元素 throughout the space，並明列 common circulation routes、shared seating areas and rooms 以及 workstations as applicable。common circulation routes 是條文直接列出的適用區域。

B.gap: 選到建築附屬交通空間，未對準 M02 明列的室內 common routes。
B.followup: M02 Part 1 條文舉例的是 common circulation routes，還是 parking deck 這類非主要占用空間？

D.gap: 將 restrooms 當作自然連結重點，忽略標準列出的共享與工作區。
D.followup: 若目標是提高 employee morale，M02 Part 1 是否要求自然元素進入 shared seating areas、rooms 與 workstations？

A.gap: 把垂直交通設備誤認為主要 circulation route 的要求核心。
A.followup: M02 Part 1 的 common circulation routes 是否指一般 occupants 行經空間，而非只在 elevators 內放裝飾？

M09_Q09.stem: How is mental health defined by the WELL Building Standard?

options.text: A state of and base understanding of physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.

A state of well-being in which every individual realizes his or her own potential, can cope with the normal stresses of life, can work productively and fruitfully and is able to make a contribution to her or his community.
The degree to which individuals can obtain, process and understand the basic health information and services they need to make appropriate health decisions.
Any combination of learning experiences designed to help individuals and communities improve their health, by increasing their knowledge or influencing their attitudes.

M09_Q09.explanation: WELL Mind concept overview 說明 mental health is not simply the absence of a mental health condition，而是能 live to fullest potential、cope with normal stresses、work productively 並 contribute to community 的 state of well-being。M01 Mental Health Promotion, Part 1 的 supportive programs and resources 即是為了支援此定義。

A.gap: 混入 WHO 健康總定義，未抓到 WELL 對 mental health 的功能性描述。
A.followup: WELL Mind overview 定義 mental health 時，是否特別提到 coping with normal stresses 和 productive work？

C.gap: 把 health literacy 定義誤當成 mental health 定義。
C.followup: 能 obtain, process and understand health information 是 health literacy；WELL Mind 如何描述 mental well-being？

D.gap: 把 health education 的定義當成 mental health 本身。
D.followup: learning experiences 可改善健康知識，但 WELL Mind 對 mental health 是否更強調 potential、stress coping 與 community contribution？

M09_Q10.stem: An office building in New York is planning an initiative to include mental health education in their WELL portfolio. Which of the following would fulfill the requirement of Feature M04: Mental Health Education?

options.text: In-person only trainings on mental health awareness.
Off-site trainings on common mental health conditions.
Links to a virtual mental health resource library distributed by email.
In-person or virtual trainings on common mental health conditions, including signs and symptoms of mental health distress.

M09_Q10.explanation: M04 Mental Health Education, Part 1 Offer Mental Health Education 要求 trainings 以 seminars, workshops or classes 形式 at least twice per year 提供，且可 in-person or virtually。內容需至少涵蓋兩項主題，包含 common mental health conditions 或 signs and symptoms of mental health distress 等。

A.gap: 知道需要 training，但誤以為 WELL 排除 virtual delivery。
A.followup: M04 Part 1 對 training delivery 是限制 in-person only，還是允許 in-person or virtually？

C.gap: 把資源連結當成教育訓練，缺少 seminar/workshop/class 形式。
C.followup: M04 Part 1 要的是 trainings；單純 email resource library 是否等同 education seminars、workshops or classes？

B.gap: 只抓到 common conditions，忽略條文允許的 delivery setting 與合格形式。
B.followup: M04 Part 1 是否要求 off-site，還是可由 vendors、on-site staff 或 qualified programs 以 virtual/in-person 提供？

M09_Q11.stem: What are some of the common mental health conditions or concerns that mental health education should cover?

options.text: Depression, anxiety, substance use, stress and social isolation.
Personality disorders and other less-recognizable stigmatizing conditions.

M09_Q11.explanation: M04 Mental Health Education, Part 1 Offer Mental Health Education 要求 common mental health conditions or concerns 至少涵蓋 depression、anxiety、substance use、stress and burnout，以及 loneliness and social isolation。這些是條文列出的 minimum topics。

C.gap: 選到可能相關但非 M04 minimum list 的疾病分類。
C.followup: M04 Part 1 的 minimum topics 是否列 personality disorders，還是列 depression、anxiety、substance use 等常見 concerns？

D.gap: 只列少數診斷，範圍窄於 M04 要求的 common concerns。
D.followup: M04 Part 1 要涵蓋的 common concerns 是否只有 anxiety/bipolar，還包含 substance use、stress、burnout 與 isolation？

B.gap: 把壓力造成的慢性病後果當成 mental health education 主題清單。
B.followup: 題目問 M04 mental health education 應 cover 的 conditions；chronic disease 是否出現在該 minimum list？

M09_Q12.stem: A sustainability manager is developing a stress management plan for Feature M05: Stress Management. What outline will the plan require for implementation?

options.text: Employee Representative, Building Owner and Project Manager.
Who, What, Where, When and Availability.

M09_Q12.explanation: M05 Stress Management, Part 1 Develop Stress Management Plan 要求 implementation outline 包含 who is leading、what is to be completed、where it will occur and who is impacted、when and how it will be implemented，以及 availability of support from key management or leadership。這些對應 who/what/where/when and availability。

D.gap: 把空間設計元素誤套到 stress management implementation plan。
D.followup: M05 Part 1 的 implementation outline 是否要求 Space and Amenities，還是要求 who、what、where、when/how？

B.gap: 套用與 restorative 或 workplace layout 無關的四項口訣。
B.followup: 在 M05 Part 1 中，plan 是描述 desk/dining 類空間，還是描述 stress intervention 的執行責任與時程？

A.gap: 只列利害關係人角色，漏掉 what、where、when/how 與 leadership support。
A.followup: M05 Part 1 的 who is leading 只是五項 outline 之一；還必須交代哪些 implementation elements？

M09_Q13.stem: According to the WELL Building Standard, employees need sufficient opportunities to psychologically detach and recover during non-work hours. What requirements must be met for all employees in Feature M06: Restorative Opportunities?

options.text: A minimum of 11 hours off per 24-hour period.
A minimum of 4 hours off per 11-hour period.
There is no minimum time off requirement.
A minimum of 60 hours off spread across each work week.

M09_Q13.explanation: M06 Restorative Opportunities, Part 1 Support Healthy Working Hours, Option 1 Working hours 要求 all employees 每 24-hour period 至少 11 consecutive hours off from work，且每 7-day period 至少 24 consecutive hours off。這是心理抽離與恢復的最低 working hours 門檻。

D.gap: 把 weekly off-hours 總量化，忽略 daily consecutive rest requirement。
D.followup: M06 Part 1 寫的是每週總共 60 小時，還是每 24 小時必須有 11 consecutive hours off？

B.gap: 顛倒 11 小時規則，錯把休息時數縮成 4 小時。
B.followup: M06 Part 1 的 ratio 是 11 hours off per 24-hour period，還是 4 hours off per 11-hour period？

C.gap: 忽略 M06 Part 1 明確設定 working hours 的最低休息門檻。
C.followup: 若 M06 Part 1 有 Option 1 Working hours，是否仍可說沒有 minimum time off requirement？

M09_Q14.stem: Restorative spaces are designed to promote restoration and relief from mental fatigue or stress. How should a restorative space be designated for Feature M07: Restorative Spaces?
M09_Q14.explanation: M07 Restorative Spaces, Part 1 Provide Restorative Space 要求至少 one designated restorative space available to all regular occupants，且該空間 must be designated for relaxation and restoration，並不得用於 work。napping 或 meditation 可是用途之一，但標準文字是 relaxation and restoration。

A.gap: 混淆 M06 nap space 與 M07 restorative space 的 designation。
A.followup: M07 Part 1 是否要求 napping room，或是要求 designated for relaxation and restoration 且 not used for work？

B.gap: 把 M02 connection to place 概念套到 M07 空間用途。
B.followup: community and place 更接近 M02 Part 2；M07 Part 1 對 restorative space 的用途怎麼寫？

D.gap: 把一種可行活動當成標準指定的空間 designation。
D.followup: meditation 可在 restorative space 發生，但 M07 Part 1 是否只限定 meditation，還是更廣的 relaxation and restoration？

M09_Q15.stem: Workday breaks are a way to encourage employees to use restorative breaks. What is another way to encourage the use of restorative spaces in Feature M07: Restorative Spaces?

options.text: Two paid breaks in an 8-hour workday.

M09_Q15.explanation: M07 Restorative Spaces, Part 1 Provide Restorative Space 的 Option 2 Workday breaks 要求 project 透過 paid breaks away from the workstation for all employees 來鼓勵使用 restorative spaces。標準未指定 15 分鐘或每日兩次的數量。

C.gap: 把 walking break 當成 M07 條文，且加入未規定的 15 分鐘。
C.followup: M07 Part 1 Option 2 是否要求 walking break，還是只要求 paid breaks away from workstation？

B.gap: 誤把離開會議當成離開 workstation 的 restorative breaks。
B.followup: M07 Workday breaks 的對象是 staff meetings，還是 all employees away from their workstation？

A.gap: 加入未出現在 M07 的每日次數與 8 小時班表條件。
A.followup: M07 Part 1 Option 2 有寫 two paid breaks in an 8-hour workday，還是只描述 paid breaks 的性質？

M09_Q16.stem: A library in Orlando is looking to satisfy the M07: Restorative Spaces. How much space is required for a restorative space designation?

options.text: At least 75 ft2 plus 1 ft2 per regular occupant, up to 2,000 ft2.
At least 50 ft2 plus 3 ft2 per regular occupant, up to 3,500 ft2.
At least 25 ft2 plus 25 ft2 per regular occupant, up to 3,500 ft2.
At least one 10 ft x 10 ft room.

M09_Q16.explanation: M07 Restorative Spaces, Part 1 Provide Restorative Space 要求 designated restorative space total 至少 75 ft² plus 1 ft² per regular occupant，最高計到 2,000 ft²。空間可為 single 或 multiple spaces，且可 indoor 或 outdoor。

D.gap: 把固定 100 ft² 房間當成公式，忽略 occupant-based area calculation。
D.followup: M07 Part 1 的面積是固定 10 ft x 10 ft，還是 75 ft² 加上每位 regular occupant 1 ft²？

B.gap: 選到相似公式但基礎面積、每人面積與上限都錯。
B.followup: M07 Part 1 的 cap 是 2,000 ft² 還是 3,500 ft²？每位 regular occupant 要加多少面積？

C.gap: 大幅誤用 per occupant 係數，將 restorative space 要求膨脹。
C.followup: 若每位 occupant 要加 25 ft²，會遠高於 M07 標準；原文的 per occupant 數字是多少？

M09_Q17.stem: George is trying to satisfy Feature M08: Restorative Programming by providing restorative programming to his employees. At least two offerings are required from the following: mindfulness training course, mindfulness programming or digital mindfulness offerings. What is the cost to employees for this programming?

options.text: No cost or subsidized by at least 50% to eligible employees.

M09_Q17.explanation: M08 Restorative Programming, Part 1 Provide Restorative Programming 要求至少 two offerings 提供給 all eligible employees，成本須 no cost or subsidized by at least 50%。可選 offerings 包含 live mindfulness training course、weekly mindfulness programming 或 digital mindfulness offerings。

D.gap: 只記得 no cost，忽略標準允許至少 50% subsidy。
D.followup: M08 Part 1 對 eligible employees 的成本要求是必須完全免費，還是可 subsidized by at least 50%？

C.gap: 知道 subsidy 概念，但把最低補助門檻誤記成 75%。
C.followup: M08 Part 1 寫的是 at least 50% subsidized；75% 是合規結果還是最低要求？

B.gap: 漏掉 subsidized 路徑，將可選成本模式縮成免費一種。
B.followup: 若 project 對 eligible employees 補助 50% mindfulness programming，M08 Part 1 是否可接受？

M09_Q18.stem: Feature M02: Nature and Place requires the integration of nature throughout the project, as well as design that celebrates the project’s unique identity and inspires human delight. Natural elements such as plants and daylight have been linked with health-promoting benefits, including:

options.text: Sense of achievement when caring for a desk plant.
Decreased irritation in the work space.
Better recovery from job stress and illness.
Improved air quality of a space.

M09_Q18.explanation: M02 Nature and Place, Part 1 Provide Connection to Nature 的 Issue 說明 plants and daylight 等 natural elements 與 decreased depression and anxiety、increased attentional capacity、better recovery from job stress and illness、increased pain tolerance 及 psychological well-being 有關。這些 benefits 支撐 throughout the space 的 nature integration requirement。

B.gap: 把情緒改善泛化成 irritation，未對準標準列出的 benefit。
B.followup: M02 Issue 列出的 health-promoting benefits 中，有直接寫 decreased irritation，還是 better recovery from job stress and illness？

D.gap: 把植物可能影響 air quality 的概念誤當成 M02 心理效益重點。
D.followup: M02 Nature and Place 在 Mind concept 中強調 air quality，還是 stress recovery、attention 與 psychological well-being？

A.gap: 把照顧盆栽的個人成就感當成 WELL 條文列出的研究效益。
A.followup: M02 Issue 是否提到 desk plant care achievement，還是提到 natural elements 對 stress、illness recovery 的連結？

M09_Q19.stem: In order to provide nature access indoors as required for M09: Enhanced Access to Nature, what percentage of workstations should have a direct line of sight to an indoor plant/water feature or an outdoor view of a natural area?
M09_Q19.explanation: M09 Enhanced Access to Nature, Part 1 Provide Nature Access Indoors 要求 indoor plants、water features 或 natural area views 需 within direct line of sight of at least 75% of all workstations and seats within conference rooms, lecture halls or classrooms。此 Part 另要求距離 within 33 ft(10 m)。

B.gap: 大幅低估 M09 Part 1 的 direct line of sight 覆蓋比例。
B.followup: M09 Part 1 要 at least 75% 的 workstations/seats 有自然視線；25% 是否足以代表多數 occupants？

D.gap: 選到中間比例，但未達 M09 明確的三分之三近似門檻。
D.followup: M09 Part 1 的 line of sight threshold 是 45%，還是 at least 75% of all workstations and seats？

A.gap: 把 minimum threshold 誤加嚴為所有座位必須直接可見。
A.followup: M09 Part 1 說 at least 75%，是否等於要求 100% workstations 都有 direct line of sight？

M09_Q20.stem: In order to provide nature access indoors, what distance from an interior plant or water feature or an external natural area is required to receive the points for M09: Enhanced Access to Nature?
M09_Q20.explanation: M09 Enhanced Access to Nature, Part 1 Provide Nature Access Indoors 要求自然元素或 natural area views 位於 all workstations and seats within conference rooms, lecture halls or classrooms 的 33 ft(10 m) 內。這個 distance requirement 與 75% direct line of sight threshold 搭配使用。

A.gap: 選到較寬鬆距離，未記住 M09 的 33 ft/10 m 門檻。
A.followup: M09 Part 1 同時寫 33 ft 與 10 m；50 ft 是否出現在 indoor nature access requirement？

C.gap: 把自然可及距離放寬到標準未允許的 75 ft。
C.followup: 若 workstation 距離 plant 75 ft，是否仍符合 M09 Part 1 的 within 33 ft(10 m) requirement？

B.gap: 可能混淆公制換算，將 10 m 誤換成 82 ft。
B.followup: M09 Part 1 括號中的 10 m 約等於 33 ft；82 ft 對應的公制距離是否合理？

M09_Q21.stem: Tobacco cessation is a feature in which category?
M09_Q21.explanation: M10 Tobacco Cessation appears under the WELL Mind concept, after M09 Enhanced Access to Nature and before M11 Substance Use Services。M10 Part 1 Provide Tobacco Cessation Resources and Part 2 Limit Tobacco Availability treat tobacco cessation as a Mind intervention supporting mental and behavioral health.

B.gap: 把 tobacco use 誤連到飲食營養，忽略其位於 Mind concept。
B.followup: M10 Tobacco Cessation 在 WELL v2 章節順序中，是出現在 Nourishment，還是在 Mind 的 M features 之下？

C.gap: 因煙霧暴露聯想到 Air，但此 feature 聚焦 cessation services。
C.followup: Air concept 會處理 secondhand smoke exposure；M10 Part 1/2 的 cessation resources 與 tobacco availability 屬於哪個 concept？

D.gap: 把政策與服務支援誤歸到 Community，而非 Mind 行為健康。
D.followup: M11 Substance Use Services 也在 Mind；M10 Tobacco Cessation 是否與它相鄰於同一 concept？

M09_Q22.stem: A retail building in Seattle wants to limit tobacco availability in their building. What requirements need to be met for feature M10: Tobacco Cessation?

options.text: Prohibit the sale of all tobacco products, including e-cigarettes.
Add additional cancer awareness labeling to tobacco products.

M09_Q22.explanation: M10 Tobacco Cessation, Part 2 Limit Tobacco Availability applies to Retail Spaces and requires sale of tobacco products, including e-cigarettes, to be prohibited within the building site。It also requires tobacco products, including e-cigarettes, not be marketed or promoted。

A.gap: 只禁 cigarettes，漏掉 M10 明列的 all tobacco products including e-cigarettes。
A.followup: M10 Part 2 的 prohibition 是只針對 cigarettes，還是涵蓋 tobacco products including e-cigarettes？

D.gap: 只抓到 e-cigarettes，忽略其他 tobacco products 也必須禁止銷售。
D.followup: 若 retail space 只禁 e-cigarettes 但仍賣其他 tobacco products，是否滿足 M10 Part 2？

C.gap: 誤以為加警示標籤可替代禁售與禁止促銷。
C.followup: M10 Part 2 要求 additional cancer labeling，還是要求 no sale and no marketing/promotion within building site？

M09_Q23.stem: The use of alcohol is a leading global risk factor, accounting for how many deaths per year?
M09_Q23.explanation: M11 Substance Use Services, Issue states harmful use of alcohol is a leading global risk factor, accounting for 3.3 million deaths per year and 5.9% of all deaths。這個背景支持 Part 1 Offer Substance Use Education 與 Part 2 Provide Substance Use and Addiction Services。

C.gap: 高估 alcohol deaths，可能混入其他疾病負擔或百分比數字。
C.followup: M11 Issue 同時列 deaths per year 與 5.9% of all deaths；哪個數字對應 3.3 million？

A.gap: 選到接近但非標準文字的死亡數，未核對 M11 Issue。
A.followup: M11 Substance Use Services 的 Issue 段落中，harmful use of alcohol 的 annual deaths 是 4.5 million 嗎？

B.gap: 明顯低估 alcohol 作為 global risk factor 的死亡負擔。
B.followup: 若 alcohol alone 佔 5.9% of all deaths，M11 Issue 給出的 annual deaths 是否可能只有 1.2 million？

M09_Q24.stem: Feature M11: Substance Use Services requires that a substance abuse policy is in place and clearly communicated to all regular occupants. What does this policy need to cover to fulfill the requirement of this feature?

options.text: The use of prescription medication on-site.
Alcohol use and the use of legal recreational drugs on-site.

M09_Q24.explanation: M11 Substance Use Services, Part 1 Offer Substance Use Education, Option 1 Substance use policy 要求 policy regarding the use of alcohol and legal, recreational drugs on-site，且 clearly communicated to all regular occupants。條文焦點不是 prescription medication 或 illegal drug enforcement alone。

D.gap: 把 substance use policy 解讀成 illegal drug enforcement，漏掉 alcohol/legal recreational drugs。
D.followup: M11 Part 1 Option 1 的 policy 原文是 illegal drugs，還是 alcohol and legal, recreational drugs on-site？

A.gap: 只涵蓋 alcohol，漏掉 legal recreational drugs 也在 policy 範圍。
A.followup: 若 policy 只寫 on-site alcohol use，是否完整符合 M11 Part 1 對 legal recreational drugs 的要求？

B.gap: 把 prescription medication 使用誤認為此 policy 的核心範圍。
B.followup: M11 Part 1 的 substance use policy 是否處理 prescription medication，或是在 education topics 中才談 prescription opioid education？

M09_Q25.stem: Substance abuse education is part of Feature M11: Substance Use Services. This requirement provides for training to address the following topics: management of personal substance use, prescription opioid education and________?

options.text: How to report substance abuse in coworkers.
How to respond to alcohol abuse.
How to appropriately respond to a peer struggling with substance abuse.
Who to contact about substance abuse suspicions.

M09_Q25.explanation: M11 Substance Use Services, Part 1 Offer Substance Use Education, Option 2 Substance use education 要求 annual trainings address 三個主題：management of personal substance use、prescription opioid education，以及 how to appropriately respond to a peer struggling with substance use。該主題至少涵蓋 support a peer's recovery efforts 與 relapse or emergency response。

A.gap: 把 peer support 誤讀成舉報 coworker substance abuse。
A.followup: M11 Part 1 的第三項 training topic 是 reporting coworkers，還是 support a peer's recovery efforts？

D.gap: 把教育重點縮成通報對象，漏掉如何適當回應同儕。
D.followup: 若只知道 contact person，是否涵蓋 M11 要求的 relapse 或 substance use emergency response？

B.gap: 只聚焦 alcohol abuse，未涵蓋 peer struggling with broader substance use。
B.followup: M11 Part 1 第三項主題是否只限 alcohol，還是涵蓋 substance use、relapse and emergency response？

M09_Q26.stem: Alcohol and drug use contribute to the global burden of disease. What is the estimated percentage of people living with disability?
M09_Q26.explanation: WELL Mind concept overview states that mental health and substance use conditions account for an estimated 32% of years lived with disability; M11 Substance Use Services, Part 2 Provide Substance Use and Addiction Services then requires access to screening, counseling, outpatient/inpatient treatment and medication-assisted treatment.

B.gap: 把 disability burden 大幅低估，未連到 Mind overview 的 32%。
B.followup: 若題幹問 years lived with disability，Mind overview 給的是個位數、十幾趴，還是三成以上？

A.gap: 混淆 18% 十二個月盛行率與 disability burden 指標。
A.followup: 18% 在 Mind overview 是 adults 12-month condition prevalence，是否等於 years lived with disability？

D.gap: 選了接近但未出現的比例，忽略 overview 明列數值。
D.followup: 在 Mind overview 同一句中，global burden of disease 與 years lived with disability 各是多少？

M09_Q27.stem: Mental health conditions are common among the global population and over _____% of adults will experience a mental health condition during their lifetime?
M09_Q27.explanation: M01 Mental Health Promotion, Part 1 Promote Mental Health and Well-being is grounded in the Issue statement that common mental health conditions affect 18% of adults over 12 months and 30% of adults over their lifetime.

D.gap: 把 lifetime prevalence 低估成未列明的 25%。
D.followup: M01 的 Issue 同時列 12-month 與 lifetime 數字，哪一個才是 over lifetime？

B.gap: 把 lifetime prevalence 誤放大到標準未支持的比例。
B.followup: M01 Issue 是否說超過半數 adults lifetime 會經歷 mental health condition？

C.gap: 混淆 18% 十二個月數值，且選項又略改成 17%。
C.followup: 題目問 lifetime，而 M01 Issue 的 18% 是哪一個時間尺度的估計？

M09_Q28.stem: What is the intent of Feature M01: Mental Health Promotion?

options.text: Mandate breaks every 2 hours to maximize employee restoration.
To gamify activities shown to prevent mental health issues.
To provide programs, policies, and resources that support and promote occupant health.
Screen for mental health issues before rewarding employees additional responsibility.

M09_Q28.explanation: M01 Mental Health Promotion, Intent and Part 1 Promote Mental Health and Well-being require supportive programs, policies and resources that support and promote occupant mental health, including no-cost education, trainings, restorative programming, working-hour policy or restoration space.

D.gap: 把 promotion 誤解成篩檢與績效管理用途。
D.followup: M01 Part 1 是提供 supportive resources，還是用篩檢結果決定員工責任？

B.gap: 把 mental health promotion 縮小成遊戲化活動，忽略 policy/resource 要求。
B.followup: M01 Part 1 列出的策略是 gamification，還是教育、training、policy 與 restorative support？

A.gap: 混淆 M01 支持性政策與 M06/M07 的休息恢復策略。
A.followup: M01 Intent 是否要求每兩小時強制 break，還是提供多種 mental health supports？

M09_Q29.stem: The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals are advancing through the WELL Building Standard. How many SDGs are a part of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development?
M09_Q29.explanation: M01 Mental Health Promotion, Part 1 Promote Mental Health and Well-being illustrates WELL’s SDG-linked policy and program approach; the UN 2030 Agenda referenced by WELL contains 17 Sustainable Development Goals, which is an external alignment fact rather than a WELL feature threshold.

D.gap: 低估 SDGs 總數，將 2030 Agenda 記成較小集合。
D.followup: 若 WELL 引用的是 UN 2030 Agenda，該 Agenda 的 SDGs 是十個還是十七個？

B.gap: 把 SDGs 數量誤加到 22，混淆目標與細項 targets。
B.followup: UN 2030 Agenda 中 goals 和 targets 是同一層級嗎？題目問的是哪一層？

C.gap: 把全球永續目標數量過度簡化，忽略完整 Agenda。
C.followup: WELL 對齊的 2030 Agenda 是五大主題，還是明列多個 Sustainable Development Goals？

M09_Q30.stem: A college is in the process of certifying its largest building and looking to complete the requirements for M01: Mental Health Promotion. What verification method is required to submit the details of this feature?
M09_Q30.explanation: M01 Mental Health Promotion, Part 1 Promote Mental Health and Well-being requires policies, programs, schedules and annual/onboarding communications; Appendix 1 defines Policy and/or Operations Schedule as documents communicating enacted policies, programs or event cadence to occupants or staff.

B.gap: 混淆敘述性說明與正式政策或活動時程文件。
B.followup: M01 Part 1 要證明 quarterly、annual 或 weekly cadence 時，單純 narrative 是否足夠？

C.gap: 把技術圖說或規格文件誤套到政策型 feature。
C.followup: M01 的 no-cost programs 和 annual communications 是 technical product spec，還是 policy/schedule？

D.gap: 把現場照片當成政策與溝通流程的主要證據。
D.followup: 對 M01 Part 1 的 benefits communication，照片能否證明全年 policy 或 operations cadence？

M09_Q31.stem: A corporate campus in Boise is applying for WELL certification of an office building. Their campus is large, but in order to receive credit for M09: Enhanced Access to Nature, regular occupants will have to walk to a nearby green space. What is the required walk distance from the project boundary in order to fulfill the requirements of this feature?
M09_Q31.explanation: M09 Enhanced Access to Nature, Part 2 Provide Nature Access Outdoors requires at least one green space or blue space within a 650 ft (200 m) walk distance from the project boundary and available during open hours, with combined green space at least 1.25 acre.

D.gap: 把 nearby nature 的步行距離放寬到標準未列的 825 ft。
D.followup: M09 Part 2 的 nearby nature access 是 650 ft 還是比 200 m 更遠的距離？

B.gap: 把 M09 的 nearby nature 距離誤縮短成其他近距離門檻。
B.followup: 題目問 project boundary 到 green/blue space，M09 Part 2 是否要求只有 250 ft？

A.gap: 選了非 WELL 條文數字，未對照 200 m 換算。
A.followup: M09 Part 2 將 200 m 換算成英制時，標準寫的是哪個 walk distance？

M09_Q32.stem: Peter is working on the design of a designated restorative space for the building he manages. Peter is looking at different options to add sound to the environment. What types of sound would provide for a restorative environment according to Feature M07: Restorative Spaces?

options.text: Dampening noise from machinery, traffic, electronics, etc..
Water features, natural sounds, or sound masking.

M09_Q32.explanation: M07 Restorative Spaces, Part 1 Provide Restorative Space requires the restorative environment to consider at least five elements, and lists Sound examples as water feature, natural sounds or sound masking.

B.gap: 只抓到 water sound，漏掉 natural sounds 與 sound masking。
B.followup: M07 Part 1 的 Sound 範例是否只有 running water，還包含哪些聲音策略？

D.gap: 把聲音要求過度限縮成 natural sounds only。
D.followup: M07 Part 1 對 Sound 的括號範例是否允許 sound masking 作為 restorative element？

A.gap: 把降噪控制誤當成 restorative sound 元素本身。
A.followup: M07 Part 1 的 Sound 是列水景、自然聲與 masking，還是要求消除機械噪音？

M09_Q33.stem: Connection to place is a precondition for Mind and part of Feature M02 Nature and Place. What must the design elements address to fulfill this requirement?

options.text: Pictures of local wildlife and environments.

M09_Q33.explanation: M02 Nature and Place, Part 2 Provide Connection to Place requires design elements addressing celebration of culture, celebration of place, integration of art and human delight.

D.gap: 把 connection to place 縮成建築史敘事，漏掉 culture 要求。
D.followup: M02 Part 2 列的是 building history narrative，還是 celebration of culture/place/art/delight？

C.gap: 混淆地方感與國際知名建築展示，偏離 project identity。
C.followup: M02 Part 2 強調 local identity 與 culture，展示 international buildings 是否能直接滿足？

B.gap: 把自然意象誤當成 connection to place 的完整要求。
B.followup: M02 Part 2 的 place connection 是否只靠 local wildlife images，還需要哪些設計面向？

M09_Q34.stem: According to the WELL Building Standard Mind concept, worldwide spending on mental health is less than $2 per person. In low-and middle-income countries, what percentage of people living with mental health conditions do not receive the necessary treatment?
M09_Q34.explanation: M03 Mental Health Services, Part 2 Offer Mental Health Services is grounded in the treatment-gap issue statement: in low- and middle-income countries, 76-85% of people living with mental health conditions do not receive necessary treatment.

C.gap: 低估低中收入國家的 treatment gap，混到高收入國家情境。
C.followup: M03 Issue 對 high-income 和 low-/middle-income countries 的 no-treatment ranges 是否相同？

D.gap: 把 treatment gap 誤記成很低比例，忽略標準說差距更大。
D.followup: 若 worldwide spending less than $2/person，M03 Issue 對低中收入國家治療缺口給多高？

A.gap: 選了接近但仍低於標準明列範圍的數字。
A.followup: M03 Issue 寫的是 55-68%，還是 76-85% 未獲必要治療？

M09_Q35.stem: What are the preconditions of the Mind concept?

options.text: Mental Health Promotion and Nature and Place.
Mental Health Services and Mental Health Education.

M09_Q35.explanation: The WELL feature list identifies M01 Mental Health Promotion and M02 Nature and Place as Mind concept preconditions; M01 Part 1 covers supportive mental health programs/policies/resources, while M02 Part 1 and Part 2 require connection to nature and place.

D.gap: 把兩個 optimization 誤認為 Mind preconditions。
D.followup: M03 和 M04 在 WELL feature list 是標示 P，還是 O？哪兩個 Mind features 才是 P？

A.gap: 混淆 restoration 類 optimization 與 certification 必備條件。
A.followup: M05/M06 是否是 Mind preconditions，還是取得額外 points 的 optimization？

C.gap: 把 restorative feature 組合誤當成最低必備項。
C.followup: M07/M08 在 feature list 的狀態是 optimization；precondition 欄位列的是哪些 feature？

M09_Q36.stem: What is an example of an optimization in the Mind concept?
M09_Q36.explanation: The WELL Mind concept feature list marks M10 Tobacco Cessation as an optimization, and M10 Part 1 Provide Tobacco Cessation Resources requires incentive programs or individual cessation resources for eligible employees.

B.gap: 混淆 Air concept 的 smoke-free requirement 與 Mind optimization。
B.followup: Smoke-Free Environment 屬於哪個 WELL concept？它是否在 Mind feature list 的 M10/M11 中？

D.gap: 把 Movement concept 的 active design feature 誤放進 Mind concept。
D.followup: Active Buildings and Communities 是 Movement feature，還是 Mind 的 tobacco/substance service optimization？

C.gap: 混淆 Community health benefits feature 與 Mind 的 tobacco cessation。
C.followup: Health Services and Benefits 支援 healthcare access，但它在標準中屬於哪個 concept？

M09_Q37.stem: As an employee of a company who achieved the optimization Feature M06: Restorative Opportunities, you would receive a minimum of _____ days per year as paid time off, not including sick or paid holidays?
M09_Q37.explanation: M06 Restorative Opportunities, Part 1 Support Healthy Working Hours, Option 2 Paid time off requires eligible employees to receive paid time off with a minimum of 20 days per calendar year, excluding designated sick days or standard paid holidays.

D.gap: 把 PTO minimum 少記兩天，未排除 sick days/holidays。
D.followup: M06 Part 1 Option 2 的 paid time off minimum 是 18 還是 20 calendar-year days？

B.gap: 把最低要求誤加嚴到標準未要求的 22 天。
B.followup: 若專案提供 22 days 可合規，但 M06 Part 1 的最低門檻實際是多少？

A.gap: 低估 restorative opportunities 對 PTO 的最低要求。
A.followup: M06 Part 1 Option 2 是否允許只有 15 days paid time off 且仍拿分？

M09_Q38.stem: For a school who achieves Feature M06: Restorative Opportunities, what is the requirement for start time of students in secondary schools?
M09_Q38.explanation: M06 Restorative Opportunities, Part 1 Support Healthy Working Hours, Option 3 Student start times requires secondary school students’ school day to start no earlier than 8:30 a.m.

A.gap: 把 secondary school start time 提早了半小時。
A.followup: M06 Part 1 Option 3 的 no earlier than 門檻是 8:00 還是 8:30 a.m.？

D.gap: 把最低要求誤延後，選了比標準更嚴的時間。
D.followup: 若標準說 no earlier than 8:30，8:45 是必要門檻還是更晚的做法？

B.gap: 忽略 adolescent sleep 支持策略，選了標準禁止的更早時間。
B.followup: M06 Part 1 對 secondary schools 是否允許 7:45 a.m. 作為合規 start time？

M09_Q39.stem: An employee of your company is interested in mental health services to support recovery from a traumatic event. An in-person service may be offered if it is within _______ of the project boundary?
M09_Q39.explanation: M03 Mental Health Services, Part 4 beta Support Mental Health Recovery requires traumatic-event recovery services at no cost or subsidized, on-site, in-person within 0.25 mi (400 m) of the project boundary or virtually, including at least three listed supports.

A.gap: 把 in-person service 的距離放寬到標準未列的 0.5 mi。
A.followup: M03 Part 4 對 traumatic-event recovery services 的 in-person boundary distance 是 0.25 mi 還是 0.5 mi？

B.gap: 把服務可及距離放寬到 1 mi，超過 Part 4 門檻。
B.followup: M03 Part 4 是否允許 1 mi 內都算 in-person access，還是只到 400 m？

C.gap: 忽略 Part 4 允許虛擬或鄰近 in-person 服務。
C.followup: M03 Part 4 是否只接受 on-site services，還包含 project boundary 0.25 mi 內或 virtual？

M09_Q40.stem: What natural elements have been linked with health-promoting benefits including decreased levels of depression and anxiety and increased psychological well being?
M09_Q40.explanation: M02 Nature and Place, Issue for Part 1 Provide Connection to Nature states that natural elements such as plants and daylight are linked with decreased depression and anxiety, increased attentional capacity and increased psychological well-being.

C.gap: 只抓到水元素，漏掉題目所指 plants 與 daylight。
C.followup: M02 Issue 明列的 natural elements 是 plants and daylight，還是只說 water features？

B.gap: 把通風策略誤當成 Mind 自然元素效益。
B.followup: Natural air ventilation 主要屬於 Air/ventilation 議題，M02 Issue 直接列出的元素是什麼？

A.gap: 混淆自然聲音與更廣泛的 plants/daylight 健康效益。
